# Freshsales Alternatives: Top Picks for CRM ✅ in 2026

Best Freshsales Alternatives for Streamlining Your Sales Process
================================================================

![Flowlu Team](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24571/zaglushka.png)

Flowlu Team

December 6, 2024

24 min read

Table of contents

*   [Why Do Businesses Look for Freshsales Alternatives?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/freshsales/#title_0)
    
*   [11 Top Freshsales Competitors & Alternatives in 2026](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/freshsales/#title_1)
    
*   [Conclusion](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/freshsales/#title_2)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![Best Freshsales Alternatives for Streamlining Your Sales Process](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24555/freshsales.776x0.webp)

Customer Relationship Management systems are pivotal for businesses looking to streamline their sales processes, enhance customer relationships, and drive sales growth. Choosing the optimal CRM system can profoundly influence your enterprise's operational effectiveness and client satisfaction.

Freshsales, by Freshworks, is a dynamic CRM solution designed for high-velocity sales teams. It offers a range of functionality including lead scoring, email tracking, and a robust AI-powered assistant. However, it may not be the perfect fit for every business due to certain limitations.

Why Do Businesses Look for Freshsales Alternatives?
---------------------------------------------------

Freshsales by Freshworks has been a popular choice for many, offering a suite of features designed to streamline sales processes and enhance customer interactions. However, with the tech landscape ever-evolving and businesses' needs growing more complex, a question arises: Is sticking with Freshsales still the best bet, or is it time to explore analogues? This deep dive aims to shed light on why companies are considering a switch, ensuring you're equipped with all the info you need to make an informed decision.

1.  **The Evolution of CRM Needs:** As companies grow and markets change, the demand for more sophisticated, flexible, and scalable CRM solutions becomes apparent. Freshsales, with its user-friendly interface and array of features, has served many companies well. Yet, some find themselves bumping against its limitations, seeking functionalities that align more closely with their unique workflows and industry-specific needs.
2.  **Budget Constraints and ROI Considerations:** While Freshsales by Freshworks offers competitive prices, the cost can still be a significant factor, especially for small businesses or startups keeping a tight rein on expenses. The quest for a CRM that offers a better balance between cost and functionality is a common journey for companies striving for efficiency and growth.
3.  **The Value of Responsive Customer Service:** The quality of customer support can make or break the CRM experience. Businesses dissatisfied with the level of support received from Freshsales by Freshworks might look for alternatives known for their stellar customer service and support ecosystems.
4.  **The Quest for Advanced Functionality:** As companies evolve, so do their CRM requirements. Advanced analytics, AI-driven insights, and automation functionality are becoming must-haves for companies looking to stay ahead. Alternatives that offer these next-gen features are increasingly appealing to those finding Freshsales by Freshworks a bit behind the curve.
5.  **Analytics and Reporting That Go the Extra Mile:** In-depth analytics and customizable reports are crucial for businesses keen on making data-driven decisions. CRM equivalents that offer more comprehensive data analysis tools can be a significant draw for companies needing more from their CRM in this area.
6.  **User Experience and Accessibility:** A CRM is only as good as its usability. An intuitive, accessible, and mobile-friendly platform can significantly enhance user adoption and productivity. Companies facing usability challenges with Freshsales might explore equivalents that prioritize a frictionless user experience.
7.  **The Mobile CRM:** With the rise of remote work and on-the-go business management, mobile CRM capabilities are more important than ever. Equivalents that offer superior mobile apps or more robust mobile functionalities could be a game-changer for businesses prioritizing flexibility and mobility.
8.  **Collaboration and Communication:** A CRM should serve as a collaboration hub, facilitating seamless communication and teamwork. Businesses finding Freshsales by Freshworks lacking in this aspect might seek out equivalents designed with team collaboration and communication at their core.

11 Top Freshsales Competitors & Alternatives in 2026
----------------------------------------------------

Before we explore similar apps, it's crucial to understand what makes a CRM solution the right fit for your business. It's not just about managing customer information; it's about enhancing customer relationships, streamlining sales processes, and ultimately driving growth. Consider factors such as ease of use, integration capabilities, customization, and cost-effectiveness.

### #1: Flowlu

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24555/flowlu-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/freshsales/#popup)

Flowlu provides an integrated business management solution that combines CRM with project management, finance, and powerful collaboration tools. It's designed to serve businesses of all sizes, offering a bird's eye view of operations and ensuring teams can work more efficiently and cohesively. The platform emphasizes automation and customization, aiming to streamline workflows and enhance productivity across different business functions.

#### Main Functionality:

*   **CRM Capabilities:** Flowlu offers comprehensive [CRM functionality](https://www.flowlu.com/features/crm/)
    , including contact and lead management, sales pipeline visualization, [client portal](https://www.flowlu.com/features/client-portal/)
    , sales automation, email and telephony integrations, and customer interaction tracking.
    
*   **Project Management:** Integrated [PM tools](https://www.flowlu.com/features/projects/)
     enable task assignment, progress tracking, and time management, facilitating seamless project execution.
    
*   **Financial Tools:** Flowlu includes [invoicing](https://www.flowlu.com/features/invoice/)
    , expense tracking, and financial reporting functionalities, providing a clear overview of a business's financial health.
    
*   **Collaboration:** [Team collaboration](https://www.flowlu.com/features/collaboration/)
     is supported through shared calendars, task commenting, and real-time notifications, ensuring everyone is aligned and informed.
    
*   **Automation:** Workflow automation helps in reducing repetitive tasks, from lead capture to invoicing, thereby improving efficiency.
    

#### Pros:

*   **All-in-One Solution:** Flowlu's broad range of features reduces the need for multiple separate tools, potentially lowering costs and simplifying operations.
    
*   **Customization:** The platform presents significant customization options like custom fields and lists, white-label, dashboards, allowing businesses (marketing, law, tech, creative, etc) to tailor it to their specific processes and requirements. It also provides an API for custom integrations.
    
*   **User-Friendly Interface:** Despite its extensive functionalities, Flowlu maintains a clean and intuitive interface, making it accessible for users of all technical levels.
    

#### Cons:

*   **Learning Curve:** The wide array of features and capabilities might overwhelm new users initially, requiring some time to fully leverage the platform's potential.
    

#### Pricing:

Flowlu's pricing structure is designed to accommodate a range of business sizes and requirements, starting with a free tier that includes basic CRM and PM functionality for a limited number of users. Paid plans begin at $29/month for the Team plan, which expands the feature set and user limit, with more comprehensive plans offering advanced functionalities like automation, financial management, and custom fields. The platform also presents an Enterprise plan with custom prices, tailored to larger organizations with specific requirements.

In summary, Flowlu stands out as a versatile alternative to Freshsales by Freshworks, particularly for businesses seeking an integrated platform to manage various aspects of their operations alongside CRM and marketing projects. Its blend of project management, financial tools, and powerful collaboration functionality, combined with CRM capabilities, makes it a comprehensive and powerful solution for businesses looking to streamline their processes and enhance team productivity.

### #2: Salesforce

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24555/salesforce.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/freshsales/#popup)

_via [Salesforce](https://www.salesforce.com/)
_

[Salesforce](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/salesforce/)
 stands as the giant in the CRM world, known for its extensive customization capabilities , comprehensive features, and robust ecosystem. Ideal for businesses of all sizes, Salesforce presents unparalleled scalability and a vast array of integrations, making it a compelling alternative to Freshsales by Freshworks.

#### Main Functionality:

*   Advanced analytics and reports.
    
*   Customer information and account planning.
    
*   Highly customizable to fit any business need and industry (tech, marketing, healthcare,etc.)
    

#### Pros:

*   **Extensive Customization:** Salesforce presents unparalleled customization capabilities, allowing businesses to tailor the CRM to their specific requirements.
    
*   **Robust Ecosystem:** A vast marketplace of applications and integrations (Salesforce AppExchange, API) enhances its functionality.
    
*   **Scalability:** Ideal for businesses of all sizes, it grows with your business, handling increasing complexity with ease.
    

#### Cons:

*   **Complexity:** The extensive features and customization options can be overwhelming for new users.
    
*   **Cost:** It can be expensive, especially for small businesses or those requiring advanced features.
    
*   **Resource Intensive:** To fully leverage its capabilities, businesses might need dedicated staff or consultants.
    

#### Pricing:

Salesforce presents several packages starting with the Essentials plan at $25/user/month (billed annually) to more advanced plans that can exceed $300/user/month for comprehensive enterprise features.

### #3: HubSpot CRM

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24555/hubspot-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/freshsales/#popup)

_via [Hubspot](https://www.hubspot.com/products/crm)
_

For businesses seeking an intuitive and easy-to-use CRM, HubSpot CRM is a standout choice. With its seamless integration with the HubSpot marketing suite, it presents a holistic approach to managing customer relationships, making it a great alternative for those prioritizing ease of use and comprehensive inbound marketing features.

#### Main Functionality:

*   Free tier available with essential CRM and email functionalities.
    
*   Inbuilt marketing, sales, and service hubs.
    
*   Intuitive user interface and easy setup.
    

#### Pros:

*   **Ease of Use:** Known for its user-friendly interface, making it accessible for users of all skill levels.
    
*   **Free Tier:** Offers a robust free version that's feature-rich compared to other CRMs.
    
*   **All-in-One Platform:** Integrates seamlessly with HubSpot's marketing, sales, and service hubs.
    
*   **Comprehensive Email Sync:** It presents seamless connections with Gmail and Outlook, enabling email tracking and notifications directly within the CRM.
    

#### Cons:

*   **Costly Add-ons:** The cost can escalate quickly as you add more advanced features and exceed the limits of the free plan.
    
*   **Limited Customization:** While easy to use, it presents less customization than more complex CRMs like Salesforce.
    
*   **Integration Limitations:** While it offers many integrations, some key options may require premium packages.
    

#### Pricing:

HubSpot CRM presents a free tier with basic features. Paid packages start at $45/month (billed annually) for the Starter plan, with more advanced plans increasing in price based on features and scale.

### #4: Zoho CRM

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24555/zoho-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/freshsales/#popup)

_via [Zoho CRM](https://www.zoho.com/crm/)
_

Zoho CRM presents a budget-friendly alternative without compromising on features. It's designed for businesses looking for an all-in-one powerful CRM solution that provides excellent value for money. With a range of customization capabilities and AI-powered insights, Zoho CRM caters to the needs of small to medium-sized businesses efficiently.

#### Main Functionality:

*   Affordable pricing with a generous free tier.
    
*   AI-powered sales assistant.
    
*   Extensive customization and automation capabilities for sales and marketing projects.
    

#### Pros:

*   **Affordability:** Offers competitive pricing with a generous free tier for up to 3 users.
    
*   **AI Features:** Zia, the AI sales assistant, provides valuable insights and automation.
    
*   **Customization and Automation:** Offers a high degree of customization, automation capabilities and API.
    

#### Cons:

*   **User Interface:** The interface can be less intuitive compared to competitors like HubSpot.
    
*   **Learning Curve:** Due to its extensive features, new users might find it a bit overwhelming.
    
*   **Support:** The quality of customer support can be inconsistent.
    

#### Pricing:

Zoho CRM's pricing starts with a free plan for up to 3 users. Paid packages begin at $14/user/month (billed annually) for the Standard plan, with more advanced features available in higher-tier plans.

### #5: Pipedrive

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24555/pipedrive-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/freshsales/#popup)

_via [Pipedrive](https://www.pipedrive.com/)
_

Pipedrive is built with a laser focus on sales management. It's known for its visual sales pipeline, which provides a clear overview of sales and marketing processes, making it an excellent choice for sales teams looking for a straightforward and powerful CRM solution.

#### Main Functionality:

*   Visual sales pipeline, email sync, API.
    
*   Easy to use with minimal setup required.
    
*   Strong focus on sales-related activities.
    

#### Pros:

*   **Sales Pipeline Management:** Offers a visual and intuitive sales pipeline that simplifies tracking deals.
    
*   **Ease of Use:** Focuses on user-friendliness and simplicity, ensuring a minimal learning curve.
    
*   **Customization:** Allows for significant customization of pipelines, fields, and activities.
    

#### Cons:

*   **Limited Reports:** While improving, its reports might not be as comprehensive as those of larger CRMs.
    
*   **Focused on Sales:** Primarily geared towards sales, it might lack depth in other areas like marketing or service.
    
*   **Integrations:** While it offers a range of integrations, some key software functionality might require workarounds or additional costs.
    

#### Pricing:

Pipedrive's pricing starts at $14.90/user/month (billed annually) for the Essential plan, with more advanced packages offering additional features.

### #6: Insightly

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24555/insightly-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/freshsales/#popup)

_via [Insightly](https://www.insightly.com/)
_

[Insightly](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/)
 stands out by combining CRM functionalities with project management features and email sync, making it ideal for companies that are project-driven. It offers a unique blend of powerful tools to manage customer interactions, marketing and projects in one platform.

#### Main Functionality:

*   Integrated PM features.
    
*   Customizable workflows and processes.
    
*   Comprehensive mobile app.
    

#### Pros:

*   **Project Management Features:** Insightly combines CRM functionalities with PM, offering a unified platform for customer, marketing and project data.
    
*   **Integration Capabilities:** Insightly offers a range of integrations with popular apps and services. The software also provides API capabilities.
    
*   **Customization:** Insightly provides a good level of customization to tailor the CRM to your company’s processes.
    
*   **Email Sync:** This functionality enables the monitoring of communications, the planning of emails, and the assessment of how successful email marketing initiatives are.
    

#### Cons:

*   **Learning Curve:** The combination of CRM and project management features can lead to a steeper learning curve.
    
*   **User Interface:** Some users may find the Insightly’s interface less intuitive compared to more streamlined CRMs.
    
*   **Performance Issues:** Users have reported occasional slowdowns and performance issues as the database size increases.
    

#### Pricing:

Insightly presents a no-cost package that accommodates up to two users. Insightly’ \[aid plans start at $29/user/month (billed annually) for the Plus plan, with more features available in higher-tier packages.\
\
### #7: Agile CRM\
\

\

### #8: Copper\

### #9: Streak\
\
[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24555/streak-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/freshsales/#popup)\

_via [Streak](https://www.streak.com/)\

\
Streak transforms Gmail into a powerful CRM, offering a unique software for companies looking for simplicity and minimal disruption to their existing workflows. It's particularly suitable for growing businesses and solopreneurs who rely heavily on email communication.\

*   **Dependency on Gmail:** Its effectiveness is tied to Gmail, limiting its appeal to companies using other email platforms.\

Exploring user reviews, seeking demos, and comparing feature sets in detail can help ensure that the move is a step towards enhanced efficiency, growth, and customer satisfaction.\

While Freshsales offers a solid foundation, the equivalents listed above provide a range of choices tailored to different business needs, sizes, and industries. Flowlu positions itself as an all-in-one business management software, designed to cater to various aspects of business operations, including PM, invoicing, and financial tracking, in addition to customer relationship management. This holistic approach makes it a versatile choice for companies looking to consolidate multiple tools into a single platform.\

As you ponder the possibility of transitioning to a new CRM platform, remember that the goal is not just to find a replacement for Freshsales but to discover a powerful software that propels your business forward in an ever-changing market landscape.\

FAQs\
\
See the most answers to the most frequently asked questions. You can find even more information in the knowledge base.\

\
What are some popular alternatives to Freshsales for CRM solutions?\
\
Popular alternatives to Freshsales by Freshworks include Flowlu, Salesforce, HubSpot CRM, Zoho CRM, Pipedrive, and Insightly. Each of these platforms offers a unique set of features tailored to different business needs, such as sales automation, customer service, email sync, marketing automation, and analytics.\
\
What are the key functionality to look for in a Freshsales alternative?\
\
Main functionality to consider when looking for a Freshsales alternative include contact and deal management, email sync and tracking, lead scoring, sales pipeline visualization, marketing functionality, automation capabilities, reports and analytics, mobile access, and integrations with other tools and platforms your business uses. Depending on your specific business needs, you may also want to look for industry-specific functionalities or advanced AI capabilities.\
\
How to choose the best Freshsales alternative?\
\
When selecting the best Freshsales alternatives, it's crucial to delve into a comprehensive list of programs and technologies, ensuring you don't settle for a mere clone. Beyond just scanning through various sites, consider the scale of your operations; big or small, there's powerful software tailored to your needs. Avoid focusing solely on cheap choices, as the value they offer might be limited. Instead, evaluate each alternative based on its unique functionality and integration capabilities, which should stand out except for being just another name in the vast sea of CRM software. This approach ensures you harness the most effective tools to enhance your customer relationship management strategies.\
\
Can I migrate my data from Freshsales to another CRM easily?\
\
Yes, most CRM platforms including Flowlu, Salesforce, Insightly, HubSpot CRM, Zoho CRM, and Pipedrive offer data migration tools and services to facilitate the transfer of your data from Freshsales by Freshworks. However, the complexity of the migration process can vary depending on the amount of data and custom configurations you have. It's advisable to consult with the support team of the new CRM or their website’s help center for guidance and to consider using professional migration services for a smooth transition.\
\
How do the customer support options for Freshsales alternatives compare?\
\
Customer support options can vary among Freshsales equivalents. Flowlu, HubSpot CRM, Zoho CRM, Insightly and Pipedrive provide robust support through live chat, email, and online knowledge bases. Salesforce and Microsoft Dynamics 365 are known for their comprehensive support offerings, including 24/7 support, dedicated account managers for enterprise clients, and extensive online resources. When evaluating analogs, consider the level of support your business requires and check if the provider offers support in your preferred channels and languages.\
\
Are there any free CRM alternatives to Freshsales?\
\
Yes, there are several free CRM alternatives to Freshsales by Freshworks, including Flowlu, which offers a robust free plan with essential CRM functionality, and Zoho CRM, which also provides a free tier suitable for growing companies and startups in marketing, IT and other industries. \
\
Can I integrate other business tools with CRM alternatives to Freshsales?\
\
Yes, most CRM alternatives to Freshsales by Freshworks offer integration capabilities with a wide range of business tools, including email platforms, social media, accounting software, and marketing automation tools, allowing for a seamless workflow and data exchange across different systems.\
\
What are the best CRMs for prospect management?\
\
Prospect management is a critical component of sales and marketing strategies. It involves tracking potential customers and systematically moving them through the sales pipeline. Effective prospect management ensures that no opportunity is missed and that each prospect receives the right attention at the right time.\
\
When choosing a CRM for prospect management, several main functions should be considered:\
\
1.  Contact Management: The core of any CRM, this feature allows for the organization and retrieval of customer information.\
2.  Lead Scoring: This helps in prioritizing prospects based on their likelihood to convert, ensuring that efforts are concentrated on the most promising leads.\
3.  Pipeline Management: A visual representation of where prospects stand in the sales process helps in forecasting and strategy planning.\
4.  Email Sync: This feature ensures seamless communication with prospects without leaving the CRM environment.\
\
Several CRMs stand out for their prospect management capabilities: Flowlu, Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoho CRM, Pipedrive.\

![Best Asana Alternative for Teams and Businesses](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24465/asana.776x0.webp)

If you need an Asana alternative that doesn’t expand your bill as your work grows, start by listing what you actually manage day to day: projects, tasks, clients, budgets, and reporting.

Many teams begin with a task tool, then add more tools for CRM, invoices, and time tracking. That stack can cost more than the core management software itself.

This guide compares leading options and shows which tools can prove a stronger fit for teams and small businesses.

### Best picks by use case

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **Use case** | **Tool** |
| Task-only teamwork and cross-team coordination | _Asana_ |
| Client delivery plus CRM, time tracking, and invoicing in one place | _Flowlu_ |
| Software teams running Scrum or Kanban with backlog planning | _Jira_ |
| Database-style work tracking with flexible views and automations | _Airtable_ |
| Simple personal task lists and lightweight planning | _Todoist_ |

Why Look for an Alternative to Asana?
-------------------------------------

### Common Limitations of Asana

Asana is strong for task management and cross-functional project planning, with list, calendar, and Kanban views, plus paid tiers for deeper workflows and portfolio management.

But once teams manage client work end-to-end, the gaps show up quickly.

Asana is primarily a task and project management tool, so many business-critical workflows still require additional tools, add-ons, or manual work, such as:

*   **Budgets and financial visibility:** tracking project costs, profitability, or cash flow typically happens outside the platform.
*   **Billing and invoicing:** estimates, invoices, and payments usually require separate software.
*   **Client-facing work:** sharing progress, collecting approvals, and keeping clients in the loop can turn into extra admin work.
*   **Time tracking depth:** teams often need another tool to track time consistently, report on time, or connect time to billing.
*   **Tool sprawl:** as needs grow, teams stack CRM, finance, and reporting tools around Asana—and spend extra time keeping data consistent.

If your team mainly needs task lists and simple project coordination, Asana can be a good fit.

But if your workflows include clients, revenue, and reporting, it’s common to start searching for a better alternative to Asana that keeps everything in one place.

### When Asana Becomes Expensive for Growing Teams

Here’s the problem with Asana: the [pricing](https://help.asana.com/s/article/asana-subscriptions-and-pricing?language=en_US)
 hits you twice.

First, you can’t just pay for the users you actually have.

Asana sells licenses in fixed increments, so it’s not possible to add just a single seat to your subscription.

Say your team grows from 10 to 11 people. With Asana , you’re suddenly stuck paying for 15 seats instead of 11. That’s 4 empty seats costing you about $1,200 a year in wasted money on Advanced subscription.

Second, even the seats you do use aren’t cheap.

Asana’s Starter tier is $10.99 per user per month ($13.49 monthly), and Advanced jumps to $24.99 per user per month ($30.49 monthly). As your team grows, that per-user cost multiplies fast.

The problem is you’ll probably still need separate tools for CRM, finance, and time tracking.

### Missing Business Tools Beyond Task Management

If you run client work, internal operations, or multiple departments, you’re not only managing projects.

You’re also managing requests, pipelines, estimates, invoices, profitability, and a client portal.

Reviews of Asana alternatives often call out missing [reliable native time tracking and budget management](https://www.reddit.com/r/Asana/comments/1loeo36/time_tracking_within_asana/)
 as reasons teams switch.

What Is the Best Alternative to Asana?
--------------------------------------

The best Asana alternative is the one that matches your workflow, your team size, and your pricing ceiling.

### What Makes a Good Asana Alternative

The right pick depends on whether you need a task app, a project management suite, or a full business platform.

The best option usually:

*   keeps project and task work simple for the whole team
*   scales workflows, reporting, and permissions without creating admin overhead
*   reduces tool sprawl by covering CRM, time tracking, finance, and collaboration in one place

### Key Criteria: Pricing, Features, Scalability

Compare:

1.  pricing per user and what each plan includes
2.  free plan limits (users, storage, automation, client access)
3.  features that matter for your workflow (views, templates, workflows, automations, time tracking, reporting)

A quick project management software checklist for teams:

*   **Project setup:** create a project, add tasks, assign a task owner, and set start time and due time.
*   **Views:** list, Kanban, calendar, and timeline views so every team can work their way.
*   **Workflows:** status workflows that match how your team actually runs projects, plus workflow rules that reduce manual work.
*   **Templates:** reusable project templates for onboarding, marketing, operations, and client delivery.
*   **Time tracking:** built-in time tracking so users can log time, review time reports, and bill time when needed.
*   **Reporting:** dashboards that show project health, time spent, and workload for each team.
*   **Business context:** CRM and finance features that connect deals, invoices, and project delivery.
*   **Enterprise controls:** permissions, roles, audit history, and governance for enterprise rollouts.

Track how the software performs in a week or two and whether the software still feels good to use.

--------------------------------------

### All-in-One Project and Business Management

 inside a unified workspace. The plans are built around real business needs: client work, internal requests, and repeatable workflows.

Flowlu’s [paid plans](https://www.flowlu.com/pricing/)
 add more project management features without splitting core business management into separate add-ons:

*   **Essential** includes Gantt, dependencies, sprints, project workflows, finance reports, and task time tracking.

### Built-in CRM, Finance and Client Portal

Flowlu’s pricing plans include [CRM functions](https://www.flowlu.com/features/crm/)
 and [financial management features](https://www.flowlu.com/features/finance/)
, plus a secure [client portal](https://www.flowlu.com/features/client-portal/)
 for updates, approvals, estimates, and invoices.

That matters when your projects involve external stakeholders and you want fewer emails and less status chasing.

### Task Management, Kanban, and Workflows

Flowlu covers [task management](https://www.flowlu.com/features/tasks/)
 with Kanban, Gantt, calendar, and list views, plus project workflows and automation rules to route work, assign tasks, and keep processes consistent.

For teams that run client delivery, a template library matters.

A template turns a repeatable project into a repeatable process: the same task list, the same workflows, the same time tracking steps, and the same reporting every time.

You can build templates for onboarding, monthly reporting, content production, sprint planning, and billing cycles, then reuse those templates across projects to protect team productivity.

### Time Tracking and Reporting

Flowlu includes [time tracking](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/438--time-tracker/)
 with task timers, logs, filters, exports, and billing workflows that connect tracked time to invoices.

That means better reporting on project effort, profitability, and workload without a separate tracking tool.

If your main goal is productivity, measure it in time: fewer status meetings, less copy/paste between tools, and less time spent reconciling project data.

When the team can move from task to invoice without leaving the platform, productivity improves for every user, and reporting stays accurate for all users.

Asana vs Flowlu: Detailed Comparison
------------------------------------

If you want an Asana project management alternative that covers client work end to end, focus on what’s built in versus what requires extra tools.

### Feature Comparison Table

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Category** | **Asana** | **Flowlu** |
| Seat flexibility | Rigid. Seats sold in blocks. | Flexible. Any number of seats can be added to any paid subscription. |
| Core task management | Tasks, projects, list, board, calendar views | Tasks, projects, Kanban, Gantt, calendar, list views, project milestones |
| Project planning | Gantt timeline in paid tiers | Gantt chart, sprints/backlog in paid tiers |
| Workflows & automation | Rules and workflow builder; AI Studio for AI workflows | Workflow automation across apps, triggers, actions, reusable workflows |
| CRM | Not built-in (typically separate tool) | Built-in CRM with pipelines and email sync |
| Financial visibility | Limited. Requires integrations to see project budgets vs. actual spend. | Real-time. Built-in project cash flow, expense tracking, and profitability reports. |
| Time-to-revenue | Manual. Timers exist, but require 3rd-party apps or manual exports to bill clients. | Automated. Native integration between task timers, billable rates, and final invoicing. |
| Client portal | Guest access; dedicated client portal typically via other tools | Built-in client portal for updates, approvals, estimates, invoices |
| Reporting | Dashboards and reporting | Detailed reports and customizable dashboard widgets |

### Free Plan Comparison

Asana’s Personal plan is free forever and supports 2 users with unlimited tasks & projects and list/board/calendar views.

Flowlu’s Free plan is also free and is built as a 2-user workspace, with CRM basics (100 contacts, 1 pipeline), tasks & projects with 1 workflow and Kanban/list views, plus invoices and expense tracking.

### Pricing per User per Month

Flowlu Essential is listed at $9 per user per month billed yearly, and Flowlu Advanced is $17 per user per month billed yearly.

Asana Starter is $10.99 per user per month billed annually ($13.49 monthly) and Advanced is $24.99 per user per month billed annually ($30.49 monthly).

### Which Platform Is Better for Small Teams?

For a best Asana alternative for small teams, check how many projects, users, and workflows you can run before you hit plan limits.

If your small team is mainly tracking tasks, most tools work.

But if you need CRM, invoicing, and time tracking tied to projects, Flowlu is often a stronger fit because it reduces extra tools, keeps data connected, and simplifies reporting across the whole business.

Best Free Alternative to Asana
------------------------------

If you’re starting small, the free plan should help you build a workflow you can keep using as your team grows.

Many tools offer free task tracking, but growth usually forces you into paid upgrades plus separate tools for CRM, billing, or reporting.

Flowlu works well as a free alternative to Asana because it supports projects and tasks while also covering the basics of client operations—so you can scale without rebuilding your process.

### What You Get in the Free Plan

With Flowlu’s Free plan you get:

*   a 2-user workspace
*   CRM basics for leads and contacts
*   tasks and projects with Kanban/list views, stages and milestones
*   invoices, estimates, and expense tracking
*   collaboration basics and a basic internal wiki

### Is Flowlu a Good Free Alternative to Asana?

Yes. For teams that want a free tool that also covers basic project management, CRM, and finance.

In practice, Flowlu can replace Asana on a budget because you can manage projects, clients, and business operations without adding extra software as early.

Cheap and Lightweight Alternatives to Asana
-------------------------------------------

If you want a budget-friendly option with fewer moving parts, focus on the simplest workflow that still matches your project needs.

### Cheap Alternative to Asana for Startups

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24465/flowlu.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/asana/#popup)

Other Asana Alternative Programs
--------------------------------

If you’re comparing alternatives, these are the tools that show up most often in review roundups and team recommendations. Each one fits a slightly different workflow.

### ClickUp

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24465/clickup.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/asana/#popup)

_

 is popular for its robust free plan and wide feature set, including many views and automations. Expect a learning curve as you configure the platform for your team workflows.

### Trello

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24465/trello.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/asana/#popup)

_via [Trello](https://trello.com/)
_

[Trello](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/trello/)
 is a classic Kanban board tool with Power-Ups and Butler automation. It’s a good lightweight option for card-based workflows, but some teams outgrow it when they need deeper cross-board visibility, more structured planning, or advanced reporting.

**Pricing:** _Free is $0 (up to 10 collaborators per Workspace); Standard is $5/user/month billed annually ($6 billed monthly), Premium is $10/user/month billed annually, and Enterprise is $17.50/user/month billed annually._

### Monday.com

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24465/monday.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/asana/#popup)

 is widely used for flexible, visual work management with customizable boards and multiple views; the experience can feel complex if you heavily customize workflows early.

### Jira

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24465/jira.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/asana/#popup)

_via [Jira](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira)
_

[Jira](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/jira/)
 is a standard choice for software teams running Scrum or Kanban, with strong boards, sprints, and backlog planning. Outside dev workflows, it often requires more configuration and can feel heavy for non-technical teams.

**Pricing:** _Free is available for 10 users; Standard is $7.91/user/month and Premium is $14.54/user/month (with Enterprise available)._

### Airtable

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24465/airtable.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/asana/#popup)

_via [Airtable](https://www.airtable.com/)
_

[Airtable](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/airtable/)
 is great for database-style workflows and data-heavy project tracking, with flexible views and automation options. The trade-off is that you’re often building and maintaining your own system (structure, permissions, and upkeep).

**Pricing:** _Free plan is available; Team is $20/user/month billed annually, Business is $45/user/month billed annually, and Enterprise Scale is custom._

Why Flowlu Is the Best Asana Alternative
----------------------------------------

So, what's the best alternative to Asana when you want fewer subscriptions, cleaner workflows, and real business visibility?

For many teams, it’s Flowlu because it combines project management, CRM, and finance.

### More Features Without Extra Tools

Flowlu is built for client work and internal operations, so you can manage projects, tasks, workflows, time tracking, invoices, and a client portal in one place.

What this means in practice:

*   fewer subscriptions to manage
*   fewer manual handoffs between tools
*   clearer reporting on time, workload, and profitability

### Scalable for Small Teams and Enterprises

Flowlu works as a best Asana alternative for small teams because you can start simple (tasks, projects, kanban, workflows) and add structure as your work grows.

For larger organizations, higher tiers support advanced workflows, deeper analytics, portfolio visibility, and enterprise controls such as role-based access and governance.

If you’re evaluating it for enterprise rollout, prioritize:

*   role-based permissions and external users
*   portfolio reporting across projects
*   planned vs logged time tracking and accountability
*   knowledge base and standardized templates

### Start Free and Grow at Your Own Pace

You can start on the free plan to test real workflows with your team, then move to Essential or Advanced when you need more automation, reporting, and client-facing features.

This pricing model keeps core business tools close to your project work, meaning scaling up doesn’t require rebuilding your process in a new platform.

For many small teams, Flowlu is a strong fit because it covers project management and business management in one place.

# Fantastical Calendar Alternative: Top Free Apps for 2026

Top Free Alternative Fantastical Calendar Apps for Time & Task Management
=========================================================================

[![Nick Rockwell](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/42459/nickrockwell-1.jpg)](https://www.flowlu.com/authors/nick-rockwell/)
[Nick Rockwell\
\
Writer](https://www.flowlu.com/authors/nick-rockwell/)

August 15, 2025

10 min read

Table of contents

*   [What’s Fantastical All About?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/fantastical/#title_0)
    
*   [Where Does Fantastical Fall Flat?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/fantastical/#title_1)
    
*   [How to Choose the Best Fantastical Alternative](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/fantastical/#title_2)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![Top Free Alternative Fantastical Calendar Apps for Time &amp; Task Management](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/42436/fantastical.776x0.webp)

Fantastical is a great app with powerful calendar and scheduling features, but is it the right choice for your work?

If you’re looking for a tool with richer team collaboration capabilities or friendlier pricing, we’ve collected the top free Fantastical alternatives to help you manage tasks, events, and times efficiently.

You may have stumbled upon Fantastical, and used it to keep your calendar and tasks in sync. It is a go-to tool for [scheduling daily activities](https://www.flowlu.com/blog/productivity/calendar-management-tools/)
. However, as many users discover, the optimal planning solution meshes with the workflow. Does Fantastical have this effect?

Perhaps you’re looking for more control over how tasks fit it, a different approach to managing time zones, or simply a calendar that feels more like a standalone system rather than just an interface.

We’ll explore where Fantastical is really great, the nuanced reasons why some users ultimately seek out alternatives, and how you can pinpoint the ideal planning solution for your unique needs.

What’s Fantastical All About?
-----------------------------

Fantastical is a daily planner and calendar app that’s famous for its natural language input tech. You can create events, tasks and reminders by typing conversational phrases like “Dentist’s appointment at 5 PM Wednesday”, and the event will be registered automatically.

It’s the central hub for your schedule, a sophisticated “front-end” that integrates with your existing calendar platforms like Google Calendar, Microsoft Outlook and Apple’s native calendars so you have a unified view across all your devices.

Fantastical is available on iOS, Mac, Windows, Apple Watch, Apple Vision Pro and the web.

Beyond its beautiful and easy to use interface, Fantastical has a bunch of features to help you be more productive. These include AI driven features to suggest time slots, block focus time, simplify event creation and even forward emails to create calendar entries.

Where Does Fantastical Fall Flat?
---------------------------------

*   While great for personal planning, Fantastical isn’t perfect for team-centric workflows. It leaves much to be desired in terms of more seamless ways to view availability of another person, similar to Google Calendar.
*   The app offers a trial, but a persistent free tier with comprehensive features is notably absent. Users online debate whether the annual subscription fee is worth it compared to free native calendar apps which have caught up in features.
*   Fantastical can be integrated with major platforms such as Google Calendar, Microsoft Outlook and Apple fairly well. But there is a lack of direct connections with other popular [productivity tools](https://www.flowlu.com/blog/productivity/best-ai-productivity-tools/)
     compared to competitors.
*   The mobile version of the Fantastical planner is not well adapted for on-the-go productivity. Some features may be missing, while some may not work as expected.

How to Choose the Best Fantastical Alternative
----------------------------------------------

*   Opt for an alternative that not only allows you to create tasks and subtasks with deadlines but also present them in your calendar view without creating clutter.
*   Prioritize tools with many sync options to both popular and less popular calendar applications.
*   Seek out solutions with features for more than simple meeting scheduling, these are team task assignments, file sharing, comments, or even dedicated chats.
*   Consider calendar platforms with free tiers that have essential functions, or affordable per-team plans with a feature set large enough to cover the costs.
*   Favor calendars with native applications for the devices you use (iOS, Android, Mac, and Windows), and a functional web interface for effortless access.

If you take these points into consideration, task management, extensive calendar integration, team support, friendly pricing, and an intuitive, cross-device access, you will find an alternative that not only directly addresses Fantastical’s shortcomings, but also takes your personal planning to another level.

Routine blends a task manager and a calendar into a single tool. You can jot down notes, schedule meetings, and block time for work—all in the same place. ![Any.do](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36099/anydo.jpg)

Any.do keeps things simple. It’s a mix of a calendar, to-do list, and reminder app that helps you keep track of everything without getting lost in settings. SkedPal is for people who like their schedule to adapt in real time. It takes your to-do list and automatically finds the best time for each task, based on your priorities and available time. Clockwise

Clockwise frees up focus time by reorganizing your meetings. It moves flexible events around to create longer blocks for deep work, so you’re not constantly switching gears. It also helps teams coordinate schedules without endless back-and-forth. If your calendar is a mess of overlapping meetings, Clockwise brings some order to the chaos.

Amie makes scheduling feel more personal. It blends a calendar, tasks, and even social features, so you can see what friends and teammates are up to. FAQs

See the most answers to the most frequently asked questions. You can find even more information in the knowledge base.

[Knowledge base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

Is Fantastical still worth it?

Whether Fantastical is “worth it” largely depends on your current priorities. If you can't live without its natural-language input for scheduling, suggested time slots to view accessibility of teammates, or event creation from emails, then the premium subscription can be justified.

However, the subscription cost is a bit too high, too expensive for a calendar. The free tier also can feel unsatisfactory, with limited functionality forcing users to make a decision - pay or leave. 

If you’re in need of a robust task management system, want more integrations with third-party apps, or a genuinely free plan, you will find better value by looking at alternatives.

What is the best free calendar app?

For many users seeking the best free calendar app, the answer is simple, an app that is native to most devices or widely adopted by the people. Google Calendar is a perfect example, with a clean intuitive interface, cross-device sync, and handy reminders across web and mobile.

If you’re already invested in the Microsoft toolset, the Outlook Calendar is a strong contender. Its main advantages come in the form of seamless integration with Microsoft Teams messenger and other Office apps.

For macOS and iOS users, Apple Calendar is a good enough choice, featuring native device support, easy sharing, and reliable sync across Apple devices. If you stick with it, it can cover your basic scheduling needs.

Is Flowlu a good substitute for Fantastical?

Yes, Flowlu’s calendar is not just a basic scheduler. It acts as a part of a unified platform where you can manage tasks, projects, and clients alongside your events. While Fantastical delivers a solid calendar experience, Flowlu lets you tie your schedule directly into large-scope project processes without having to use multiple applications.

# Clockify Alternative: Why Users Switch in 2026

Clockify Alternative: Why Users Are Switching and How to Find the Right Fit
===========================================================================

![Leah Brooks](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36089/leah_brooks-1.png)

Leah Brooks

Writer

February 24, 2025

11 min read

Table of contents

*   [What Is Clockify, Anyway?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/clockify/#title_0)
    
*   [Why Are People Ditching Clockify?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/clockify/#title_1)
    
*   [How Do You Pick a Better Alternative?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/clockify/#title_2)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![Clockify Alternative: Why Users Are Switching and How to Find the Right Fit](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36071/clockify.776x0.webp)

Not loving Clockify? You’re not alone. Whether it’s clunky reports, syncing issues, or just not the right fit, plenty of users are looking for a Clockify alternative that actually works. Let’s break down why—and how to find a better time tracker.

Time tracking. It’s not the most exciting thing in the world, but it sure matters. Whether you’re running a business or juggling multiple projects, keeping track of hours helps keep everything (and everyone) on track.

A lot of people use Clockify for this. It’s free (for the most part), it works on different devices, and it does the job. Or at least, that’s the idea. But if you’re reading this, I’m guessing you’ve already run into a few headaches with it. You’re not alone.

Let’s talk about why some people are looking for alternatives—and how to find one that doesn’t make you want to throw your laptop out the window.

What Is Clockify, Anyway?
-------------------------

Clockify is a time-tracking tool that lets you log hours, track projects, and pull reports. It’s popular, mostly because it offers a decent free plan and works for teams. You can use it to manually enter time, set up automatic tracking, or get reports on where your hours are going. Simple enough, right?

Except… not always.

Why Are People Ditching Clockify?
---------------------------------

No tool is perfect. And while some folks love Clockify, others have a few complaints. I took a deep dive into real user reviews (from places like G2, Capterra, and Trustpilot) to see what’s bugging people the most. Here’s what I found:

### 1\. The Interface Feels Clunky

Some users say Clockify’s interface isn’t as intuitive as they’d like. Especially for newbies.

_“The interface is kind of confusing at first. You have to dig around to find what you need.”_ – [G2 Review](https://www.g2.com/products/clockify/reviews)

### 2\. Syncing Issues (Especially on Mobile)

If you switch between your phone and your laptop, you might run into syncing delays. And that’s frustrating when you’re trying to log time accurately.

_“Sometimes the mobile app doesn’t sync right away, and I end up with missing time logs.”_ – [G2 Review](https://www.g2.com/products/clockify/reviews)

### 3\. Reports Aren’t Flexible Enough

Need custom reports? You might be out of luck. A lot of users want more ways to organize and export their data.

_“I wish the reporting was more customizable. Right now, it’s pretty limited.”_ – [G2 Review](https://www.g2.com/products/clockify/reviews)

### 4\. Employee Monitoring Feels a Bit… Creepy

Clockify has features like GPS tracking and screenshots. Some users feel this crosses a line, making employees feel like they’re being watched. And honestly? That’s fair.

_“I get why some companies use it, but monitoring employees like this just makes people anxious.”_ – [Toggl Blog](https://toggl.com/blog/clockify-alternatives)

So, if you’ve been feeling frustrated with Clockify, you’re not imagining things. Other people have the same gripes.

How Do You Pick a Better Alternative?
-------------------------------------

Alright, so Clockify isn’t working for you. But what should you look for in a replacement? Here are a few things I’d keep in mind:

### Ease of Use

If a time-tracking tool makes you spend more time tracking time than actually working… that’s a problem. Pick something that’s easy to navigate.

### Features That Actually Matter

Do you need detailed reports? Automatic time tracking? [Invoicing](https://www.flowlu.com/features/invoice/)
? Figure out what you actually need, so you don’t end up paying for features you’ll never touch.

### Works on Every Device

A tool that doesn’t sync properly between your phone and computer? Useless. Double-check that whatever you choose plays nice with all your devices.

### Pricing That Makes Sense

Some tools charge per user. Others offer unlimited tracking for a flat rate. Make sure the pricing structure fits your team size and budget.

### No Sketchy Privacy Stuff

If employee monitoring makes you uneasy, skip tools that include it. There are plenty of great options that respect privacy.

Clockify works fine for some people. But if you’re tired of fighting with the interface, waiting on sync delays, or wishing for better reports, it might be time for a switch.

Take advantage of free trials. Test a few options. And don’t settle for something that makes tracking time feel like a second job. Because honestly? You’ve got better things to do.

Top Clockify Competitors & Alternatives for Time Tracking in 2025
-----------------------------------------------------------------

![Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36037/flowlu.png)

Flowlu

Flowlu is more than just a time tracker—it handles tasks, projects, and client management all in one place. You can track time on tasks, create invoices, send estimates, and keep an eye on payments without switching between apps. It also includes a built-in CRM to manage leads and customers, plus automation to streamline business processes. Unlike most of the software solutions that focus on time tracking and invoicing, Flowlu covers everything from project planning to getting paid.

Zoho One is a full-fledged business platform with over 40 integrated apps, covering everything from sales to marketing to project management. It streamlines your operations by keeping all your tools under one login. Customize and scale the platform as your business grows, without worrying about the complexity of managing multiple systems.

Scoro helps you optimize every aspect of your business with ease.

Nifty combines task management, collaboration, and project timelines in one intuitive platform. Plan and track your projects from start to finish with real-time updates that keep everyone aligned. Nifty’s easy-to-use interface and automation features make it ideal for growing teams that need flexibility.

![Bitrix24](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32084/bitrix24.png)

Bitrix24

Integrated business collaboration

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/bitrix24/)

Bitrix24 combines project management, communication, and CRM into a single, integrated platform. Manage tasks, collaborate with your team, and keep client information organized—all without leaving the app. Designed for small teams and large organizations alike, Bitrix24 offers the tools you need to streamline operations.

![Plutio](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32087/plutio_2x-1.png)

Plutio

Freelancer business management

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/plutio/)

Plutio is built for freelancers and small teams who need an all-in-one solution to manage tasks, clients, and invoices. It offers everything you need to run your business seamlessly, including project tracking and communication, without the need to switch between tools. Customizable workflows allow you to manage projects your way, and integrated invoicing helps you get paid faster.

![Avaza](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32093/avaza_2x-1.png)

Avaza

Cloud-based team collaboration

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/avaza/)

Avaza brings together project management, team collaboration, and invoicing into a single, cloud-based platform. Organize tasks, track time, and manage client communications without the need for separate tools. Its clean interface and powerful features make it a great choice for teams seeking to simplify workflows and boost productivity.

![Paymo](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32094/paymo_2x-1.png)

Paymo

Time tracking with billing

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/paymo/)

Paymo is designed for teams and freelancers who need to manage projects, track time, and handle billing from one central platform. It’s perfect for businesses that want a seamless way to stay on top of tasks, deadlines, and client invoicing. Paymo’s real-time tracking and reporting give you full visibility into project progress and profitability. It’s an all-in-one solution for those who want to manage work and payments effortlessly.

![Wrike](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32098/wrike.png)

Wrike

Real-time team alignment

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/wrike/)

Wrike is built for teams needing detailed visibility into their projects. It offers tools for managing tasks, tracking time, and collaborating in real time, all from one platform. Wrike’s customizable dashboards and reporting make it easy to keep everyone aligned and on top of deadlines.

![Monday](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32099/mondey.png)

Monday

Visual task management

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/monday-com/)

Monday is a visual project management tool that adapts to your team's workflow. It lets you track projects, set up automations, and manage everything from daily tasks to big projects on a colorful, user-friendly interface. Its flexibility makes it ideal for teams of all sizes looking to streamline processes.

![ClickUp](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32100/clickup.png)

ClickUp

Customizable productivity hub

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/clickup/)

ClickUp is an all-in-one productivity platform where you can manage tasks, docs, goals, and more. It’s designed to replace multiple apps by combining project management, communication, and automation in one place. Customizable views and extensive integrations help teams tailor their workspace to fit their requirements.

![Teamwork](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32101/teamwork_2x-1.png)

Teamwork

End-to-end project tracking

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/)

Teamwork is a project management platform designed to streamline team collaboration. It allows companies to manage tasks, track time, and stay on top of project timelines with ease. With its client-focused features, Teamwork makes managing complex projects and keeping everyone aligned simple.

![Harvest](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36038/harvest_2x.png)

Harvest

Track Time, Budget, And Invoices

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/harvest/)

Harvest keeps things simple: track time, send invoices, and keep an eye on budgets without extra hassle. It works well for teams that need to track billable hours and project costs. Plus, the reporting makes it easy to see where time (and money) is going.

![Toggl Track](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36040/toggl_track_2x.png)

Toggl Track

Simple Tracking With Smart Reminders

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/toggl/)

Toggl Track is great if you want to track time without overcomplicating things. You just click a button to start tracking, and it works across web, mobile, and desktop. It also has reminders and automatic tracking to catch any gaps, so you don’t have to rely on memory.

![RescueTime](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36041/rescuetime_2x.png)

RescueTime

Automatic Tracking For Better Focus

RescueTime runs in the background and tracks how you actually spend your time on your computer and phone. Instead of manual entries, it automatically logs time spent on different apps and websites. It’s great for figuring out where your focus goes and cutting down on distractions.

![Everhour](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36042/everhour_2x.png)

Everhour

Track Time Inside Project Tools

Everhour works well if you need time tracking built into your project management tool. It integrates with apps like Asana and Trello, so you can track time without switching tabs. Plus, it helps with budgeting and invoicing, making it useful for teams working on billable projects.

![TimeCamp](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36043/timecamp_2x.png)

TimeCamp

Smart Tracking With Productivity Insights

TimeCamp is built for tracking both billable and non-billable hours, with automatic tracking to fill in the gaps. It also has productivity reports and integrations with popular project management tools. If you need a mix of time tracking and budget monitoring, it covers both.

![Hubstaff](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36044/hubstaff_2x.png)

Hubstaff

Time Tracking With Team Monitoring

Hubstaff is a time tracker with built-in productivity monitoring, making it useful for remote teams. It logs time, tracks activity levels, and even has GPS tracking for field teams. It’s a good fit if you need to keep an eye on work hours and productivity in one place.

![FreshBooks](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36045/freshbooks_2x.png)

FreshBooks

Track Time And Invoice Clients

FreshBooks is known for invoicing, but it also has built-in time tracking. You can log hours, attach them to projects, and turn them into invoices with a few clicks. It’s handy for freelancers and small businesses that need to track billable hours and get paid without extra steps.

![QuickBooks Time](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36046/quickbooks_time_2x.png)

QuickBooks Time

Employee Tracking With Payroll Sync

QuickBooks Time (formerly TSheets) is a time tracker that works well for businesses managing hourly employees. It has GPS tracking for teams on the go and integrates with QuickBooks for easy payroll processing. Whether you’re tracking work hours or billable time, it keeps things organized.

![Clockodo](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36047/clockodo_2x.png)

Clockodo

Easy Tracking With Project Reports

Clockodo is a straightforward time tracker that helps small businesses and freelancers keep track of hours worked. You can track time by client, project, or task, and the reporting makes it easy to see where time is going. It also integrates with invoicing tools to simplify billing.

FAQs

See the most answers to the most frequently asked questions. You can find even more information in the knowledge base.

[Knowledge base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

What are the best Clockify alternatives for time tracking?

The best Clockify alternatives depend on your needs. Some popular options offer better reporting, automation, or integrations. Look for tools that fit your workflow, whether you need a simple tracker or an all-in-one business management solution.

Is Flowlu a good alternative to Clockify?

Yes! Flowlu is a solid Clockify alternative, especially if you need more than just time tracking. It includes project management, invoicing, CRM, and team collaboration—making it a great all-in-one solution for businesses looking to streamline their workflow.

Why do people switch from Clockify to other time-tracking tools?

Users look for alternatives due to issues like limited reporting, mobile sync problems, or a confusing interface. Some also prefer tools with better integrations or additional features like invoicing, budgeting, and automated tracking.
