# Flowlu Help Center | Action: Create Project

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  [CRM Automation](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532--automation/ "CRM Automation")
    
5.  Action: Create Project

Action: Create Project
======================

* * *

1\. To create a project automatically, click the "Create" button and select "Project."

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/540/ef1c13c0-3990-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

2\. Enter a name for your automation. This should briefly outline your automated action. For example, "New Project". It will be displayed on the automation board.

3\. In the **Run** field, choose a time when specific action (sending notification) will occur:

*   **Immediately.** The automation rule is run immediately after the stage/status change.
*   **X minutes/days/hours after** the **Current time**, **End Date** or the **S****tart Date, Created** and **Modified,** as well as **Completed** and **Opened** dates for tasks. For example, the notification is to be created in 5 days after the current time.
*   **X minutes/days/hours befor****e** the **End Date** or the **S****tart Date**. Automation is run the specified number of minutes (hours, days) before the specified date. For example, the notification is to be created 1 day before the opportunity end date.
*   **Exact time** — select the **Start date** or the ****End date**, Created and Modified,** as well as ****Completed**** and ****Opened**** dates for tasks. The automation is run exactly at a specified time, for example, on the task deadline.

4\. Add the **condition** that must be met for your rule to continue running. For instance, the project should be created only if the opportunity amount is more than $5.000. To create a condition, select an opportunity field and custom values for the condition. [Learn more](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-544--conditions/)
. 

5\. **After previous automation rule**. You may set up the sequence of automation rules. This option empowers you to create a sequence of actions when your rule shouldn’t be run before the previous rule is applied.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/540/1237828e-3991-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

6\. Add a name and a description of your project. In Flowlu, you can automatically replace data with information provided in the opportunity/task, by which this project will be created. To do it, use variables that can be found by clicking on "three dots" button on the right. All main fields of the opportunity/task as well as custom fields can be used in the variables. For example, using the Opportunity _Name_ variable, you can create a project with the same name as your opportunity.   

7\. Select project priority and link it to the project workflow if needed.

8. Add an assignee. For example, use the "Opportunity: Assignee" variable to automatically place the required responsible person from each opportunity.

9\. Choose project team. 

10\. Select Project Financial Management mode.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/540/768854ea-3991-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/540/9e54a4da-3991-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

  

11\. Click **Save** to apply your changes.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/540/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Action: Send Email

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  [CRM Automation](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532--automation/ "CRM Automation")
    
5.  Action: Send Email

Action: Send Email
==================

* * *

**Please note** that you should have at least one synced mailbox to use this automatic action. The messages will be sent from this connected email.

1\. To send an email automatically, click the "Create" button and select "Email."  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/539/167a575b-3992-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

2\. Enter a name for your automation. This should briefly outline your automated action. For example, "Email to the Customer". It will be displayed on the automation board.

3\. In the **Run** field, choose a time when specific action (sending notification) will occur:

*   **Immediately.** The automation rule is run immediately after the stage/status change.
*   **X minutes/days/hours after** the **Current time**, **End Date** or the **S****tart Date, Created** and **Modified,** as well as **Completed** and **Opened** dates for tasks. For example, the notification is to be created in 5 days after the current time.
*   **X minutes/days/hours befor****e** the **End Date** or the **S****tart Date**. Automation is run the specified number of minutes (hours, days) before the specified date. For example, the notification is to be created 1 day before the opportunity end date.
*   **Exact time** — select the **Start date** or the ****End date**, Created and Modified,** as well as ****Completed**** and ****Opened**** dates for tasks. The automation is run exactly at a specified time, for example, on the task deadline.

4\. Add the **condition** that must be met for your rule to continue running. To create a condition, select an opportunity field and custom values for the condition. [Learn more](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-544--conditions/)
.

5\. **After previous automation rule**. You may set up the sequence of automation rules. This option empowers you to create a sequence of actions when your rule shouldn’t be run before the previous rule is applied.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/539/35edf4db-3992-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

6\. Select a sender if you have several email accounts available.

7\. Specify the recipient and add CC or BCC if needed. Use variable, to automatically place the email of a linked CRM contact.

8\. Enter the subject line and a description. In Flowlu, you can automatically replace data with information provided in the opportunity/task, by which this email will be created. To do it, use variables that can be found by clicking on "three dots" button on the right. All main fields of the opportunity/task as well as custom fields can be used in the variables. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/539/1ff7a177-3993-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

9\. Select **Save** to apply your changes.  

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/539/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Action: Field Update

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  [CRM Automation](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532--automation/ "CRM Automation")
    
5.  Action: Field Update

Action: Field Update
====================

* * *

1\. To change the opportunity/task automatically, click the "Create" button and select "Field Update."

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/541/8a17bff1-3993-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

2\. Enter a name for your automation. This should briefly outline your automated action. For example, "Changing the Assignee." It will be displayed on the automation board.

3\. In the **Run** field, choose a time when specific action (sending notification) will occur:

*   **Immediately.** The automation rule is run immediately after the stage/status change.
*   **X minutes/days/hours after** the **Current time**, **End Date** or the **S****tart Date, Created** and **Modified,** as well as **Completed** and **Opened** dates for tasks. For example, the notification is to be created in 5 days after the current time.
*   **X minutes/days/hours befor****e** the **End Date** or the **S****tart Date**. Automation is run the specified number of minutes (hours, days) before the specified date. For example, the notification is to be created 1 day before the opportunity end date.
*   **Exact time** — select the **Start date** or the ****End date**, Created and Modified,** as well as ****Completed**** and ****Opened**** dates for tasks. The automation is run exactly at a specified time, for example, on the task deadline.

4\. Add the condition that must be met for your rule to continue running. To create a condition, select an opportunity field and custom values for the condition. [Learn more](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-544--conditions/)
.

5\. **After previous automation rule**. You may set up the sequence of automation rules. This option empowers you to create a sequence of actions when your rule shouldn’t be run before the previous rule is applied.

6\. Choose the field that needs to be updated and define the opportunity value. For example, you can add an assignee or replace a previous one. Field details are mentioned in brackets. For example: Employee (add) or Employee (replace).   

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/541/5398e587-3994-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

7\. Select **Save** to apply your changes.

# Flowlu Help Center | Action: Activity Feed Post

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  [CRM Automation](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532--automation/ "CRM Automation")
    
5.  Action: Activity Feed Post

Action: Activity Feed Post
==========================

* * *

> The main difference between publishing a comment or a post to the activity feed is that you can edit or delete the comment, while the activity feed post cannot be modified. 

1\. To create an activity feed post automatically, click the "Create" button and select "Activity Feed Post."

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/556/bec93551-39a9-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

2\. Specify a name for your automation. This should briefly outline your automated action. For example, "New post to the activity feed". It will be displayed on the automation board.

3\. In the **Run** field, choose a time when specific action (sending notification) will occur:

*   **Immediately.** The automation rule is run immediately after the stage/status change.
*   **X minutes/days/hours after** the **Current time**, **End Date** or the **S****tart Date, Created** and **Modified,** as well as **Completed** and **Opened** dates for tasks. For example, the notification is to be created in 5 days after the current time.
*   **X minutes/days/hours befor****e** the **End Date** or the **S****tart Date**. Automation is run the specified number of minutes (hours, days) before the specified date. For example, the notification is to be created 1 day before the opportunity end date.
*   **Exact time** — select the **Start date** or the ****End date**, Created and Modified,** as well as ****Completed**** and ****Opened**** dates for tasks. The automation is run exactly at a specified time, for example, on the task deadline.

4\. Add the **condition** that must be met for your rule to continue running. For instance, the post should be published only if the project end date is tomorrow. To create a condition, select an opportunity/task field and custom values for the condition. [Learn more](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-544--conditions/)
.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/556/6d8ad0be-39aa-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

5\. **After previous automation rule**. You may set up the sequence of automation rules. This option empowers you to create a sequence of actions when your rule is not to be run before the previous rule is applied.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/556/97b16d3a-39aa-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

5\. Add a text of the post that will be displayed in the activity feed. In Flowlu, you can automatically replace data with information provided in the opportunity/task, by which this comment will be created. To do it, use variables that can be found by clicking on "three dots" button on the right. All main fields of the opportunity/task as well as custom fields can be used in the variables.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/556/ec919e81-39aa-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

6\. Add the post's author. For example, use the "Task: Assignee" variable to automatically substitute the assignee of each specific task. Or, you can leave it as Not set.  

7\. Select **Save** to apply your changes.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/556/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

[Previous Action: Comment](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-555--action-comment/)

[Next Action: Slack Message](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-599--action-message-in-slack/)

*   **X minutes/days/hours befor****e** the **End Date** or the **S****tart Date**. Automation is run the specified number of minutes (hours, days) before the specified date. For example, the notification is to be created 1 day before the opportunity end date.
*   **Exact time** — select the **Start date** or the ****End date**, Created and Modified,** as well as ****Completed**** and ****Opened**** dates for tasks. The automation is run exactly at a specified time, for example, on the task deadline.

4\. Add the **condition** that must be met for your rule to continue running. For instance, the comment should be created only if the opportunity end date is less than 5. # Flowlu Help Center | Setting Up Email Sync

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Email](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494--email/ "Email")
    
4.  Setting Up Email Sync

Setting Up Email Sync
=====================

* * *

By connecting your inbox to Flowlu you can send emails and receive replies without leaving your account.   

Please check that IMAP connection is enabled at your email account settings. For example, if you use Gmail, go to your Gmail account and click Settings → See all settings → Forwarding and POP/IMAP > IMAP access  → Enable IMAP. 

To set up an email integration, open the "Email" module in the Flowlu main menu. Please make sure the [Email app is installed at your account](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494--email/)
.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/512/0ce36143-b1c3-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Click **Connect an Account**. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/512/f9095833-b1c2-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

If you already have connected accounts, click the "'three dots" button in the right corner and select the appropriate option from the list.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/512/23eea67e-b1c3-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

> Before connecting Gmail or G-Suite account, please check [this article](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-512-522--how-to-connect-gmail/)
> . If your provider is not listed, please use this [guideline](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-512-513--imap-synchronization/)
> .  

The first thing you’ll need to do is to enter your login and password details. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/512/ae36b4bc-b1c3-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Select the time period for which you want to upload emails to the system: 1-3-7 days, 2 weeks, 1-2-3 months.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/512/c866fe5f-b1c3-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Change the name of your email account or the name of the sender, if necessary. Here you can also set the sync frequency depending on the current subscription plan: 1, 5, 15 or 30 minutes.  

> _**Please note:** Avoid using @ character in the Sender Name._ 

In the **CRM** section, check the **Use CRM** box if you want to use the features of this module when working with emails such as:  

*   Create opportunities for incoming emails from a new email address.
*   Create contacts for incoming emails from a new email address.

Indicate the default **source** of an opportunity and the **sales pipeline** for all opportunities.

Set **opportunity distribution queue** and determine employees that will work with new opportunities. New opportunities are distributed evenly and exactly as the users are enqueued.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/512/1f8eecbf-b1c4-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In Flowlu, you can also create new opportunities for each new incoming email. To do it, specify the necessary email addresses in the appropriate field. Each email address should be located on a new line.

If you want to track whether the recipient has opened your email or clicked on the links, select the appropriate options.

In Flowlu, you can specify who else accesses your emails and set permissions for certain employees. The access rights are set up individually for each account connected to Flowlu.

In the **Access Management** tab, select users or roles and set access rights individually for each employee/role:

*   read 
*   send 
*   edit and send

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/512/416a7e9c-b1c4-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Save.  

Well done! You have connected your email to Flowlu account. You can now [manage all customer messages in one single place](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-515--managing-email/)
. The number of email accounts you can set up will depend on your subscription plan.  

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/512/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Conditions

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  [CRM Automation](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532--automation/ "CRM Automation")
    
5.  Conditions

Conditions
==========

* * *

When creating a new automation rule, you can indicate certain conditions that must be met so your action will be performed. For example, you can notify a sales manager on an opportunity only if its value (amount) is greater than $50.000.

To create a new condition, select opportunity/task field. They include both main and custom fields.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/544/ebb34203-39ac-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Then, select the condition for your field.   

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/544/1bf1cae5-39ad-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Available conditions for opportunities include:  

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **Field** | **Conditions** |
| Name | is, is not, starts with, ends with, contains, does not contain, is unknown, has any value |
| Start date | Relative conditions: more than, less than, exactly  <br>Absolute conditions: after, on, before, is unknown, has any value |
| End date | Relative conditions: more than, less than, exactly  <br>Absolute conditions: after, on, before, is unknown, has any value |
| Amount (value) | is, is not, less, more, is unknown, has any value |
| Description | is, is not, starts with, ends with, contains, does not contain, is unknown, has any value |
| Assignee | is, is not, less, more, is unknown, has any value |
| Stage | is, is not, less, more, is unknown, has any value |
| Customer | is, is not, less, more, is unknown, has any value |
| Contact | is, is not, less, more, is unknown, has any value |
| Source | is, is not, less, more, is unknown, has any value |
| Pipeline | is, is not, less, more, is unknown, has any value |

### Available conditions for the tasks include:  

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **Field** | **Conditions** |
| Name | is, is not, starts with, ends with, contains, does not contain, is unknown, has any value |
| Start date | Relative conditions: more than, less than, exactly  <br>Absolute conditions: after, on, before, is unknown, has any value |
| Deadline | Relative conditions: more than, less than, exactly  <br>Absolute conditions: after, on, before, is unknown, has any value |
| Task report | is, is not, starts with, ends with, contains, does not contain, is unknown, has any value |
| Description | is, is not, starts with, ends with, contains, does not contain, is unknown, has any value |
| Task priority | is, is not, is unknown, has any value |
| Task status | is, is not, less, more, empty, not empty |
| Completion date | Relative conditions: more than, less than, exactly  <br>Absolute conditions: after, on, before, is unknown, has any value |
| First view date | Relative conditions: more than, less than, exactly  <br>Absolute conditions: after, on, before, is unknown, has any value |
| First closed date | Relative conditions: more than, less than, exactly  <br>Absolute conditions: after, on, before, is unknown, has any value |
| Owner's note | is, is not, starts with, ends with, contains, does not contain, is unknown, has any value |
| Owner | is, is not, is unknown, has any value |
| Assignee | is, is not, is unknown, has any value |
| CRM Account | is, is not, is unknown, has any value |
| Stage | is, is not, is unknown, has any value |
| Parent task | is, is not, is unknown, has any value |
| Project | is, is not, is unknown, has any value |
| Opportunity | is, is not, is unknown, has any value |

After you select a condition, you can enter custom values for the condition. You can add more than one condition to your rule.  

_Example:_ I want to schedule a meeting if the opportunity value is more than $5.000 AND sales pipeline is not Advertising AND source is Referral AND End date is after 03/05/2020.  

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/544/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | IMAP Synchronization

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Email](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494--email/ "Email")
    
4.  [Setting Up Email Sync](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-512--how-to-set-up-email-sync/ "Setting Up Email Sync")
    
5.  IMAP Synchronization

IMAP Synchronization
====================

* * *

If your provider is not listed in the available synchronizations in Flowlu, connect your inbox via IMAP. It is one of the most prevalent Internet standard protocols for email retrieval.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/513/d8baabeb-b1cf-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

You need the settings for incoming mail, which can be found on the email hosting website where you've created the email account.

You need the following parameters:

*   Server (host)
*   Port (SSL)
*   Your login (email address) and password 

For example, to set up ICloud email via IMAP, specify the following: server—imap.mail.me.com, port— 993, check the SSL box, login—[\[email protected\]](mailto:company@icloud.com)
 and password —12345.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/513/20cfa4b4-b1d2-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

You can also use custom mail settings for outgoing server.   

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/513/40d98a48-b1d2-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Once you have specified all necessary parameters, you can proceed to the account settings described in the [previous article](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-512--how-to-set-up-email-sync/)
.  

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/513/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

[Previous How to Connect Outlook](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-512-701--how-to-connect-outlook/)

# Action: Post to Messenger

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  [CRM Automation](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532--automation/ "CRM Automation")
    
5.  Action: Post to Messenger

Action: Post to Messenger
=========================

* * *

If you want to communicate with your clients through instant messengers, you can also automate lead processing and communication workflows for such needs.

In the CRM module, an automation rule is available that allows the system to automatically send messages on behalf of the company through connected communication channels.

To use this automation, you will also need:

*   The Communications module installed on your portal;
*   A configured integration with the corresponding messenger — e.g., **WhatsApp**.

For more information on how to set up the WhatsApp integration, click [here](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/676-677-688--how-to-connect-whatsapp/)
.

Set an Automation Rule
----------------------

On the Automation screen of the desired Pipeline, select the necessary stage and add an automation rule of the "Post to messenger" type.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1733/30330021-fd11-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

In the creation form that opens, specify the name, as well as the execution time and conditions.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1733/1b53b2ce-fdbd-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

Next, fill in the fields related to the intended message:

1.  Select the communication channel — from those available in the Communications module. After selecting the channel, specify one of the added bots.
2.  Specify the message recipient — you can choose:
    *   Opportunity participants within the system;
    *   CRM account contacts;
    *   Specific numbers directly — for example, those belonging to your own bots.
3.  Write the message text to be sent. If necessary, you can add variables from the corresponding menu.

**Important:** For correct delivery, numbers must be in the E.164 format: `+[Country Code][Subscriber Number]`.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1733/240c5417-fdbd-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

Then, save the automation rule.

When an Opportunity appears at the selected Pipeline stage, the specified message will be sent to the assigned recipients.

This allows you to send both reminders for your managers and welcome messages for your clients.

Important to Know
-----------------

1.  If there was no previous chat with the recipient, the system will create a new chat; if a chat already exists, the automation will send the message to the existing chat.
2.  If the recipient's card contains multiple phone numbers:
    *   In the absence of existing chats, the message will be sent to the mobile number.
    *   If a chat already exists, the message will be sent directly to that chat.
3.  If several recipients have the same phone number, the message within one automation rule will be sent only once.
4.  If the message is intended for a system user, it will be sent to their mobile number. If not available, it will be sent to the work number.

[Previous Action: Slack Message](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-599--action-message-in-slack/)

[Next Conditions](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-544--conditions/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Action: Slack Message

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  [CRM Automation](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532--automation/ "CRM Automation")
    
5.  Action: Slack Message

Action: Slack Message
=====================

* * *

**Please note** that you should have at least one workspace integrated from Slack to use this automatic action. You can find out how to do this [here](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/201-597--how-to-integrate-slack-with-flowlu/)
.

1\. To send a message to Slack automatically, click the "Create" button and select "Email."  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/599/27405461-39ab-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

2\. Enter a name for your automation. This should briefly outline your automated action. For example, "Message to the Customer". It will be displayed on the automation board.

3\. In the **Run** field, choose a time when specific action (sending notification) will occur:

*   **![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/599/6a064366-39ab-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)
    
    Immediately.** The automation rule is run immediately after the stage/status change.
*   **X minutes/days/hours after** the **Current time**, **End Date** or the **S****tart Date, Created** and **Modified,** as well as **Completed** and **Opened** dates for tasks. For example, the notification is to be created in 5 days after the current time.
*   **X minutes/days/hours befor****e** the **End Date** or the **S****tart Date**. Automation is run the specified number of minutes (hours, days) before the specified date. For example, the notification is to be created 1 day before the opportunity end date.

4\. Add the **condition** that must be met for your rule to continue running. This condition is set if the message needs to be sent depending on the content of the fields in the opportunity. To create a condition, select an opportunity field and custom values for the condition. [Learn more](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-544--conditions/)
.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/599/f233f56c-39ab-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

5\. **After previous automation rule**. You may set up the sequence of automation rules. This option empowers you to create a sequence of actions when your rule shouldn’t be run before the previous rule is applied.

6\. Select the workspace and channel/user to where/whom the message will be sent.

7\. Add the text of the message that will be sent to the specified workspace. With variables that are displayed when you click on the three dots on the right, you can automatically substitute data from the opportunity for which this message will be created. All the main and custom fields of the opportunity are displayed as variables.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/599/608655fc-39ac-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

8\. Select **Save** to apply your changes.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/599/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# CRM Report

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  [Opportunities](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-323--opportunities/ "Opportunities")
    
5.  CRM Report

CRM Report
==========

* * *

The CRM Report provides a comprehensive overview of your CRM pipeline, highlighting key metrics and insights into the progress of your deals. Let’s break down the key elements of this report

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/731/26b45d5d-990f-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

1.  **Active Opportunities Amount**: The total value of all active opportunities in your pipeline.
2.  **Won Opportunities**: The total value of deals that have been won.
3.  **Sales Conversion Rate**: The percentage of opportunities successfully converted into won deals.
4.  **Pipeline Visualization**: The pipeline visually represents the different stages of your sales process, showing the number of opportunities that were at each stage.
5.  **Breakdown**: A breakdown of each stage, showing the corresponding value of opportunities.
6.  **Loss Reason Rating**: This section displays the number of deals lost, offering insights into the reasons for these losses.
7.  **Won Opportunities and Activities**:  This section visualizes the activities associated with won opportunities.   
    Here, you can see the actions taken by each team member, sort by time, and draw conclusions. You can download the report in CSV, PNG, or SVG format to include in a presentation or share during a one-on-one meeting.

  

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/731/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

[Next CRM Automation](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532--automation/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Email

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  Email

Email
=====

* * *

With the Email integration, you can synchronize Flowlu with your email accounts and manage messages from your clients in a single place. Utilize email templates and instantly send emails to your clients without leaving your Flowlu account.

Automatically create opportunities and contacts when you receive emails from an unknown sender. Get notified if your clients open your email or click on any links. Easily access all conversations for a particular contact or an opportunity. Gain a full overview of everything that's happening with your customer at a glance.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/494/4d744d6a-a648-11ea-9c58-fa163e7d9ee1.png)

### How to Install Email App

Go to the **Apps** section of the main menu.

Click **Email** app in the list of applications. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/494/ee8c969c-b1c1-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Select the **Install** option. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/494/d1a76186-b1c1-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

 That's it! The application is now installed in your account. Now you can proceed to [setting up email sync](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-512--how-to-set-up-email-sync/)
.

> The Email feature is available at Team, Business, Professional, and Enterprise plans.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/494/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Knowledge Base

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  Knowledge Base

Knowledge Base
==============

* * *

Flowlu Knowledge Base is an easy-to-use tool that allows your team to quickly and easily record knowledge, ideas, experience, guidelines, etc. The information entered into the knowledge base will save time for training new hires and help to quickly find the answer to the frequently asked question at any time.  

To keep Flowlu knowledge base up-to-date is very easy. It takes just a few clicks to post a new article, delete an old one, update or add new information.

The knowledge base can be not only internal and accessible to certain users of your portal, but also external, accessible to any person by a direct link. 

In this chapter, we will talk about the settings, features and how to populate the knowledge base.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/342/2d83487a-56bf-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

_Knowledge base main page_

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/342/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

  

*   [How to Create a Knowledge Base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/342-343--settings-and-creation-the-knowledge-base/)
    
*   [Populating Your Knowledge Base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/342-345--populating-a-knowledge-base/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Getting Started

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  Getting Started

Getting Started
===============

* * *

Flowlu is a software for online business management, which includes tools for effective task,  project, time, customer and financial management.

In this section, you will learn how to get started with Flowlu, explore the main settings and get the answer to the frequently asked questions. We recommend starting to learn how to use Flowlu from this very section.

Please feel free to contact us via our online support chat on your account page if you have any questions or difficulties. Or, you can always reach out to us at [\[email protected\]](mailto:support@flowlu.com)
. 

Every time, after updating the system, we publish a list of the recent improvements in the [What’s new](https://roadmap.flowlu.com/flowlu/updates)
 section on our website and your account.  

You can go to the "Help" section from your account by clicking on the icon with the question mark. Here you can find out all the necessary guidelines to improve the efficiency of your work in Flowlu. Good luck!

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/201/2ede2579-0692-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/201/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

  

*   [Signing Up](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/201-238--signup/)

# Flowlu Help Center | How to Use Custom Domain For Knowledge Base

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Knowledge Base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/342--knowledge-base/ "Knowledge Base")
    
4.  How to Use Custom Domain For Knowledge Base

How to Use Custom Domain For Knowledge Base
===========================================

* * *

### Step #1

1.  Sign in to your domain control panel.
2.  Look for the option to change DNS records. The option might be called something like DNS Management, Name Server Management, or Advanced Settings.  
    
3.  Locate the CNAME records for your domain. A CNAME record, or Canonical Name record, is a type of alias used by the Domain Name System (DNS). Among other things, a CNAME record can be used to make a domain an alias of an external website.  
    
4.  Point the CNAME record from your domain (shown in the **Host Record** field) to **hc.flowlu.com**

### Step #2

Open the Knowledge Base settings, go to **External** page and check **Change the domain name**. Enter your custom domain name. Domain name should be without any https://

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/228/24a45184-5e96-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/228/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

[Previous External Knowledge Base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/342-417--how-to-place-a-knowledge-base-on-your-website/)

[Next Access Rights: Knowledge Base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/342-344--access-rights-settings/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Opportunities

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  Opportunities

Opportunities
=============

* * *

Opportunities are designed for managing your potential deals. Each request of your potential customer, whether it's an email, a completed web form on the website or an incoming call — all of them are opportunities. During the selling process, the opportunity goes through the stages of the pipeline. Opportunity records track details about deals, including the value of potential sales, client's interaction history, estimates and invoices, etc.

Once the opportunity is won, you need to issue an invoice or create a project. The lost opportunities are closed with an indication of the loss reason. According to the lost/won opportunity performance, a report is generated as a pipeline, which visually allows you to identify bottlenecks in your sales process.

To manage your opportunities, go to the **CRM** **→ Opportunities**.

List or Kanban View
-------------------

You can choose a convenient way to display opportunities — a list or a Kanban board. To select the display type, click the corresponding tab:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/323/4ee07515-4faa-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the list, opportunities are displayed as a table. It stores the information about the opportunity stages, the budget, the company/contact name, person responsible for the opportunity, as well as the last comment on the opportunity.  

In the list, you can filter, search and sort any data:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/323/20368ce2-777f-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the list, you can also add extra columns. To do that, click the engine button and select columns you want to add. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/323/03b140da-7780-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

With the Kanban board, you can assess the current state of your business at a glance. Employees can drag opportunities from stage to stage as they negotiate with the client.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/323/abdd26c5-4faa-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Each stage shows the amount that these opportunities will bring if they are won. If you move the opportunity, the amount is immediately recalculated.

Each opportunity includes scheduled events (appointment, call, email) and depending on their date, the color indicators are displayed on the board. They prompt the managers which opportunities need their attention first.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/323/1bad4692-4fac-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Activities 
-----------

The CRM module also includes the Activities section. It show all planned and completed activities (both tasks and events) related to your clients and opportunities. With this section, you can easily determine the overdue activities, plans for today and tomorrow. This sections let you apply filters and sorting by the opportunity, CRM account, assignee and owner. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/323/5a4b7857-4fac-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

  

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/323/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

*   [Managing Opportunities](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-323-328--working-quickly-with-the-opportunities/)
    
*   [Loss Reasons and Sources](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-323-325--setting-up-loss-reasons-and-sources-of-opportunities/)
    
*   [How to Create Opportunities Automatically](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-323-329--automate-lead-creation/)
    
*   [Required Fields for Sales Pipelines](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-323-1754--required-fields-for-sales-pipelines/)
    
*   [Import CRM Opportunities](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-323-459--opportunities-import/)
    
*   [Link Products to Opportunities](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-323-655--how-to-link-products-to-opportunities/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Access Rights: Knowledge Base

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")

4.  Access Rights: Knowledge Base

Access Rights: Knowledge Base
=============================

* * *

You can set access rights for each of your knowledge bases. Access rights are assigned in two stages — at the level of the KB application and at the level of a certain knowledge base.

Stage 1. At the Application Level
---------------------------------

Go to the **Marketplace** → **Knowledge Base**→ **User Access Settings**:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/344/515704f8-5e97-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Here, you can assign a specific role at the application level to each user.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/344/72010f6d-5e97-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

*   The KB app administrator has unlimited access rights to all knowledge bases.
*   Employees have access only to those knowledge bases, that the administrator will give them access to. Employees also cannot create a new knowledge base.

Access rights to the knowledge base can also be assigned in the user's profile. To do this, go to the **My Team** section or click the icon of user you want to change access for.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/344/f2103cd1-5e97-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Stage 2. At the Knowledge Base Level
------------------------------------

Access rights for a specific section of the knowledge base are assigned in its settings. There are two ways to go to settings.

**Method #1.** The settings button is located on the main knowledge base page in the upper right corner:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/344/1f60a0a8-5e98-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

**Method #2.** In the **Knowledge Base** app **(1)** → **Knowledge Bases** **(2)**:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/344/4364d13c-5e98-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Now, you can add the users and assign access rights to them. To add the user, click on the "Add User" button:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/344/673c7cf3-5e98-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Select one or several users at once, to whom you want to give access.  After adding a user, select a role from the drop–down list.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/344/90d91313-5e98-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Access Rights

**The administrator** has unlimited access to the knowledge base provided to him. He can add the users, set access rights, open external access to the database, rename, or delete it.

**The moderator** can work with all the content of the knowledge base: add, publish, edit and delete articles.

**The author** can add, publish, edit and delete only his own articles. Changing the articles of other authors is not available to him. The author can see all the articles: both published and unpublished.

**The user** can read and comment on only published articles. He cannot add his own articles.

> Please note that an employee with a 'User' access level will see the knowledge base in the menu only after there will be published at least one article.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/344/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# How to Integrate Flowlu with Confluence

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Knowledge Base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/342--knowledge-base/ "Knowledge Base")
    
4.  How to Integrate Flowlu with Confluence

How to Integrate Flowlu with Confluence
=======================================

* * *

If you have a Confluence knowledge base, you can easily integrate it with Flowlu in a few steps. To integrate Flowlu with Confluence, we’ve added a specific module.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/624/4dcb96fb-567d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Finding the Atlassian API Token

First, you need to get an Atlassian API token. Log in to your Atlassian account and click the icon of your profile on the right top, then go to **Account Settings**.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/624/8dfc86ab-567d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Go to the Security page and find the **Create and manage API tokens** link. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/624/64a24a62-567e-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Click the **Create API token** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/624/85f5f628-567e-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Name the token and click the **Create** button. After the token is created, click the **Copy** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/624/a684a4a2-567e-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### How to Set Up Confluence Integration in Flowlu

Go to your Flowlu account **Home Page.** On the right top, click the **Add** button → **Knowledge Base → Import from Confluence**. Find the **Import from Confluence** module on the dashboard and tap the **Import** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/624/cf7770dd-567e-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Step 1

After you click the Import button, the sidebar will be opened. 

On step 1, fill in your Confluence knowledge base domain, email address linked to Atlassian and the API-token you’ve copied before. After you’ve filled in all the fields, click the **Next** button. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/624/3ad4696a-567f-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Step 2

On step 2, select the knowledge base from Confluence and choose the Flowlu knowledge base you want to import the base in. Map fields from Confluence to Flowlu and click the **Next** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/624/53165869-567f-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Step 3

On step 3, click the **Import** button. It will take a few seconds to upload. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/624/5e4280dc-567f-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

After it’s uploaded, click the **Go to the knowledge base** link.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/624/69d25e82-567f-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Done! Confluence knowledge base is in Flowlu.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/624/73a263cd-567f-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/624/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

[Next Knowledge Base Automation](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/342-668--knowledge-base-automation/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Organizations and Contacts

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  Organizations and Contacts

Organizations and Contacts
==========================

* * *

All contacts and companies that your company interacts with is displayed in the **CRM** app, on the **Accounts** tab. In their records, the entire history of work is displayed: past and upcoming events, implemented projects, invoices and estimates, the payments received, etc.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/332/b6b74502-512b-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

The companies are displayed under the **Organizations** tab:  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/332/e326a8c3-512b-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

When you click on the organization name, you will see the entire history of interaction with this organization. For example, linked contacts, planned events, current tasks and projects, opportunities, estimates, invoices, payments, etc. On the organization record, you can see all the activities, including archived ones.   

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/332/16fcc2ad-512c-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

**Contacts** — individual people, who can be either your individual clients or contacts in any company. For contacts, as well as for companies, you can issue invoices, run projects or opportunities, assign tasks to your employees, and plan appointments.

The **contacts** are stored in the Contacts section:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/332/5476e970-512c-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

When you click on the contact name, you can see all contact details, scheduled events, current tasks, opportunities, invoices, etc.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/332/68203103-512c-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Between organizations and contacts, you can create multiple links or relations. For example, one company may have several representatives—contacts. And vice versa, one contact can represent several companies and work in different positions at each company. Information about this will be displayed on the contact page.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/332/a05b316a-512c-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

_A contact represents two companies_

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/332/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Task Calendar View

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Tasks](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118--tasks/ "Tasks")
    
4.  Task Calendar View

Task Calendar View
==================

* * *

Calendar is one of the available task views. In this view, tasks are arranged according to their specified date and time.

Calendar helps you build your work schedule for the day, week, or month. Unlike the To-do List, the Calendar considers the specific times set for tasks and how they fit together.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1626/a6091c78-833a-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

> **Important:** The Calendar in the Tasks module is only available on paid plans.

Calendar Structure
------------------

The screen shares the same core elements as the main Calendar module, but it displays tasks instead of events.

On the left side of the screen, there is a mini-calendar for the current month, which you can use to switch dates quickly.

The tasks themselves are placed on the main calendar grid according to their specified dates and times.

> **Important:** Tasks without specified deadlines are not displayed in the Calendar.

Calendar Views
--------------

Using the switcher at the top of the screen, you can toggle between **Month**, **Week**, or **Day** views. Each view is suited for planning and tracking tasks over different time horizons.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1626/e362c056-833a-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

**Month View:**

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1626/c2ba9d40-8340-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

**Week View:**

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1626/c71f507e-8340-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

**Day View:**

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1626/ca9417b5-8340-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

### Working with Tasks in the Calendar

**Creating Tasks**

You can create a new task by clicking on the desired date and time slot on the calendar grid.

When you do this, the task's **Start Date** and **Due Date** fields will be pre-filled.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1626/a480cd6b-8345-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.gif)

**Changing Task Deadlines**

In the Calendar, you can also quickly and easily change the due dates of existing tasks without opening the edit screen. To do this, simply drag and drop the task to a new date and time on the calendar grid.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1626/a8254d31-8345-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.gif)

Calendar Display Settings
-------------------------

By default, the Calendar only shows active tasks—those with a global status of "To-Do" or "In Progress."

If needed, you can also choose to display:

*   Completed tasks
*   Subtasks

To do this, open the display settings by clicking the **gear icon** in the top right corner of the screen.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1626/a7433d1a-8346-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Check the corresponding boxes **(1)**. Here, you can also customize which fields are displayed on the task cards on the calendar grid **(2)**.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1626/ab1f9072-8346-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

  

> **_If you have any issues or concerns, please contact our customer support via in-app chat or email us at_ [](https://www.flowlu.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#f88b8d8888978a8cb89e94978f948dd69b9795)
> [\[email protected\]](mailto:support@flowlu.com)
>   
> **

# Migrating to Flowlu

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Getting Started](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/201--getting-started/ "Getting Started")
    
4.  Migrating to Flowlu

Migrating to Flowlu
===================

* * *

This section of our Help Center is dedicated to guiding you through the process of migrating your essential business data from other platforms into your new Flowlu workspace.

Our step-by-step guides are designed to make this transition as smooth and predictable as possible.

To keep things organized, our migration guides all follow the same four-stage structure:

*   **Preparation**: Before you move anything, you need to set up your Flowlu workspace. This means adding your teammates and creating custom fields to match the data you'll be importing. Good preparation is key to a clean import.
*   **Export**: Pull your data from your old platform. The standard way to do this is by exporting it into CSV files.
*   **Import**: Once you have your files, you'll import them into the right modules in Flowlu (like CRM or Tasks) and carefully map your data to the fields you prepared.
*   **Post-Migration**: After the import, you'll need to verify that everything transferred correctly and rebuild any automations or integrations you were using.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1670/d19b46ba-fb48-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

  

*   [How to Migrate Your Data from Monday.com to Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/201-1670-1623--how-to-migrate-your-data-from-monday-com-to-flowlu/)
    
*   [How to Migrate Your Data from HubSpot to Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/201-1670-1669--how-to-migrate-your-data-from-hubspot-to-flowlu/)

# Flowlu Help Center | How to Import Tasks from Trello to Flowlu

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338--agile-projects/ "Agile Projects")
    
4.  [Integrations with Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1639--integrations-with-agile-projects/ "Integrations with Agile Projects")
    
5.  How to Import Tasks from Trello to Flowlu

How to Import Tasks from Trello to Flowlu
=========================================

* * *

Flowlu integration with Trello allows you to import issues from Trello to a specific Agile project in Flowlu.

How to Import Tasks from Trello
-------------------------------

You can access this integration in two ways:

### Method 1 

To quickly export tasks from Trello to Flowlu, you can use our premade integration widget.

Go to the **Home** page and click the **Add** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/681/5e74f0fd-5166-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

Click **Agile Projects** → **Import Issues.**

**![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/681/e9a2c6a2-5166-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)**

After that, you'll see the import widget. Click the **I****mport** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/681/1be9f98b-516a-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

Click **Trello.**

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/681/a78e7cc8-516a-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Method 2

Go to the Agile module and open the project you want to import tasks for. Click the _three-dot button_ on the top right corner → **Import**.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/681/795e5de0-520e-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

Select **Trello**.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/681/3c52c6a7-5210-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

> Note that if you start importing directly into the project, you'll skip the step of selecting the host project to import tasks. The system will automatically pick the Agile project you set the integration from as the host one.  

How to Set the Integration: Step-by-step Guide
----------------------------------------------

1\. Log in to your Trello account.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/681/5337d7bd-516b-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

2\. In the opened window, agree to the following terms & conditions and fill out your Trello login information or change the account. Once the connection is set, you'll receive an email notification with details from Trello.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/681/ab6b5133-5201-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

3\. On the Step 2, select one of your Agile projects to import tasks to.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/681/ed64734a-5207-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

4\. On the Step 3, select one of your Trello boards to import tasks from it.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/681/6b8d6f91-5208-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

5\. On the Step 4, map task statuses from Trello to Flowlu to set the status of imported tasks in Flowlu. For example, you can export tasks from all columns in Trello to the To Do column in the selected Agile project in Flowlu.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/681/a215e9ff-5209-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

10\. On the Step 5, you can map users from Trello toFlowlu users. Once the task is imported to Flowlu, it'll be assigned to a specific user. Or you can simply leave them without an assignee and link them later from the task ticket. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/681/2996ad4c-520a-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

11\. On the final step, you'll see the total number of issues to import. After checking the information, click the **Import** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/681/9319bc48-520a-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

12\. Once the import is started, you'll see the uploading screen. If you have lots of tasks and files to import, it can take a few minutes. 

> Note that imported tasks natively lie in the backlog. You can add them to sprints later.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/681/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | User Access Permissions

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Team Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1473--team-management/ "Team Management")
    
4.  User Access Permissions

User Access Permissions
=======================

* * *

For every Flowlu application, you can set certain access rights for each teammate.  

The invited team members get limited access to all installed Flowlu applications by default. They see only those tasks, opportunities, projects and other features which they take part in. You can change access to your applications on your own — set administrator role for your teammate or deny the access.

There are several ways of how you can configure user access rights.

### Method #1. In Employee’s Profile

Select **My Team** module and go to **Members** list**:**

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/272/8bd3a9b2-7f18-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

Click the name of a user that you want to set access rights for to open their profile

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/272/43762cc1-7f19-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

Click the 3-dot button and select **Edit Profile:**

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/272/49aa3063-7f19-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

To set access rights, go to **Access rights** tab.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/272/d9d8b98d-7f29-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

In this tab, all installed applications will be displayed. You can set up access rights for each employee depending on their role in the company. When you choose a permission, the settings will be automatically saved.

You can go quickly to employee profile settings out of any section where his username is clickable. For example, from the feed, tasks, projects pages and so on.  

### Method #2. Using Portal Settings

Go to **Portal Settings**.

The main settings page will be opened. In the left menu you will see applications with various tabs to set up.

Choose a Module, click the **Main Settings** and go to the **Users Access Settings**. There you can define permissions for every employee. When you choose a role, settings are saved automatically.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/272/ee7217fc-7f29-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

### Method #3. Using the Apps list

Click your profile picture in the top right corner and select **Apps** in the drop-down menu:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/272/5b9e47d3-7f23-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

Select a module that you want to configure user access for, e.g. **Projects**:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/272/f0fca157-7f23-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

Then go to User Access Settings tab to configure user access to this module:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/272/0d5064d8-7f24-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

Using this page you can grant full or limited access or deny any access to a module for all existing users on your account. If a user does not have access to a module, it will not be displayed in a teammate’s left menu, nor will they be able to go to a module via a direct link.

You can learn more about user permission settings in this [Help Center article](https://support1.aspro.cloud/_module/knowledgebase/view/article/492--user-roles/)
.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/272/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | How to Invite a new User

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Team Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1473--team-management/ "Team Management")
    
4.  How to Invite a new User

How to Invite a new User
========================

* * *

This guide will walk you through the different ways to add new users to your Flowlu account, allowing you to build your team and collaborate effectively.

You can begin adding users from two primary locations in your account:

*   From the Top Menu: Click the “+” icon in the top navigation bar and select “New User” from the dropdown menu  
    ![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/271/42e99685-4ce1-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)
*   From the My Team Module: Navigate to the “My Team” module from the left sidebar. Click the “Add” button in the upper right corner and select “User” from the dropdown.![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/271/88831962-4ce2-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)  
    

Once you start, you can choose from several methods to add or invite new members to your team.

Full Invite
-----------

The Full Invite method allows you to directly create a new user's profile, set their password, and fully configure their access rights from the start.

1.  Select the “Full Invite” tab.
2.  Fill in the user’s details, including their First Name, Last Name, and a required Email. You can also add their Phone and Position.
3.  Set a password for the user. You can either type a password yourself or click “Generate password” to create a secure one automatically.
4.  Expand the "Access rights" section to define the user's permissions for various modules and features within Flowlu.
5.  Once all details and permissions are set, click “Save”. The user account will be created and ready for them to use.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/271/fc899e65-4ce3-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Quick Invite
------------

For a faster process, the Quick Invite method lets you send invitations by simply entering your colleagues' email addresses.

1.  Select the “Quick Invite” tab.
2.  Enter the email address of a coworker in the provided field. To invite more people, click "More" to add more email fields.
3.  Click “Send” to send the email invitations.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/271/4af4a77e-4ce4-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Bulk Invite
-----------

If you need to invite many users at once, the Bulk Invite feature is the most efficient method.

1.  Select the “Bulk Invite” tab.
2.  In the text box, enter the email addresses of the users you wish to invite. You can separate the emails with a comma, a space, or a line break.
3.  Click “Send”. Invitations will be dispatched to all the listed email addresses simultaneously.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/271/9fb28b68-57ef-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Invite by Link
--------------

You can also invite new members by sharing a unique, automatically generated invitation link.

1.  Select the “Invite by Link” tab.
2.  Click the copy icon to copy the unique link to your clipboard.
3.  Share this link with your coworkers through any communication channel you prefer, such as email or a team chat.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/271/bf3dbaa9-4ce7-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

After following the link, new users can quickly sign up and join your portal. If a user already has a Flowlu account for another company, they will be able to join your portal immediately and then easily switch between accounts.

If you need to invalidate the link (for instance, if it was shared with the wrong person), simply click the “Reset” button. This will generate a new unique link, and the old one will no longer work.

How to Manage Invitations
-------------------------

After you send email invitations, you can track and manage them.

1.  Navigate to the “My Team” module.
2.  Click on the “Invitations” tab. This screen displays a list of all pending invitations that have been sent by email.

From here, you can take two actions for any invitation that has not yet been accepted:

*   Resend: If a user didn't receive the invitation, you can send it again.
*   Cancel: If an invitation was sent in error, you can cancel it. This will make the link in the original email inactive.

Once a teammate accepts an invitation and signs up (or once you create their account using Full Invite), they will appear in the main user list in the My Team module.

# Flowlu Help Center | How to Delete a User

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Team Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1473--team-management/ "Team Management")
    
4.  How to Delete a User

How to Delete a User
====================

* * *

You can delete employees or just disable authorization to your portal. It's convenient if you often use freelancers as contractors or you need to grant temporary access to colleagues who you're working with on a project. _See also: [Client Portal](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/586--client-portal/)
._

Method #1. User Deletion  

---------------------------

Go to **My Team**. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/274/830f33b9-6731-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Check the box next to the employee’s name, then click **Delete from account**. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/274/4f1836e5-6732-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

> **Please note:** When you delete an employee account, all connected information (tasks, projects, contacts, invoices) is saved to Flowlu. But a user will not able to enter your portal using their account. If you decide to add them again, information and notes will be restored.

### Method #2. Disable Authorization

Go to the **User Management** and uncheck the **Allow Authorization** box. After that, an employee will not be able to enter the Flowlu portal. All notes left by an employee will be saved. 

This option is better than deletion in a case when you want to see the task assignee, project manager, etc. but do not want this person to log into. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/274/5311f69d-6732-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/274/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

[Previous How to Archive Users](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1473-682--archive-users/)

# How to Archive Users

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Team Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1473--team-management/ "Team Management")
    
4.  How to Archive Users

How to Archive Users
====================

* * *

Sometimes it happens that a person is no longer in your team and you want to delete them from your account and take away the access to company's portal. To not lose all important data related to your former employee, you can archive its account without losing any data. It will help restrict permission to the portal, so you can reassign tasks, projects, and other entities previously related to this user. 

How to Archive Users?
---------------------

Go to the list of users in the My Team module and find the user you need to archive. Fill in the checkmark next to this user. If you need to bulk archive multiple users, mark all of them.

![My Team Module location](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/682/722d1c45-8ab3-11ee-9772-fa163e2ff576.png)

Then click the recycle bin icon on the bottom bar.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/682/deae7a73-8ab5-11ee-9772-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the opened window, click the **Archive** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/682/f2c8e113-8abe-11ee-9772-fa163e2ff576.png)

Done! The user is archived. 

Where to Find Archived Users
----------------------------

All archived users are arranged under the **Archive** tab in the **My Team** module.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/682/ee478707-8ac3-11ee-9772-fa163e2ff576.png)

You can unarchive the user by clicking the **Unarchive** button. All the entities related to the user will be saved, and access to the account will be restored as well.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/682/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)
