# Flowlu Help Center | Import CRM Opportunities

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Account Settings](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270--account-settings/ "Account Settings")
    
4.  [Data Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270-1472--data-management/ "Data Management")
    
5.  [Import your Data to Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270-1472-426--data-import/ "Import your Data to Flowlu")
    
6.  Import CRM Opportunities

Import CRM Opportunities
========================

* * *

To import opportunities, go to the **CRM** > **Opportunities** page and select the import option.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/459/a96579b9-5128-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Step #1. Import Settings

*   upload a file with the data;

*   select the file encoding (we recommend setting the Auto-Detect option);

*   select the field separator for your file (the most frequently used is a comma);

*   if the first line in your file contains field names (titles), check the option;

*   set a default owner (assignee) for your contacts, if required;

*   for a proper search for a responsible person specify the name format (optionally).  
    

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/459/10145274-5129-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Step #2. Field Matching

As soon as the file is uploaded, an active **Next** button will appear at the bottom to the right of the form, click on it and go to the field matching settings.

If your file has been correctly recognized, then you will see blocks arranged horizontally, each of which corresponds to a column in your file.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/459/2839d713-5129-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

If you see a different picture, then the file was not recognized correctly—check the selection of the field separator on step 1. If you cannot solve the problem, please contact our support service by email: [\[email protected\]](mailto:support@flowlu.com)
 or via the support chat.

Next, select the Flowlu field in which the field data of the imported file will be written, if you want to skip the field, then select the skip option.

After setting the field matching, click Next.

### Step #3. Duplicate Control Settings

If some opportunities already exist in Flowlu, the system will find them (if it’s possible) and process them according to the selected option:

*   Allow (All data will be imported without duplicates),

*   Replace (All fields will be replaced with the imported data),

*   Merge (All empty fields will be replaced with the imported data),

*   Skip (Duplicate records will be skipped).

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/459/3c300a01-5129-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Select the relevant option and start the import with the **Next** button. After a successful import, you will see a confirmation page and you can proceed to work with opportunities.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/459/5a562a21-5129-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/459/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Link Products to Opportunities

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  [Opportunities](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-323--opportunities/ "Opportunities")
    
5.  Link Products to Opportunities

Link Products to Opportunities
==============================

* * *

In Flowlu, you can link your products and services to calculate the total amount from the product prices, quickly create invoices and set up the type of estimation.

How to Enable Products in Opportunities
---------------------------------------

To enable products in opportunities, go to the **Portal Settings **→ CRM → Main Settings**** and fill in the **"****Apply Products to Opportunities**" checkbox.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/655/d791c614-c2ff-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Once the checkbox is filled, you'll see two additional options: 

*   **Apply the automated calculation of the opportunity’s amount** — total opportunity amount will be calculated automatically from the products’ prices.
*   **Block manual total price setup** -  total amount will be estimated only based on the product's price and can't be changed manually.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/655/ed21e47d-c304-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Click **Save** to apply changes.

How to add Products to Opportunities
------------------------------------

While creating a new opportunity, go to the **Products** tab. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/655/6268f843-c309-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Here you can add products from the list you created via the **Products module**. You can manually set up price, QTY, taxes and discounts. Also, the same as in invoices, you can add a discount, taxes, shipping charges and adjustments. If you don't need this additional information, just leave the checkmarks empty. 

To add more items, click **Add Another Line**.

If you want to add products to already created opportunity, open this opportunity and click the three-dot button ****→**** **Edit**. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/655/800232ad-c30d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the opened window, go to the **Products** tab. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/655/b650bcbb-c30d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Here you can add products or change already added ones.

You can also edit the list of products for each opportunity without going to the editor's mode. Simply open the **Products** tab inside the opportunity and click the **Edit** button. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/655/0955d7ca-c314-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

The list of products is also visible on the main opportunity page. If the total amount calculates from product prices, the budget field will show the total amount.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/655/8d888156-c321-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

How to Create Invoices for Opportunities with Products
------------------------------------------------------

Once you've linked products to the opportunity, you can create invoices and the list of products will be automatically added to the invoice. 

> Note, that you should change the total amount estimation type to "**Use product prices**". To check if this setting is enabled, go to the **Edit** mode of the opportunity and check the slider next to the **Opportunity amount** field.

To create an invoice, click the **Create → Create Invoice** button inside the opportunity.

You'll see the regular invoice creation form with the list of linked products. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/655/d26520ce-c320-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

  

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/655/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | How to Create Contacts and Organizations

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  [Organizations and Contacts](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-332--companies-and-contacts/ "Organizations and Contacts")
    
5.  How to Create Contacts and Organizations

How to Create Contacts and Organizations
========================================

* * *

In Flowlu, there are several ways to create a CRM account.

Method #1. Through "+" in the Upper Menu
----------------------------------------

To add a new account, click the "+" button in the top menu and select the necessary button:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/334/968e7466-512d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

When creating a new company, you can specify:  

*   name;
*   assignee, [category and industry](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-332-333--classification-of-companies-and-contacts/)
    ;
*   contact details;
*   create or link it to the existing contacts;
*   add tags and notes;
*   attach files;
*   enter full legal name (to issue invoices), address, banking details;
*   fill in custom fields. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/334/b4f01d9d-512d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

When creating a new contact in the opened sidebar, you can specify:

*   title, first and last name;
*   assignee, [category and industry](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-332-333--classification-of-companies-and-contacts/)
    ;
*   email, phone number, website;
*   one or more organizations in which the contact operates. You can create a new one or link it to an existing organization;
*   additional and contact data;
*   add tags and notes;
*   custom fields. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/334/c958461f-512d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Method #2. In Accounts  

-------------------------

You can create new record in the top right corner:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/334/1385d2cb-512e-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

The creation of a company or contact in these sections is performed the same way as described above. You just need to select the account type:  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/334/3e9de31b-512e-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Method #3. In Any System Object With the Customer Field  

----------------------------------------------------------

You can add a new account on the opportunity page, task, project, invoice, transaction via the **Create** button:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/334/44c35810-512f-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

_Creating a customer card when issuing the invoice_  

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/334/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | How to Segment Organizations and Contacts

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  [Organizations and Contacts](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-332--companies-and-contacts/ "Organizations and Contacts")
    
5.  How to Segment Organizations and Contacts

How to Segment Organizations and Contacts
=========================================

* * *

You can classify all CRM accounts by category and industry. For example, you can divide accounts into suppliers, partners, customers, and advertising agencies, or trading companies.

Setting Up Categories and Industries
------------------------------------

To do the settings, go to **Portal Settings → CRM → Reference** and select the **Categories** tab or the **Industry** tab.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/333/e6e9c101-512f-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

By default, the system already has categories and industries created.  

You can delete, edit, deactivate and add your own categories and industries. To edit or delete, move the cursor over the name of category or industry.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/333/2ec7ba0a-5130-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

To deactivate, uncheck the box in **Active** column. After that, the category or industry will not be displayed in the list for selection.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/333/54d2837f-5130-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

To add a new category or industry, enter the title and press the **Enter** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/333/c3a79e0e-5130-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Each category and industry, has the number of accounts that have been assigned to a given category or industry.

The category and industry are selected when creating or editing an account record.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/333/0cf93a7b-5131-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/333/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Import CRM Contacts

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Account Settings](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270--account-settings/ "Account Settings")
    
4.  [Data Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270-1472--data-management/ "Data Management")
    
5.  [Import your Data to Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270-1472-426--data-import/ "Import your Data to Flowlu")
    
6.  Import CRM Contacts

Import CRM Contacts
===================

* * *

To import contacts, go to **CRM** **→** **Contacts** page and select the import option.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/429/a121016d-e546-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

### Step #1. Import Settings

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/429/a7896fa8-e546-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

*   Upload a file with the contacts;
*   Select the file encoding (we recommend setting the Auto-Detect option);
*   Select the field separator for your file (the most frequently used is a comma);
*   If the first line in your file contains field names (titles), check this option;
*   Set a default owner (assignee) for your contacts, if required;
*   For a proper search for a responsible specify the name format (optional).

### Step #2. Field Matching

As soon as the file is uploaded, an active **Next** button will appear at the bottom right of the form. Click on it and go to the field matching settings.

If your file has been correctly recognized by the system, then you will see blocks arranged horizontally, each of which corresponds to a column in your file.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/429/ae4bbc06-e546-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

**Note:** If you see a different picture, then the file was not recognized correctly - check the selection of the field separator at the Step 1. If you cannot solve the problem, please contact our support service by email: [\[email protected\]](mailto:support@flowlu.com)
 or via the support chat.

Next, select the Flowlu field in which the field data of the imported file will be written. If you want to skip the field, then leave the skip option active.

Please note that if you upload contacts with companies, you should specify the Company field for each contact, where the company name should be indicated, and you can also add the main company fields. The Position field belongs to the company fields array, because your contact may be in different companies with different positions.

After setting the field matching, click **Next**.

### Step #3. Duplicate Control Settings

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/429/b76e1796-e546-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

If some companies or contacts already exist in Flowlu, the system will find them (if possible) and process them according to the selected option:

*   Allow (All data will be imported without duplicates);
*   Replace (All fields will be replaced with imported data);
*   Merge (All empty fields will be replaced with imported data);
*   Skip (Duplicate records will be skipped).

Select the required option and start the import with the **Next** button.

After a successful import, you will see a confirmation page and you can proceed to work with contacts.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/429/cd635584-e546-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

Importing Contacts Linked to Companies
--------------------------------------

If you are uploading contacts along with companies, these two entities can be linked in several ways:

*   Upload contacts and link them to the required companies manually;
*   Map contact fields with company fields during step #2.

Each row of the table corresponds to one contact, a row can be linked to only one company.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/429/c4b82186-e546-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

The system searches for existing companies based on fields with the following priority levels:

1.  Business Tax ID;
2.  VAT #2;
3.  Other ID;
4.  Full Legal Name;
5.  Phone;
6.  Email.

**Important:** Any match is sufficient to link a company. Priority levels apply only if a single contact contains data belonging to different companies.

**Example:** If two counterparties are found in the database - one with a matching Business Tax ID and another with a matching Email, the contact will be linked to the company with the Business Tax ID match.

To link a contact to several companies, simply duplicate the contact in the source file and specify fields for different companies in each row. In this case, the contact in the system will be linked to all specified companies.

|     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Legal Name | Industry | Assignee | Website |
| Company #1 | IT  | J.K. Taylor | site.com |
| Company #2 | IT  | J.K. Taylor | site.com |

If a company is not found based on the provided details, the system will create it automatically and link it to the contact.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/429/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# ﻿Notification

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Email](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494--email/ "Email")
    
4.  [Email Automations](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-690--email-automations/ "Email Automations")
    
5.  ﻿Notification

﻿Notification
=============

* * *

The notification automation rule automatically creates a new notification for your teammates. You can send notifications to certain teammates or to a group of users based on their role.

![](https://support1.aspro.cloud/files/download/ee9618f6-c99a-11ee-b6f1-fa163e2ff576)

In the automation rule settings window, you can set up the following fields:

1\. **Name**

In this field, you can specify a name of the notification. It'll be visible in the list of email automations only.

2\. **Event**

Here you can set for which action with the email message the automation will be working. Currently, there are three types of triggers:

*   Create - the automation will be working once the mail is created.
*   Update - the automation will be working once the mail is updated.
*   Delete - the automation will be working once the mail is deleted.

3\. **Run**.

It's an optional setting. By default, the automation is proceeding right after meeting the _Event_ rule. Defines when the automation is going to work. Currently, there are two options: **Immediately**, or **X minutes/hours/days after current time**.

4\. **Conditions.**

Defines a condition for an automation rule, so it'll run only when meeting the condition. Conditions are similar to the **"If**" statement.

You can set the following condition for email automations:

*   Email subject: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Email type: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Email content: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Folder: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   From: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   To: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.

For example: If the _email folder is INBOX,_ send a notification to _sales manager_.  

If the option _"After previous automation rule"_ is enabled, the automation will run only when the previous automation is completed.

5\. **Notification.**

Text field for the content of your notification. By clicking the three-dot button next to the text window, you can ad variables from the email module.

6\. **Recipients**.

User who'll receive your notification.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/691/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

[Previous Email Automations](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-690--email-automations/)

# Request a Response

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Email](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494--email/ "Email")
    
4.  [Email Automations](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-690--email-automations/ "Email Automations")
    
5.  Request a Response

Request a Response
==================

* * *

This automation allows you to request a response to a certain email from your teammate.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/692/316aaea1-c9a2-11ee-b6f1-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the automation rule settings window, you can set up the following fields:

1\. **Name**

In this field, you can specify a name of the action. It'll be visible in the list of email automations only.

2\. **Event**

Here you can set for which action with the email message the automation will be working. Currently, there are three types of triggers:

*   Create - the automation will be working once the mail is created.
*   Update - the automation will be working once the mail is updated.

3\. **Run**.

It's an optional setting. By default, the automation is proceeding right after meeting the _Event_ rule. Defines when the automation is going to work. Currently, there are two options: **Immediately**, or **X minutes/hours/days after current time**.

4\. **Conditions.**

Defines a condition for an automation rule, so it'll run only when meeting the condition. Conditions are similar to the **"If**" statement.

You can set the following condition for email automations:

*   Email subject: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Email type: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Email content: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Folder: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   From: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   To: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.

For example: If the _email folder is INBOX,_ request a response from the _user named Ann_ _Watson_.  

If the option _"After previous automation rule"_ is enabled, the automation will run only when the previous automation is completed.

5\. **Request a Response.**

Text field for the content of your message. By clicking the three-dot button next to the text window, you can add variables from the email module.

6\. **Sender**.

User who's sending the message.

7\. **Recipients**.

Users who'll receive your notification.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/692/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Required Fields for CRM Accounts

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  [Organizations and Contacts](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-332--companies-and-contacts/ "Organizations and Contacts")
    
5.  Required Fields for CRM Accounts

Required Fields for CRM Accounts
================================

* * *

To maintain a clean and high-quality database, it is essential to ensure that your team captures all critical information when adding new clients. Flowlu allows you to make specific fields mandatory, preventing the creation of incomplete CRM records.

How to Access Required Fields Settings
--------------------------------------

Only users with Administrator access to the CRM module can configure these settings. To find them:

1. Navigate to the CRM module.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1816/664606ce-268e-11f1-9021-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

2. Click the Settings (gear icon) in the top-right corner.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1816/01e264cb-2698-11f1-9021-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

3. Go to the Required fields tab.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1816/6b8da9aa-268e-11f1-9021-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

Inside this tab, you will find sub-sections for Organizations, Contacts, and All Accounts.

Configuring Required Fields
---------------------------

You can set requirements for both standard system fields and your own custom fields.

### System Fields

Under the Organizations or Contacts tabs, you will see a list of standard system fields such as Email, Phone, Industry, or Last Name.

To make a field mandatory, simply check the box in the Required field column.

### Custom Fields

If you have created unique fields to track specific data, you can make them mandatory as well.

*   Navigate to the All Accounts tab within the Required Fields menu.
*   Locate the specific custom field and check the Required field box.

**![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1816/44057657-2698-11f1-9021-3a787cc0d110.jpg)**

**Note:** If a field is set as required under "All Accounts," it must be filled out for both Organization and Contact records.  

When a user attempts to create a new record (Organization or Contact), all fields marked as required will be highlighted. The system will block the user from saving the record until these specific fields are populated with data. This ensures that your sales and marketing teams always have the essential data needed to follow up with leads effectively.

[Previous Import CRM Organizations](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-332-430--companies-import/)

# How to Migrate Your Data from Monday.com to Flowlu

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Getting Started](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/201--getting-started/ "Getting Started")
    
4.  [Migrating to Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/201-1670--migrating-to-flowlu/ "Migrating to Flowlu")
    
5.  How to Migrate Your Data from Monday.com to Flowlu

How to Migrate Your Data from Monday.com to Flowlu
==================================================

* * *

This guide provides a detailed, step-by-step process for successfully moving your projects, tasks, and CRM data from Monday.com to Flowlu.

Since there is no direct migration tool between these two services, the process involves exporting your data from Monday.com and then importing it into Flowlu using CSV files. For highly complex migrations, you can also consider using our [API](https://www.flowlu.com/api/)
 for a custom solution.

Stage 1: Prepare for Migration
------------------------------

Thorough preparation is the key to a smooth transition. Before you move any data, complete these essential setup steps.

### Analyze and clean your Monday.com account.

**Identify Critical Data:** Review your Monday.com boards, dashboards, and workspaces. Decide what information is essential to your current operations and what can be archived. This is a perfect opportunity to clean up old projects and irrelevant data.

**Handle Complex Columns:** Monday.com columns like "Formula," "Mirror," or "Link to Item" cannot be directly imported into Flowlu. Before exporting, you must convert the data in these columns into simple text or number formats.

### Set Up Your Flowlu Workspace

**Configure Your Modules:** Before importing, ensure all the modules you need are active. You will be mapping your Monday.com boards to specific Flowlu modules like [Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/126--projects/)
 for project management, the [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/)
 for customer data and sales pipelines, and [Tasks](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118--tasks/)
 for your to-do lists. To get a better understanding of the platform's capabilities, we also recommend booking a free [Flowlu product demo](https://calendly.com/flowluteam/flowlu-demo)
 with our team.

**Add Your Users:** Invite all your team members to your Flowlu account. To assign tasks and other records to the correct people during the import, their user accounts must already exist in the system.

**[Create Custom Fields](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/303-304--how-to-add-a-custom-field/)
 (Crucial Step):** This is the most important preparatory step. Identify all the custom columns you use in Monday.com. You must create corresponding custom fields in Flowlu **before** you begin importing. Go to **Portal Settings** → **Custom Fields** to add new fields for projects, tasks, opportunities, and more.

**Important:** The standard CSV import process currently supports creating and populating custom fields of the following types only: _Text Field, Text Area, Whole Number, Price, Date, Date and Time, User, and Yes/No_.

If your Monday.com boards use other field types (like dropdowns, status lists, or linked items), you have two main options:

*   During preparation, convert the data from these complex columns into a simple text format and import it into a "Text Field" or "Text Area" in Flowlu to preserve the information.
*   Use the [Flowlu API](https://www.flowlu.com/api/)
     for a more advanced migration that can handle creating and populating complex field types like lists and catalogs.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1623/a71ac3e2-fb45-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

Stage 2: Export Data from Monday.com
------------------------------------

The primary method for extracting your data is by exporting your boards to Excel, which can then be easily saved as a CSV file.

### To Export an Individual Board:

1.  Open the board you wish to export.
2.  Click the three-dot menu icon in the top-right corner.
3.  Select **"More actions"** and then **"Export board to Excel."**
4.  **Pro Tip:** If you export a board with sub-items, Monday.com often places them in a separate sheet. You may need to import these as a separate batch of tasks and link them to their parent tasks in Flowlu after the main import.

### To Export the Entire Account (Admin Only):

1.  As an account administrator, navigate to the **Administration** section.
2.  Under the **General** tab, find the **Account Data** section.
3.  Click **"Export"** to request a full export of your account's data.
4.  You will receive an email with a download link for a .zip archive. **This link is only valid for 24 hours.**

Stage 3: Prepare and Import Data into Flowlu
--------------------------------------------

Once you have your exported Excel files, you must prepare them for a clean import. For detailed instructions on each data type, please see our guides on how to [Import your Data to Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270-1472-426--data-import/)
.

### Prepare Your CSV Files

**Separate Data by Type:** Flowlu imports different record types separately. You will need to create individual CSV files for each data type you want to migrate, such as:

*   CRM Contacts & Organizations
*   CRM Opportunities (Sales Deals)
*   Tasks
*   Products & Services
*   Financial Records (Payments/Expenses)

**Clean and Format:** Open each Excel file and save it as a CSV (UTF-8 encoding is recommended). The first row of your file must contain the column headers (e.g., "Task Name," "Assignee," "Due Date"). Ensure the data is consistent.

### The Import Process in Flowlu

1.  Navigate to the appropriate section in Flowlu (e.g., **Tasks**, or **CRM -> Contacts**).
2.  Find the three-dot menu or settings icon and select the **"[Import](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270-1472-426--data-import/)
    "** option. Note: The "Import" module must be installed from the Marketplace.
3.  Upload your prepared CSV file.
4.  **Map Your Fields:** This is the most critical part of the import. Carefully match the columns from your CSV file to the corresponding fields in Flowlu, including the custom fields you created earlier.
5.  **Run a Test Import:** For large datasets, we highly recommend importing a small sample file (10-20 rows) first to verify that all fields are mapped correctly.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1623/aeca135c-fb45-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

Stage 4: Post-Migration Checklist
---------------------------------

After your data has been imported, complete these final steps to ensure your team is ready to work in Flowlu.

**Verify Your Data:** Perform a spot-check on several projects and contacts. Confirm that data like assignees, dates, and custom field information appears as expected. Your tasks will be visible in various views, including a Kanban board similar to Monday.com's layout.

**Recreate Automations and Integrations:** Monday.com's "Automations" and "Integrations" will not be migrated. You must manually rebuild them in Flowlu.

*   For workflow automation (e.g., "when status changes, notify someone"), use Flowlu's built-in [Automation Rules](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118-532--automation/)
     for CRM, Tasks, and Projects. You can set triggers and actions like creating tasks, sending emails, or posting notifications.
*   For connecting third-party apps, browse our [list of integrations](https://www.flowlu.com/integrations/)
     or use a service like Make.com or Zapier for wider connectivity.

**Onboard Your Team:** Conduct a training session to introduce your team to the Flowlu interface. Our [YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@Flowlu/)
 has helpful video tutorials that are a great resource for visual learners.

By following these structured steps, you can execute a successful and organized migration from Monday.com to Flowlu.

Need More Help?
---------------

If the migration process seems complex or you'd like professional assistance, our team offers a dedicated [implementation service](https://www.flowlu.com/implementation/)
 to manage data transfers, setup, and team training for you. For any other questions, please visit our comprehensive [Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)
.

[Previous Migrating to Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/201-1670--migrating-to-flowlu/)

[Next How to Migrate Your Data from HubSpot to Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/201-1670-1669--how-to-migrate-your-data-from-hubspot-to-flowlu/)

# Comment

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Email](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494--email/ "Email")
    
4.  [Email Automations](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-690--email-automations/ "Email Automations")
    
5.  Comment

Comment
=======

* * *

This automation allows you to automatically comment on each email that was created or updated.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/693/37193e12-c9a5-11ee-b6f1-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the automation rule settings window, you can set up the following fields:

1\. **Name**

In this field, you can specify a name of the comment. It'll be visible in the list of email automations only.

2\. **Event**

Here you can set for which action with the email message the automation will be working. Currently, there are three types of triggers:

*   Create - the automation will be working once the mail is created.
*   Update - the automation will be working once the mail is updated.

3\. **Run**.

It's an optional setting. By default, the automation is proceeding right after meeting the _Event_ rule. Defines when the automation is going to work. Currently, there are two options: **Immediately**, or **X minutes/hours/days after current time**.

4\. **Conditions.**

Defines a condition for an automation rule, so it'll run only when meeting the condition. Conditions are similar to the **"If**" statement.

You can set the following conditions for email automations:

*   Email subject: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Email type: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Email content: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Folder: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   From: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   To: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.

For example: If the _email folder is INBOX,_ leave a "send a report" comment.  

If the option _"After previous automation rule"_ is enabled, the automation will run only when the previous automation is completed.

5\. **Notification.**

Text field for the content of your comment. By clicking the three-dot button next to the text window, you can add variables from the email module.

6\. **Author**.

User who'll be linked as the comment's author.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/693/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Customizing the Task Page Layout

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Tasks](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118--tasks/ "Tasks")
    
4.  Customizing the Task Page Layout

Customizing the Task Page Layout
================================

* * *

You can change the appearance of a task page to suit your needs—remove unnecessary fields, add new ones, or rearrange their order.

To do this, open the context menu in the upper right corner of the task (1) and select **"Configure Layout"** (2).

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1620/7d72741b-7e7f-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

The page will switch to editing mode, and additional interface elements will appear.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1620/88680300-7e7f-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

To exit editing mode, click the large button at the top of the page.

Setting Up Access to the Page Type
----------------------------------

First, decide for whom you are editing the page:

*   for yourself;
    
*   for all users;
    
*   for specific users or a user role.
    

> **Important:** Only portal administrators can edit the common page layout and the layouts for other users.

Choose the editing scope from the selector—by default, you can change:

*   **Default account view**  — for all users in the system;
    
*   **My page view**  — the settings will only apply to you.
    

You can also create a separate page view and specify the users for whom it will be available. To do this, select **"New page view"** from the selector.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1620/b202b400-7e7f-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Then, in the editing window:

*   specify the name of the page type;
    
*   select the **user role** or specific users (3) who will have access to it.
    

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1620/f923ac98-7e7f-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

After saving the changes, the new page type will appear in the list.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1620/01fdfc24-7e80-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

You can change the access settings for the page via the context menu in the upper right corner. There you can:

*   set the page type for all users on the portal;
    
*   change the list of users and/or **user roles** through the settings.
    

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1620/105ef75a-7e80-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

There you can also:

*   reset the page settings — return it to the default values;
    
*   delete the created page type.
    

Editing the Page
----------------

### Working with Fields

To the right of each field and block, there is an interaction button. Clicking it will reveal the option to hide the field.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1620/9f6ee2e3-7e80-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.gif)

By holding down the mouse button on the move area, you can change the order of fields using drag-and-drop.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1620/a4bf50a2-7e80-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.gif)

To add new fields, click the **"Add field"** button at the bottom of a block and select the desired field from the list.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1620/a86b30e0-7e80-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.gif)

### Working with Blocks

The task page editor is best suited for working with custom fields. Fields can be grouped into different blocks—based on their meaning and/or purpose.

To create a block, scroll to the very bottom of the page and click the **"Create block"** button. By clicking on the title, you can rename the block.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1620/b0f9cd6c-7e80-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.gif)

To delete a block, click on the button to the left of its name and delete it.

# Create Webhook

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Email](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494--email/ "Email")
    
4.  [Email Automations](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-690--email-automations/ "Email Automations")
    
5.  Create Webhook

Create Webhook
==============

* * *

This automation allows to create a webhook to pass the information from Flowlu to another app.

> Note that Flowlu currently supports outgoing webhooks only.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/694/473a6007-c9a6-11ee-b6f1-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the automation rule settings window, you can set up the following fields:

1\. **Name**

In this field, you can specify a name of the webhook. It'll be visible in the list of email automations only.

2\. **Event**

Here you can set for which action with the email message the automation will be working. Currently, there are three types of triggers:

*   Create - the automation will be working once the mail is created.
*   Update - the automation will be working once the mail is updated.
*   Delete - the automation will be working once the mail is deleted.

3\. **Run**.

It's an optional setting. By default, the automation is proceeding right after meeting the _Event_ rule. Defines when the automation is going to work. Currently, there are two options: **Immediately**, or **X minutes/hours/days after current time**.

4\. **Conditions.**

Defines a condition for an automation rule, so it'll run only when meeting the condition. Conditions are similar to the **"If**" statement.

You can set the following conditions for email automations:

*   Email subject: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Email type: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Email content: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Folder: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   From: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   To: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.

If the option _"After previous automation rule"_ is enabled, the automation will run only when the previous automation is completed.

5\. **Webhook.**

Place the webhook URL and action code in the following fields: By clicking the three-dot button, you can add variables from the email module.

# Tasks Widgets

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Tasks](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118--tasks/ "Tasks")
    
4.  Tasks Widgets

Tasks Widgets
=============

* * *

In Flowlu, you can customize your homepage by displaying key information from the Tasks module as interactive widgets. These widgets serve as a powerful real-time dashboard, allowing you to monitor team workload, track progress and other statistics you need without manually navigating to the Tasks module or applying repetitive filters. By bringing your most critical data to the forefront, widgets help you identify bottlenecks, such as overdue tasks, and prioritize your day more effectively.

Each widget is interactive; clicking on a metric will immediately open a side panel with a filtered list of the corresponding tasks, allowing for seamless transitions between high-level overviews and detailed task management.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1714/3baac0dd-f5f4-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

In the following articles, we will take a closer look at how to add widgets, configure them, and manage them.

  

*   [Tasks Widget: Doughnut Chart](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118-1714-1715--tasks-widget-doughnut-chart/)
    
*   [Task Widgets: Column Chart](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118-1714-1716--task-widgets-column-chart/)
    

[Previous Task Calendar View](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118-1626--the-calendar-view/)

# How to Integrate Flowlu With UseResponse

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338--agile-projects/ "Agile Projects")
    
4.  [Integrations with Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1639--integrations-with-agile-projects/ "Integrations with Agile Projects")
    
5.  How to Integrate Flowlu With UseResponse

How to Integrate Flowlu With UseResponse
========================================

* * *

You can integrate the Agile module with UseResponse, a multi-channel customer support software, to collect customers’ feedback in Flowlu.  

How to Install the Module
-------------------------

Click the icon on the top right corner and go to the Apps tab.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/0c1f682f-fae0-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Click the **UseResponse** module.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/24a7cb04-fae0-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

To add this module, click the **Install** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/43d569a1-fae0-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

API-token
---------

To set up the integration, you need to get a unique API token. To get the token, login to your UseResponse account and go to your portal.  
Hover over your icon in the top right corner and click the API Settings.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/5dda5bbb-fae0-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the opened window, you can see the API settings. Copy the information from the **URL** and **Key** fields.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/9ba33652-fae0-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Ensure that the API token is enabled by clicking the **Enabled** button.

Done! Once the token is enabled, go to your Flowlu account.

Step #1. Setting the Integration
--------------------------------

After the module is installed, you can see the list of UseResponse integrations. Click the **Connect Integration** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/c28d7802-fae1-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the opened window, you can see the integration settings. Add the URL you copied from UseResons to the Host field. API-token needs to be added to the API field.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/5cb9ff01-fae2-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Switch the slider to **active**.

Click the **Next** button.

On the next step, you need to match the UseResponse fields with Agile projects.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/9f65b48f-fae3-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Once all fields are filled, the integration is configured on Flowlu’s side. The system will create a custom link to which UseRespons will send requests. This link is required during UseResponse configuration, so copy it to your clipboard.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/2e086653-fae4-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Step #2. Setting the Trigger
----------------------------

To make the integration work, you need to create a UseResponse webhook.  
Go to your UseResponse portal and open the advanced settings via the agent interface.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/ba034df8-fae3-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Go to the **Administration** tab.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/c4d75515-fae3-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Go to the **Automation & Notifications** and click the **New Trigger** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/c8934aec-fae3-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

A webhook trigger is an action that sends a request to Flowlu's Agile module. 

To create a trigger, you need to fill in the following information: 

*   Name;
*   Select the "Object is Created" condition in the Event field;
*   If needed, set additional conditions via the **Add Condition** field;
*   In the **Action** field set the following rule:

**_HTTP Request_** _—_ **_POST_** _—_ **_#Flowlu link from the step #1_**.

*   Once all conditions are set, click the **Submit** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/55d4f101-fae8-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Integration is ready to use!

How does it look in practice?
-----------------------------

A user leaves feedback on your UseResponse portal (bugfix request or feature request). 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/2d23033d-faf2-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Once the ticket is created, it automatically creates an issue in your Flowlu's Agile project, which you linked to UseResponse.  
You can also track the connection with the UseResponse request in the issue's ticket, see the number of votes for the request, and quickly go to the issue's source in UseResponse by clicking the link.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/565d1ceb-faf2-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/664/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Manage File Storage

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Account Settings](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270--account-settings/ "Account Settings")
    
4.  [Data Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270-1472--data-management/ "Data Management")
    
5.  Manage File Storage

Manage File Storage
===================

* * *

To manage the file storage and see the files it incorporates:

Go to Portal Settings → File Storage.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/606/31c2f63b-803c-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the File Storage page, you can view the allowed storage space, total used space and all the system objects it includes such as images, documents, attachments, etc.  

In the list, you can sort files by size or created / changed date. You can also filter records by a user, creation or modification date, file format. 

Images and PDF files can be previewed. 

You can also see the file relation to the system objects. For example, you can see which task or project contains this file. 

You can easily delete files in bulk or one by one from this page.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/606/5ca379db-803c-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

The space depends on your subscription plan. You can find out more about it [here](https://www.flowlu.com/pricing/)
.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/606/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Date & Time Settings

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Account Settings](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270--account-settings/ "Account Settings")
    
4.  [Main Settings](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270-1470--main-settings/ "Main Settings")
    
5.  Date & Time Settings

Date & Time Settings
====================

* * *

In Flowlu, you can set up your date settings for the whole portal: time zone, date format and regular days off for your company.

You can also create custom holidays, which will be displayed in [**Time Off Management**](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1473-1627--time-off-management/)
.

You can find the **Date & Time Settings** in the **Portal Settings → Main Settings.**

How to Set Up the Default Time Zone
-----------------------------------

To set up your current time zone, choose the one from the drop down menu. This time zone will be displayed as the default, but each user can set up its own personal time zone later in the portal settings.   

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/635/c55ec851-a850-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

External users can set up the time zone in the **Account Settings**.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/635/e1a2edbb-a850-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

How to Set Up the Date Format
-----------------------------

In the **Date Format** section, you can set when the week starts, the date format and the time format. These settings are common for all modules.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/635/09a05963-a851-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

How to Set Up the Days-Off of Your Company
------------------------------------------

By default, Saturdays and Sundays are displayed as regular days off in Flowlu. Anyway, you can pick any days as regular days off, and they will be colored gray in the calendar while creating a task, project, etc..

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/635/3cf7201e-a851-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

How to Create Custom Holidays
-----------------------------

To create a custom holiday, which will be displayed in **[Time Off Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1473-1627--time-off-management/)
**, go to the **Custom Holidays** tab.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/635/6e3fe45e-a851-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Click the **Create** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/635/742c3e68-a851-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

While creating the custom holiday, you can specify the name, description, and start and end dates. After filling in all fields, press **Save**. Custom holidays will be taken into account while creating an absence for employees.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/635/81c23e11-a851-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Public Holidays
---------------

Public holidays are linked to the country of your Flowlu portal. They'll be marked with a red color in the calendar when creating any activity inside Flowlu. For example, while creating a project, public holidays are marked in red, as are your custom holidays.

You can edit each holiday, set start and end dates, make it visible or hide it, and change the name or description. You can change settings only for the current year or in general.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/635/c41b1cfc-c496-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/635/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Import CRM Organizations

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Account Settings](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270--account-settings/ "Account Settings")
    
4.  [Data Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270-1472--data-management/ "Data Management")
    
5.  [Import your Data to Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270-1472-426--data-import/ "Import your Data to Flowlu")
    
6.  Import CRM Organizations

Import CRM Organizations
========================

* * *

To import companies, go to **CRM** > **Organizations** page and select the import option.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/430/b9a333b8-5139-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Step #1. Import Settings

*   upload a file with the data;
*   select the file encoding (we recommend to set the Auto-Detect option);
*   select the column separator for your file (the most frequently used is a comma);
*   if the first line in your file contains field names (titles), check the option;
*   set a default owner (assignee) for your contacts, if required;
*   for a proper search for a responsible person, specify the name format (optional).

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/430/bf1aa11b-5139-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Step #2. Field Matching

As soon as the file is uploaded, an active **Next** button will appear at the bottom to the right of the form, click on it and go to the field matching settings.

If your file has been correctly recognized, then you will see blocks arranged horizontally, each of which corresponds to a column in your file.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/430/f893ef6a-5139-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

> **If you see a different picture, then the file was not recognized correctly—check the selection of the field separator on the step 1. If you cannot solve the problem, please contact our support service by email: [\[email protected\]](mailto:support@flowlu.com)
>  or via the support chat.  
> **

Next, select the Flowlu field in which the field data of the imported file will be written, if you want to skip the field, then select the skip option.

After setting the field matching, click **Next**.

### Step #3. Duplicate Control Settings

If some companies already exist in Flowlu, the system will find them (if it’s possible) and process them according to the selected option:

*   Allow (All data will be imported without duplicates);
*   Replace (All fields will be replaced with imported data);
*   Merge (All empty fields will be replaced with imported data);
*   Skip (Duplicate records will be skipped).

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/430/09b477cc-513a-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Select the required option and start the import with the **Next** button.

After a successful import, you will see a confirmation page and you can proceed to work with contacts.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/430/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Create Task

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Email](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494--email/ "Email")
    
4.  [Email Automations](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-690--email-automations/ "Email Automations")
    
5.  Create Task

Create Task
===========

* * *

This automation rule allows you to automatically create tasks and predefine their settings.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/695/03851a10-ca34-11ee-98ce-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the automation rule settings window, you can set up the following fields:

1\. **Name**

In this field, you can specify a name of the automation. It'll be visible in the list of email automations only.

2\. **Event**

Here you can set for which action with the email message the automation will be working. Currently, there are three types of triggers:

*   Create - the automation will be working once the mail is created.
*   Update - the automation will be working once the mail is updated.
*   Delete - the automation will be working once the mail is deleted.

3\. **Run**.

It's an optional setting. By default, the automation is proceeding right after meeting the _Event_ rule. Defines when the automation is going to work. Currently, there are two options: **Immediately**, or **X minutes/hours/days after current time**.

4\. **Conditions.**

Defines a condition for an automation rule, so it'll run only when meeting the condition. Conditions are similar to the **"If**" statement.

You can set the following conditions for email automations:

*   Email subject: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Email type: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Email content: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   Folder: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   From: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.
*   To: is/is not/in unknown/has any value.

If the option _"After previous automation rule"_ is enabled, the automation will run only when the previous automation is completed.

5\. **Task Settings**

In this area, you can set the name of the task, its description, and choose if the automation will be completed only when the task is finished.

6. **Start and End Dates**

Here, you can optionally set the start and end dates of the task. If the task needs to be completed in a certain timeline, for example, in two hours, you can also send its **estimated time**.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/695/cd822a63-ca35-11ee-98ce-fa163e2ff576.png)

7. **User Roles**

In this section, you can set an assignee of the task, the owner, collaborators, and folloers (optional). Each role, except of the owner, can consist of multiple users.

8\. **Workflow**

Pick a workflow to which the task will be related. If the task can't be approved without the review of the owner, you can choose the "Task needs approval from the owner" option. 

9\. **Custom fields**

If you have any custom fields related to the task module, you can set these fields for automatically created tasks.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/695/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

[Previous Create Webhook](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-690-694--email-webhook/)

[Next Create Event](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-690-696--email-create-event/)

# Unlinking a Project from a Project Workflow

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/126--projects/ "Projects")
    
4.  [Project Workflows](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/126-199--how-to-create-project-workflow/ "Project Workflows")
    
5.  Unlinking a Project from a Project Workflow

Unlinking a Project from a Project Workflow
===========================================

* * *

Project workflows are designed to streamline and accelerate the creation of new projects. This is especially valuable for companies that frequently manage standardized, repeatable projects.

But what if a specific project needs to deviate from the standard process? For example, it might require a different number of stages or milestones. The work structure of a project linked to a workflow cannot be modified, and building a new project from the ground up is inefficient.

The solution is to unlink the project from its project workflow.

Effects of Unlinking a Project from a Workflow
----------------------------------------------

When you unlink a project from its workflow, the following changes take place:

*   **The work structure becomes editable** within the unlinked project. These changes will not impact other projects created using the same workflow.
*   **Automations are removed** from the unlinked project, as they are a component of the original workflow.
*   The unlinked project will no longer be visible on the **Project Workflow board**.
*   The original workflow can be edited, and those changes will not be applied to the unlinked project.

How to Unlink a Project from a Workflow
---------------------------------------

> **Important:** This action can only be performed by Project module administrators, the project manager, or the assistant project manager.

Follow these steps to unlink your project:

1.  Navigate to the project that you created from a workflow. To confirm if a project is linked to a workflow, enter the project's edit mode—the workflow's name will be displayed in the "Project Workflow" field.  
    ![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1625/2b84193b-8336-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)
2.  From the project's main page, click the **three-dot menu** in the upper-right corner and select the **"Unlink from project workflow"** option.  
    ![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1625/8674426d-8336-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)
3.  A confirmation prompt will appear. Confirm the action to proceed.  
    ![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1625/b0df4fb1-8336-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

That's it. The project's work structure is now fully editable.

  

> **_If you have any issues or concerns, please contact our customer support via in-app chat or email us at_ [](https://www.flowlu.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#daa9afaaaab5a8ae9abcb6b5adb6aff4b9b5b7)
> [\[email protected\]](mailto:support@flowlu.com)
>   
> **

[Previous Project Workflows](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/126-199--how-to-create-project-workflow/)

[Next Project Financial Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/126-391--managing-the-project-finances/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Project Financial Management

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/126--projects/ "Projects")
    
4.  Project Financial Management

Project Financial Management
============================

* * *

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/391/78f9e5fe-d908-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

  
If you plan to track financial data for projects, invoice, calculate costs, track budget, then go to **Portal Settings**→ **Projects** → **Main Settings** and check the corresponding option.

If you do not plan to manage finances in projects throughout the company, then disable this option. In this case, all financial information in the projects will be hidden.

Also here you can choose whether to **display finances on the project's main page** and whether to **i****nclude unallocated transactions** in the amount of planned revenues.

You can also enable the **o****ption for managers to create revenues and expenses for the particular project**, even if this employee doesn't have access to a bank account for this project. To learn more about how to set up access rights for bank account, [explore this article](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-285--access-rights-to-the-finance-module/#:~:text=Access%20Rights%20to%20Money%20Transactions%C2%A0)
.

When creating a new project, you can **change the default settings of the time tracker**: hourly rate, billing by estimated or spent time, method of sorting data in the invoices (by tasks or by time log), invoice item format using variables (e.g. add a note to the time record).  

Using these settings, you can invoice customers for the time spent on project work. Please note that you can only issue an invoice for completed tasks.

Finance Section
---------------

Project **Finance** tab consists of three main sections: Money, Invoices & Estimates, Cash Flow Forecast. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/391/8a13321f-0343-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

**Money** section includes all project revenue and expenses — both estimated and actual.

Revenues are the entire incoming cash flow from the client provided for the services performed by your company. Revenues from projects can be taken into account by creating actual revenues or through invoicing.

Expenses are the outgoing cash flow of your company. Here you can account for all costs within the project.

In the Money section, you will see:

*   how much has been estimated to be paid/spent;
*   how much has been already paid/spent;
*   the balance (how much is still unpaid).

You can sort your expenses and revenue by months, financial categories, CRM accounts, project stages.   

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/391/e9cf87c9-0344-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

The **Invoices & Estimates** section comprises all generated estimates and invoices linked to this project. The list is customizable, so you can display only those data you need. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/391/2031cd29-0345-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the **Cash Flow Forecast** section, you can see planned and actual cash balances at any date. The planned balance is shown by a dotted line, and the actual balance by a continuous line. The graph clearly shows what happens to cash as the project progresses. 

Who can view project financial information? 
--------------------------------------------

The project manager keeps track of revenue and expenses. This data is available only to the project manager and to the administrators of the Projects and Finance modules.  
  
If necessary, you can hide project finances from managers who have access to all project data. If you hide access to the Finance module, the project manager will not be able to issue invoices, and will not see the budget, payments, and costs for all projects. To do this, go to System Settings → Finance → Main Settings → User Access Settings.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/391/84f75fbc-02a5-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/391/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Filters

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Working in Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1474--working-in-flowlu/ "Working in Flowlu")
    
4.  Filters

Filters
=======

* * *

Filters are amazing for quickly finding information you want based on specific criteria. In Flowlu, you can pre-define sets of filters. For example, you can create filters to view customers from a particular industry, or find projects of a certain project manager, etc.  

Every list (table) has a filter panel at the top:  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/461/d4d5ecb3-673c-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

The panel consists of a filter builder  and pinned filter fields, as well as a button to quickly add fields to the panel — **\+ Add field**.

Filter Builder
--------------

Here you can select user-created and saved filters. If you click this button, it reveals a menu that lists all the filter sets saved by the user.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/461/160ab707-673d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

The menu also contains options for creating a new filter and resetting the current active filter.

How to Create a New Filter
--------------------------

To create a new filter, click the **New Filter** button at the bottom left corner. 

Some default fields will be displayed in the opened window.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/461/6fdf614a-c4b4-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

When creating a filter, you are able to do the following:

*   Change the field and its name;

*   Delete the field from the set of filters;

*   Pin the field to the filter panel for quick access;

*   Add a new field from the available ones for this filter;

*   Save a filter, if you're planning to use it often. If it is checked, you can specify the name of a filter, for example, John Smith’s tasks that expired a week ago.

How to Use Fields in a Filter
-----------------------------

The more fields there are in the filter set, the more accurate the query and the data selection will be. The behavior of the filter is as follows — for example, we have made the following query:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/461/2df68f7d-673d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Which can be interpreted as follows:

Title contains: Health

and

Site contains: example.com

and

Owner: Barbara Bates or Julie Edwards or Nick Jackson

and

Business Type: Prospect or Customer

Therefore, the data in the fields where several options are selected will be taken into account with the **OR** operator, and the search by fields is built using the **AND** operator.

How to Operate Dates 
---------------------

In Flowlu, you can filter not only by absolute date (e.g. to show projects with the deadline of April 1, 2019) but also by relative dates, that is, by dates that are calculated relative to today's date. Flowlu offers presets of the most common requests by relative dates: 

*   Yesterday.
*   Today.
*   Tomorrow.
*   This Week.
*   Previous Month.
*   Current Month.
*   Next Month.
*   Current Quarter.
*   etc.

If the presets are not enough for your needs, you can select a custom Period of X days.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/461/a6f2d5be-411a-11ec-9810-fa163e7d9ee1.png)

The example shown in the above picture means the following: to show items with a date that is no earlier than 30 days in the past and no later than 30 days in the future. When the current date changes, the sampling results will change accordingly. If you want to sample by date, with no date limitation in the past, then leave the Last X days field blank, and accordingly, without limitation in the future, leave the Next X days field blank.

**For example,** show projects with a start date no later than 3 days ago:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/461/5af251b5-673d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

**For example,** show projects that start at least 3 days after today:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/461/5df476a6-673d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

How to Pin Fields to the Filter Panel
-------------------------------------

If you often use filtering by a field, you can pin it to the filter panel for quick access. To do this, click the pin icon to the right of the field.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/461/779edc3c-673d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Save the set of filters and filter the data easily.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/461/87440662-673d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

For example, we'll put the Owner and Business Type fields in the list of organizations and name a set of filters: Darth's Customers.

Please note that you can create filter sets and pin both filled and empty fields to be filtered manually.

So, the filter set is created, the fields are fixed, and the result will be as follows:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/461/a58c224c-673d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Flowlu's found two entries that match all search criteria.

To modify a request without opening a set of filters, simply click the necessary field and change the set of options: 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/461/cd87c3fe-673d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

If you want to clear the value of the filtered field, simply click the cross to the right. 

A second press on the cross removes the field from the filter panel. You can return it either through the settings of the filter set or through the button on the _+Add Field_ panel. 

If you have changed the set of filters or the values of the filtered fields, the system will offer you to save the changes in the set.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/461/f3119288-673d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

You can save a filter as new or by overwriting an existing one.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/461/052c3b7d-673e-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/461/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

[Next Flowlu Text Editor](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1474-1770--flowlu-text-editor/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Access Rights: Projects

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/126--projects/ "Projects")
    
4.  Access Rights: Projects

Access Rights: Projects
=======================

* * *

Projects access rights for each employee are configured in **Projects** -> **Settings,** and switch to **Users Access Settings** tab:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/249/995f930d-c4ef-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

You can deny module access to any employee, or grant them **administrator**, **project** **manager** or **employee** rights. If you deny access to projects to any specific employee, the module will not be visible in the sidebar. Also,they will not be able to access projects with a direct link.

**Administrators**
------------------

Administrators have full access to all projects of the company. They see all the information both on their own and on others’ projects. Administrators can create new projects or modify, archive and delete existing projects (including projects created by other users).

**Project Managers**
--------------------

Project managers, like administrators, can create projects. However, they have full access only to those projects in which they are managers. To the projects in which they are only participants, they have limited access.

### **In Others' Projects, Project Managers Can:**

*   view all tasks, leave comments on them and create new ones;
*   view the Gantt diagram, but not edit it;
*   leave comments, notes;
*   create issues on projects;
*   upload, download, delete documents.

### **In Others' Projects, Project Managers Cannot:**

*   see financial information (budget and costs);
*   edit the project, delete it, or send it to the archive;
*   change the project team;
*   enter data into the Gantt chart (view-only mode);
*   move the project card on the board from stage to stage or change the stage on the detailed project page;
*   cancel the completed work items.

In addition, managers do not have access to the Workload Board section.

The user can only become a project member when the responsible manager or administrator adds them to the Project team.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/249/985a5b3c-4fa6-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the **Portal Settings → Projects → Main Settings**, you can give managers access to view all projects:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/249/c3366d46-4fa6-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

If this option is not active, then the manager in the projects, on the board, in the archive sees only them projects and those in which this person is a participant. If the option is active, the manager will be available to view all projects, including those in which he/she is not a participant. In such projects, the manager will be able to perform the same actions as the participants.

**Employees**
-------------

Employees cannot create, modify, archive or delete projects. Employees have access only to the projects they are working on. In those projects, they can view all tasks, view the Gantt chart, upload/download/delete documents, add new tasks, notes and comments. They cannot view financial information (budget and costs) and change the project stage.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/249/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)
