# Flowlu Help Center | To-Do List

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Tasks](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118--tasks/ "Tasks")
    
4.  To-Do List

To-Do List
==========

* * *

This section is inspired by GTD methodology and brings together all the tasks that are related to you. These are personal or delegated tasks, tasks where you're a collaborator, etc. Distributing tasks into the folders will help you to quickly navigate in the large to-do lists, efficiently manage tasks and easily focus on the important assignments.

The first folder **Inbox** is designed to record the tasks that you may need to perform. These can be thoughts and ideas you come up with during the meeting or brainstorming. You can decide whether to perform them and, if so, when exactly to execute them later.  

You can create a task instantly: just click **Add Task**, type in the name of your task and press **Enter**.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/413/9de37b4a-38a9-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

You need to review tasks from Inbox section regularly. Perform them immediately if it takes less than two minutes. Assign them to someone, distribute into the folders with dates, projects, lists, etc. Or, simply delete them if you're certainly not going to do them.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/413/19e61622-38aa-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the **All** folder, the entire list of your tasks is displayed, these are the tasks in which you are **an assignee**. Tasks from the _Inbox_ folder are not displayed here.

The tasks can be additionally sorted by date, priority, owner, project, workspace, lists. This will allow you not to lose track of things and concentrate on the tasks that you need to do the most. You can also filter the tasks by name, deadline, start date, workflow, assignee, owner, tags, collaborators, workspace, project.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/413/c3b70f08-38aa-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

> **Please note** that the selected filter will be saved. And the next time you log in, you will not need to set it again.

The **Today** folder displays overdue tasks, as well as tasks that need to be started or completed today. 

The **Tomorrow** folder contains tasks that require attention only tomorrow.

The **Without Date** folder includes tasks that do not posses any start or end date. According to GTD principles, you need to start them as soon as possible.

In the **Assigned to** a folder, there are all the tasks that you have assigned to your team members. [Find out more about assigning the tasks](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118-4-20--delegating-a-task/)
.

The **I'm a collaborator** folder displays the tasks in which you are a collaborator. Collaborators track the progress of work, receive notifications on new comments, they can add their own comments as well. [Find out more about a collaborator role.](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118-4-125--what-do-roles-mean-owner-assignee-follower/)

Completed tasks that you have assigned to your teammates automatically get into the **Need my Approval** folder. Tasks from this folder should be regularly reviewed, and either be approved or rejected.

The tasks that were assigned to you by your teammates and which you **have completed**, automatically fall into the **W****aiting for Approval** folder. Tasks stay in this folder until their owner approves them.  

Find out more: [How to approve a task or send for revision](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118-4-19--approving-task-or-resubmitting-task-to-revision/)

You can also distribute tasks between your own **Lists** based on a specific task quality or characteristics. These lists are visible only to you. The users can create their own lists for structuring tasks. [Learn more](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118-413-16--creating-and-sorting-tasks-by-lists/)
.

In the **Projects** section, there are **your** projects, where you are **Project Manager**. In the project folders, you can see current and completed tasks, for which you are responsible or an owner.  

The **Workspaces** tab comprises your tasks that are related to the workspaces in which you take a part.

In the **Completed tasks** folder, you can find recently closed tasks or go to the **All Tasks**.  

The Tasks module administrators can also see how their employee to-do list look like by selecting the appropriate teammate in the following field:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/413/8dd106a0-38ac-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/413/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

  

*   [How to Sort Tasks by Lists](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118-413-16--creating-and-sorting-tasks-by-lists/)
    

[Previous Custom Task Statuses](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118-549--custom-task-workflows/)

# Main Section

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/126--projects/ "Projects")
    
4.  [Project: Overview](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/126-412--overview-of-project-sections/ "Project: Overview")
    
5.  Main Section

Main Section
============

* * *

The widgets help you to keep track of the following:

*   the number of payments and expenses, as well as the percentage of payments received;
*   the amount of planned and actual time spent, as well as the percentage of spent time resources.

If you do not need to record the time spent on tasks, you can disable this option in the settings of the Tasks app. In this case, only the number of planned and completed tasks will be displayed in the widget:

*   the remaining number of days before the project deadline;
*   the progress of work performed: current project stage is completed milestones;

![](https://support1.aspro.cloudassets/images/734/fe6f541d-c89e-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

*   custom fields;
*   you can change the project manager, customer, set a project deadline by clicking on the field with the date;

Additionally, on this page you can:

*   add all main items—invoices, tasks, events, and invoices;
*   move the project to the next stage;
*   record payments and expenses;
*   change milestones and fill in the information on them (report, description, attach files)
*   create a chat if it wasn't created during the project creation.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/734/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

Chat
====

* * *

Each project has an individual chat and includes all the team members, as well as the project manager. When you add a new user to the project team, he will automatically join the project discussion. If you didn't create a chat during the project creation, you can still create a chat after it by clicking on the following button:

# Flowlu Help Center | How to Complete or Archive Projects

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/126--projects/ "Projects")
    
4.  How to Complete or Archive Projects

How to Complete or Archive Projects
===================================

* * *

A project can be completed and archived at any point. Archiving is a mandatory part of the completion process. This ensures that the project is not lost and allows you to retrieve information about it in the future or even resume work on it.

> Only **project managers** and **administrators** of the **Projects** module can complete and archive projects.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/129/d8f777a7-0bbc-11f0-b0dd-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

How to Complete and Archive a Project?
--------------------------------------

A project can be completed with one of three statuses:

*   **Closed Successfully** – if the project goals have been achieved.
    
*   **Failed** – if the project goals were not met.
    
*   **Paused** – if work on the project has stopped but is expected to resume in the future.
    

Regardless of the selected status, a project is completed and moved to the archive. Below, we will go over ways to complete it.

### On a Project’s Page

1.  Open a project and access the context menu in the upper right corner
    
2.  Click **Complete**
    

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/129/52851fa3-0bc2-11f0-b0dd-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

A project status change window will appear, where you need to:

*   Select one of the three project statuses
    
*   Choose the project archiving date, which will be displayed on the project’s archive page
    
*   Provide a brief summary of the project’s results (optional)
    
*   Check the box to move project tasks to the archive (optional)
    
*   Check the box to mark planned expenses and revenues as executed (optional)  
    

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/129/88f0fb37-0bc3-11f0-b0dd-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

> When a project is resumed, its planned payments will not revert to their original status, unlike project tasks. They will still be considered executed.

### In the Kanban View

1.  Go to **Projects by Managers** or **Projects by Workflows** tab
    
2.  Hover over the desired project, open the context menu in the upper right corner of its card and select **Complete**
    

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/129/40427ab0-0bc7-11f0-b0dd-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

### In the project list

You can archive multiple projects at once through the list. To do this, check the boxes of the required projects in the list and click the "Finish" button at the bottom of the screen.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/129/5c06d56f-0bc9-11f0-b0dd-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

In the same way, you can complete and archive projects within project portfolios.

  

### How to restore an archived project?

At any time, you can restore a project from the archive and resume it. To do this, go to the **Archived** tab and find the desired project in the list.

Go to the project's detailed page. Open the context menu in the upper right corner (**1**) and select **Resume Project** from the list.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/129/c0a46f5b-0bbc-11f0-b0dd-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

Click **OK** to resume the project.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/129/ca65d8c7-0bbc-11f0-b0dd-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

  

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/129/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Text Editor

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Working in Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1474--working-in-flowlu/ "Working in Flowlu")
    
4.  Flowlu Text Editor

Flowlu Text Editor
==================

* * *

Flowlu features a rich-text editor integrated throughout the platform, including tasks, projects, comments, and the knowledge base. This guide explains how to use the visual toolbar, Markdown syntax, and keyboard shortcuts to create professional documentation and clear communication.

Navigating the Visual Toolbar
-----------------------------

The toolbar at the top of the editor provides a visual way to format your content. Tools are grouped into logical categories to help you find what you need quickly.

### Typography and Hierarchy

The Format dropdown allows you to switch between standard Paragraph text, six levels of Headings (H1–H6), and some special format like quote and code. Adjacent to this, you will find character-level styling for **Bold**, _Italic_, and ~Strikethrough~, along with a Clear Formatting tool to reset any selected text to its default state.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1770/f5869f11-1307-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

### Colors and Emphasis

To further highlight information, use the Text Color and Background Color tools. These are ideal for marking critical warnings in red or highlighting key terms in yellow.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1770/ffc34712-1307-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

### Layout and Organization

Managing the structure of your text is handled through the List and Alignment tools. You can create numbered or bulleted lists and adjust the visual hierarchy using the Increase/Decrease Indent buttons. For overall presentation, you can align text to the left, center, right, or justify it for a formal look.

### Media and Dynamic Content

The text editor supports the following rich media types:   

*   **Images;**
*   **Videos;**
*   **Tables;**
*   **Links.**

For visuals, you can upload images from your computer or paste them directly from your clipboard into the editor.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1770/34ff7f06-1311-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

You may also embed video content from external platforms like YouTube or Vimeo using a direct link.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1770/10f1db9a-1308-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

To create a hyperlink, highlight your text and select the chain icon to attach a web address.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1770/18458e91-1308-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

The table tool allows you to build structured data grids with a customizable number of rows and columns.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1770/1c1a9035-1308-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

Once a table is created, you can quickly add new columns or rows by clicking the plus (+) icon that appears when you hover over the table boundaries.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1770/27d13e46-1308-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

Using Markdown for Fast Formatting
----------------------------------

Flowlu supports Markdown, allowing you to format your text quickly and efficiently without relying solely on the toolbar. This feature is especially useful in areas where the formatting panel might be hidden, such as in comments.

### How It Works

There are two primary ways to use Markdown in Flowlu:

*   **On-the-fly Formatting:** As you type the Markdown syntax, the editor automatically converts it into the corresponding format. For example, typing \*\*bold text\*\* will immediately render as **bold text**.
*   **Paste from Clipboard:** If you copy text already formatted with Markdown from another application and paste it into a Flowlu editor, the system will automatically convert the syntax into formatted text

### Supported Elements

You can use the following Markdown syntax across all Flowlu editors:

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **Feature** | **Syntax Example** |
| Headings | Use # for H1, ## for H2, etc. |
| Quotes | Use > at the start of a line |
| Code Blocks | Wrap text in triple backticks: \` \` \` |
| Styles | \*\*bold\*\*, \*italic\*, or ~~strikethrough~~ |
| Links | \[Display Name\](URL) |

Essential Keyboard Shortcuts
----------------------------

Keyboard shortcuts are the most efficient way to format text during active writing. These commands work in every Flowlu text editor across the platform.

### Standard Formatting

*   **Bold:** Ctrl + B (Windows) / ⌘ + B (Mac)
*   **Italic:** Ctrl + I (Windows) / ⌘ + I (Mac)
*   **Strikethrough:** Ctrl + Shift + X (Windows) / ⌘ + Shift + X (Mac)
*   **Link:** Ctrl + X (Windows) / ⌘ + X (Mac)
*   **Clear Formatting:** Ctrl + \\ (Windows) / ⌘ + \\ (Mac)

### Structural Commands

*   **Headings (1-6):** Ctrl + Alt + 0–6 (Windows) / ⌘ + ⌥ + 0–6 (Mac)
*   **Bulleted List:** Ctrl + Shift + 8 (Windows) / ⌘ + Shift + 8 (Mac)
*   **Numbered List:** Ctrl + Shift + 7 (Windows) / ⌘ + Shift + 7 (Mac)
*   **Code:** Ctrl + Alt + C (Windows) / ⌘ + Alt + C (Mac)
*   **Increase indent:** Ctrl + \[ \] (Windows) / ⌘ + \[ \] (Mac)

### Text Alignment

*   **Left:** Ctrl + Shift + L (Windows) / ⌘ + Shift + L (Mac)
*   **Center:** Ctrl + Shift + E (Windows) / ⌘ + Shift + E (Mac)
*   **Right:** Ctrl + Shift + R (Windows) / ⌘ + Shift + R (Mac)
*   **Justify:** Ctrl + Shift + J (Windows) / ⌘ + Shift + J (Mac)

[Previous Filters](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1474-461--filters/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Turning Emails Into Contacts and Opportunities

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Email](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494--email/ "Email")
    
4.  [Managing Emails](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-515--managing-email/ "Managing Emails")
    
5.  Turning Emails Into Contacts and Opportunities

Turning Emails Into Contacts and Opportunities
==============================================

* * *

If you want to automatically create opportunities and contacts when you receive emails from an unknown sender, select the appropriate options when:

*   connecting a new email account
*   editing an email account in settings

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/517/82c7c93d-5126-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

  

In the **CRM** section, check the **Use CRM** box if you want to use the features of this module when working with emails such as:

*   Create opportunities for incoming emails from a new email address.
*   Create contacts for incoming emails from a new email address.

Indicate the default **source** of a deal and the **pipeline** for all opportunities.

In Flowlu, you can also create new opportunities absolutely for each new incoming email. To do it, specify the necessary email addresses in the appropriate field. Each email address should be located on a new line.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/517/c3ecb8a9-5126-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

If an existing contact already has one active opportunity, a new email will be automatically attached to it, if there are no opportunities yet, a new one will be created.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/517/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Bank Accounts

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Finance](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250--finance/ "Finance")
    
4.  [How to Record the Cash Flow](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-371--cash-flow-management/ "How to Record the Cash Flow")
    
5.  Bank Accounts

Bank Accounts
=============

* * *

To record the cash flow, you need to create an account for the organization. By using them, you can distribute the cash flows. Flowlu accounts are not just bank accounts. You can also add cash accounts. If you have several organizations, then each account must be linked to a specific organization.

Transactions made within bank accounts are recorded in the **Finance** module. They can be automatically recorded for cash flow transactions, but users can add and edit them manually depending on access rights to each bank account. To learn more about access rights for bank accounts, explore the **[related article](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-285--access-rights-to-the-finance-module/)**. 

Bank accounts are created in **Portal Settings** → **Finance** → **Bank Accounts**. To add an account, click on the corresponding button in the upper right corner.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/373/0df5dd18-8826-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the opened side panel enter:

*   account name. For example, the name of the bank;
*   account type — Bank account or Cash;
*   select the organization to which the account belongs and save.
*   currency
*   opening balance if you choose to record account balance

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/373/71294f4e-900f-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/373/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Import Tasks

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Account Settings](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270--account-settings/ "Account Settings")
    
4.  [Data Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270-1472--data-management/ "Data Management")
    
5.  [Import your Data to Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/270-1472-426--data-import/ "Import your Data to Flowlu")
    
6.  Import Tasks

Import Tasks
============

* * *

To import tasks, go to the **Tasks** page, press _three-dots button_ and select the **Import from CSV** option.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/432/76dc4f4d-39ad-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Import is also available from the **Reports** page > **All tasks**. Then press the _three-dots button_ and select **Import from CSV** option. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/432/ca1667d3-39ad-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Step #1. Import Settings

*   upload the CSV file with tasks;
*   select the file encoding (we recommend setting Auto-Detect);
*   select the column separator;
*   if the first line in your file contains field names (titles), check the checkbox;
*   set the default task owner;
*   set the default assignee;
*   select the name format for user search.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/432/1a890246-39ae-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Step #2. Field Matching

As soon as the file is uploaded, the **Next** button will appear at the bottom to the right of the form**,** click on it and go to the field matching.

If your file has been correctly recognized by the system, then you will see blocks arranged horizontally, each of which corresponds to a column in your file.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/432/814676a4-39ae-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

> **If you see a different picture, it means the file was not correctly recognized—check that the separator is selected correctly in step 1. If you cannot fix the problem, please contact our support team by email: [\[email protected\]](mailto:support@flowlu.com)
>  or via app messenger.**

Next, select the Flowlu field in which the field data of the imported file will be written, if you want to skip the field, then select the skip option.

> **If you want to attach your tasks to the projects, then enter the name of the project in the field of the file being imported. If the project is found in Flowlu, the task will be added to it. Otherwise, new project will be created.**

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/432/3f95cb64-39af-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

After setting the field matching, similar to step 1, click **Next.**

### Step #3. Duplicate Control Settings

If the tasks have already been entered into Flowlu, the system will find them (if it’s possible) and process them according to the selected option:

*   Allow (All data will be imported without duplicates);
*   Replace (All fields will be replaced by imported data);
*   Merge (All empty fields will be replaced with imported data);
*   Skip (Duplicate records will be skipped).

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/432/582e2cad-39af-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Select the relevant option and start the import with the **Next** button**.**

After a successful import, you will see a confirmation page and you can proceed to work with tasks.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/432/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | How to Change the Numbering

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Finance](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250--finance/ "Finance")
    
4.  [Estimates](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-251--what-is-an-estimate/ "Estimates")
    
5.  How to Change the Numbering

How to Change the Numbering
===========================

* * *

You can modify the starting number for Estimates and Invoices in [Organization Settings](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-258-259--organization-profile-settings/)
. 

> These settings are only available for users with **Portal Administrator** rights.

Select Finance in the sidebar, go to Settings -> **Accounts and Organizations**, click the name of an Organisation you want to make the changes for and go to **Invoices** tab**.**

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/390/4d5507fe-b888-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

By clicking **Change** you can set the numbering rules for both Invoices and Estimates created on behalf of this organization.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/390/550f5b25-b888-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

  
In the pop–up window, enter the number after which the numbering should start. For example, if you need the numbering of invoices to start from #50, enter 49.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/390/ba89981e-c12f-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

  
  
After saving the settings, all Invoices will be assigned a number automatically based on the entered number.

How to Change the Number of Invoice or Estimate Manually
--------------------------------------------------------

For any invoice, or estimate, you can change the number manually with the prefix. To do this, you need to enable this option in the settings of the **Finance** section.

*   Go to the **Portal Settings** → **Finance** → **Main Settings**
*   Enable **Set an invoice number manually** and **Set an estimate number manually**

After that, the field for entering the number manually will appear in Invoices and Estimates.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/390/4ee678b2-85e0-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

You can change invoice and estimate number immediately or on the Edit page after a document was created. When creating a new Invoice or Estimate, the system will automatically assign the next number in the sequence, disregarding the one that has been entered manually.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/390/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Reporting Periods

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Finance](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250--finance/ "Finance")
    
4.  Reporting Periods

Reporting Periods
=================

* * *

The reporting period is the timeframe that is covered and closed by the financial statements. Typically, one reporting period takes a month, a quarter, or a year. In Flowlu, you can set any timeframe for your reporting period.

This feature helps to avoid unexpected changes in financial transactions and reports, so you can easily compare a certain financial period with a financial statement. 

> Note that reporting periods are available only for users with the **Administrator** access to the Finance module.

How to Create a Reporting Period
--------------------------------

For users with administrator access, reporting periods are available in the **Money** tab of the **Finance** module.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/667/62b9195b-1bc0-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

Click the _three-dot button_ in the **Money** tab and go to **Reporting Periods**.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/667/a8a022d1-1bc0-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the opened window, click the **Create** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/667/152615b5-1bc1-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

While creating a reporting period, you can specify the following information:

1.  Name.
2.  Status: **Closed** – no one can change the information in the reporting period once it's closed. Administrators can change the status to **Open** to apply changes.  
    **Open** – administrators can apply changes to transactions and invoices made within the reporting period.
3.  Start date.
4.  Closure date.
5.  Organization.
6.  Invoices.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/667/eded979f-1c87-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

Once the reporting period is created, you won't be able to change transactions and invoices that occurred during the timeframe you set in the start and closure dates. Changes won't be available only for those transactions that are related to the company you've set as the organization and the bank accounts you included.

If a teammate wants to apply changes to the payment or invoice linked to any of the closed reporting periods, this user will see the following notification:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/667/f475a514-1c88-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

How to Manage Reporting Periods
-------------------------------

Once the reporting period is created, you can open or close it while editing. 

To edit a reporting period, open the list of all reporting periods in the Money tab and click the one you want to edit.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/667/b987f7db-1c8a-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the opened window, you can see the changelog for a reporting period. In the changelog, you can observe all recent updates: reporting period creation, opening, and closure. Also, there you'll see a person who is responsible for a certain change.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/667/cad78369-1c8b-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

If you want to open, close, or change the settings of the reporting period, you can do that by clicking a three-dot button on the top-right corner. By clicking this button, you can also delete the period.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/667/57f6e63e-1c8d-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

The editing mode has the same fields as the creation mode, so you can apply changes to any of them.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/667/2aeb5079-1c8e-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/667/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Where to Fill Out the Custom Fields

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Custom Fields and Lists](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/303--custom-fields-and-lists/ "Custom Fields and Lists")
    
4.  [Custom Fields](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/303-304--how-to-add-a-custom-field/ "Custom Fields")
    
5.  Where to Fill Out the Custom Fields

Where to Fill Out the Custom Fields
===================================

* * *

You can fill out all the custom fields you have added when creating an item or on its page.

For example, the **opportunity** data is specified after its creation in the **Custom Fields** section:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/307/9ea203a9-40a9-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

You can also add custom fields to the opportunity when creating it:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/307/1c5c6e81-40aa-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the **task**, you can fill in the custom fields when creating the task:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/307/7bf9979e-40aa-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Or after creating it, on the Custom Field tab:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/307/73725630-40ab-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

On the **accounts** pages, the custom fields are filled in the **Custom Fields** tab:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/307/ebaed380-40ab-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the **project**, custom fields are displayed on its page, under the description and tags:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/307/6bbb72da-40ac-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

As you can see, you do not need any specific knowledge to create such fields. You can set up and display the custom fields based on the needs of your business. Record all the information about your clients, projects, opportunities and quickly access it.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/307/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Access Rights: Agile Projects

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338--agile-projects/ "Agile Projects")
    
4.  [Main Features and Settings](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-339--main-terms-and-settings/ "Main Features and Settings")
    
5.  Access Rights: Agile Projects

Access Rights: Agile Projects
=============================

* * *

Access rights are set in the **Marketplace** section. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/347/db6ee781-641b-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/347/6bdbfb29-64d6-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

_Assigning the access rights_

You can also set the permissions on the user page. You can go to the user page from the **My Team** section:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/347/eead6a31-64d6-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

_Click the **Pen Icon** to go to the settings._ 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/347/4721b07f-64d7-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/347/8f5408b5-64d7-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

_User card_

By default, all users are granted **Employee** access rights. To any user, you can give the **Administrator** access rights for full access to the application functionality or deny access.

**An agile manager**

*   has full access to his/her own project.
*   does not see the projects where he/she is not a participant.
*   in the projects where a user is a participant, this person does not have access to settings—can not create/edit categories, invite teams, create sprints. In these projects, he sees all the issues, can add new ones, assign responsible persons, set priorities, etc.
*   does not have access to the application settings: creating the workflows or relation types for issues, importing from JIRA.

**An employee**

*   cannot create projects.
*   has no access to the project settings, in which he/she is a participant– creating/editing categories, inviting teams, creating sprints.
*   does not see projects in which he/she is not a participant
*   has full access to the issues of the project. This person can add an issue, assign responsible, set priority, etc.

### How to Share Kanban Boards in Agile Projects

Share your Agile board with other users by clicking the icon left to the **Create** button, in the right corner of an Agile board.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/347/4ac53314-8100-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

# Business Processes

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Mobile App](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487--mobile-app/ "Mobile App")
    
4.  Business Processes

Business Processes
==================

* * *

The Business Processes module in the Flowlu mobile app allows you to participate in and manage structured, automated workflows from anywhere. It is designed to streamline multi-step operations like approvals, requests, and onboarding, ensuring that tasks are seamlessly passed between employees without anything getting lost.  
This guide explains how to interact with business processes, whether you are completing an assigned task or overseeing an entire workflow.

Two Ways to Interact with Business Processes
--------------------------------------------

The mobile app provides two primary entry points depending on your role:  

1.  My tasks: For users who need to complete a specific step within a process.
2.  Business processes: For managers or users who need to view the overall progress of all process instances.

### First Way: Completing Your Assigned Tasks

When a business process requires your action (e.g., you need to approve a request), a task will be assigned to you.

### How to Access Your Tasks:  

1.  From the bottom navigation bar, tap on Modules.
2.  Locate and expand the Business Processes section.
3.  Select My tasks.

This will open a list of all business process tasks currently assigned to you.

### How to Action a Task:  

1.  From the My tasks list, tap on the task you need to complete (e.g., "Review employee's PTO request").
2.  The task detail screen will open, providing a description of what is required.
3.  You will see specific action buttons related to the process, such as Approve and Deny.
4.  Tap the appropriate button to complete your part of the process. The process will then automatically move to the next stage or be completed.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1634/80190bf7-9dd8-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

### Second Way: Viewing and Managing a Business Process

This section is for overseeing the status of all active instances within a specific business process.  
How to Access a Business Process:  

1.  From the Modules screen, expand the Business Processes section.
2.  Select Business processes. This will display a list of all available processes.
3.  Tap on a process name to open its board view.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1634/6d19c6ed-9dc1-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

### Navigating the Process Board:

The process board gives you a visual overview of all active items (instances), organized into columns that represent the stages of the workflow.  

*   Stages: Each column is a stage (e.g., "PTO Request," "Manager Approval").
*   Instances: Each card on the board is a single instance of the process (e.g., one employee's specific PTO request).
*   Filtering and Sorting: Use the Filters and Settings icons at the bottom of the screen to narrow down the view by criteria like Owner or to Group by specific fields.
*   Switching Views: Use the dropdown menu at the top to switch between the main board and the Archive to see completed or archived instances.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1634/fca0e80f-9dc1-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

### How to Launch a New Process

You can start a new instance of any business process directly from the mobile app.  

1.  Navigate to the specific process board you want to launch (e.g., "PTO Request").
2.  Tap the plus (+) icon in the top-right corner.
3.  The creation form for that specific process will appear.
4.  Fill in the required fields, which are customized for that process (e.g., Employee, Time-Off Request (Days), Reason For Request).
5.  Tap Save to launch the new instance. It will appear as a new card in the first stage of the process board.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1634/455ae75f-9dc2-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

# Flowlu Help Center | Burndown Chart

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338--agile-projects/ "Agile Projects")
    
4.  [Scrum Project Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-348--project-management-with-scrum/ "Scrum Project Management")
    
5.  [Sprint](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-348-353--sprint-planning/ "Sprint")
    
6.  Burndown Chart

Burndown Chart
==============

* * *

Due to this report, you will be able to track the speed of the team’s work and understand how it fits in with the deadlines.

The report clearly displays how many story point should be completed every day and how many of them are completed in reality. Story points are recorded in the report when the issue moves to the last stage.

To open a report, go to the project:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/358/8333bf34-6588-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

_Going to the Burndown chart_

### How is Data Measured?

Say, you have determined the sprint volume at 50 story points and set the deadline of 20 working days. A straight line (the line of ideal burndown) on the diagram will show how many story points ideally should burn each day. The actual line will display how many story points are left to be closed for the current day. If the actual line is above the ideal line, the team is lagging behind, below—it is ahead of the plan.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/358/fa05960b-6588-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

_In the report, Y-axis displays the total number of remaining story points, and on the scale X—the sprint days._

If you run multiple sprints, then each will display its own report.  

Sprint Completion
-----------------

At the sprint completion, all incomplete issues will be moved back to the backlog with the same stage of work as they were. At the planning meeting for the next sprint, they should be revised.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/358/0fad8e6f-6589-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/358/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Lists

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Mobile App](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487--mobile-app/ "Mobile App")
    
4.  Lists

Lists
=====

* * *

The Lists module in the Flowlu mobile app allows you to access, add, and manage records in custom-made lists on the go. It is the perfect tool for capturing structured data — such as user research findings, inventory checks, or contact lists — directly from your mobile device.

**Note:** The creation and configuration of the lists themselves, including setting up custom fields and managing user permissions, is done in the desktop version of Flowlu. The mobile app is designed for working with the data within those pre-configured lists.

Accessing Your Lists
--------------------

To begin working with your data, first navigate to the Lists module.

1.  From the bottom navigation bar, tap on **Modules**.
2.  Scroll down and select **Lists**.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1635/b98c4a21-9dc6-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

This will display a directory of all the lists you have permission to access (e.g., "User Research"). Each list will show the total number of records it contains.

Navigating a List
-----------------

After selecting a specific list, you can view all of its records.

1.  Tap on a list name (e.g., "User Research") to open it.
2.  You will see all the records (or items) in that list, typically showing key information like the record name and creation date.
3.  Use the **Search** bar at the top to quickly find a specific record.

How to Add a New Record
-----------------------

You can easily add new entries to any list you have access to.

1.  From within a specific list, tap the **plus (+)** icon in the top-right corner of the screen.
2.  The **"Create a list of elements"** form will appear.
3.  Fill in the information for each of the custom fields that were configured for this list.
4.  Once all the required details are entered, tap **Save**. The new record will be added to the list.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1635/f898ba5d-9dc6-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

How to View and Edit a Record
-----------------------------

You can view the full details of any record and make changes as needed.

1.  From the record list, tap on any item to open its detail view. This screen will display all the information for that record.
2.  To make changes, tap the **edit** (pencil) icon in the top-right corner.
3.  The **"Edit Topic"** screen will open, allowing you to update the values in any of the fields.
4.  After making your changes, tap **Save** to update the record.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1635/72130a33-9dc7-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

# Managing Opportunities

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Mobile App](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487--mobile-app/ "Mobile App")
    
4.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487-1488--crm/ "CRM")
    
5.  Managing Opportunities

Managing Opportunities
======================

* * *

The Opportunities section in the mobile app is a powerful tool for managing your sales pipeline on the go. This guide will walk you through how to view, filter, create, and manage your opportunities directly from your mobile device.

Accessing and Viewing Opportunities
-----------------------------------

To begin, navigate to the **Modules** tab in the bottom bar, select the **CRM** module, and then tap on **Opportunities (1)**.

*   **List View:** Displays your opportunities in a vertical list. Each entry shows the title, budget, customer, and current stage, which is indicated by a colored progress bar.
*   **Board View (Kanban):** Organizes your opportunities into columns based on their sales stage. Each column header shows the total number of deals and their combined value. You can move an opportunity to a new stage by simply dragging and dropping its card into the corresponding column.

To switch between these views, use the **Board (2)** and **List (3)** view icons located in the top-right header.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1489/d7c5c9ae-525f-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Filtering and Customizing Your View
-----------------------------------

To locate specific opportunities quickly, you have two powerful options at the bottom of the screen:

*   **Filters (1):** Tap the **Filters** button to open a comprehensive list of parameters. You can narrow down your opportunities by Currency, Status, Pipeline Stage, Start Date, Account, Budget, and many other criteria. Tap **Apply** to see the results.
*   **Settings (2):** Tap the **Settings icon** next to Filters to open additional view options. Here, you can **Split columns by** a specific field or change the **Sort** order of the opportunities in your current view

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1489/e3360c6e-525f-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Creating a New Opportunity
--------------------------

To add a new opportunity to your pipeline:

1.  Tap the **blue plus icon (1)** in the top right corner.
2.  The **New Opportunity** screen will appear. Fill in the necessary details, including the Name, Description, Opportunity Amount, Source, Assignee, and Customer.
3.  Tap **Save** **(2)** to create the opportunity.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1489/f0a924e0-525f-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Managing an Individual Opportunity
----------------------------------

Tap on any opportunity card **(1)** in either List or Board view to open its detailed page. This page is organized with tabs for easy navigation:

*   **Main:** An overview of the opportunity's core details, such as Amount, Source, Assignee, and Customer information.
*   **Activities:** View and manage associated tasks and events.
*   **Email:** Access email correspondence related to the opportunity.
*   **Documents:** Manage attached documents like contracts or proposals.

From the detail page, tap the **three-dot menu (2)** in the top-right corner to access more actions, such as **Copy link**, **Share**, **Create Invoice**, **Create Project**, **Duplicate**, or **Delete** the opportunity

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1489/00947782-5260-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Changing an Opportunity's Stage
-------------------------------

*   **In Board View:** Drag and drop the opportunity card into the new stage's column.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1489/055f1a92-5260-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

*   **In List View:** Tap the opportunity to open its details, tap the current stage, select the new stage from the list, and confirm.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1489/0c5a1107-5260-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

[Previous Managing Organizations and Contacts](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487-1488-1490--managing-organizations-and-contacts/)

# Payables & Receivables

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Finance](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250--finance/ "Finance")
    
4.  Payables & Receivables

Payables & Receivables
======================

* * *

Business is based on obligations-relationships between a debtor and a creditor, where one party must perform a certain action for the benefit of the other.

A customer must perform a final settlement under a contract, a company must pay a contractor for services, a supplier must ship goods. All these and many other similar relationships are united by the existence of obligations.

To record obligations in the system, there is a corresponding module called Payables & Receivables.

Installing the Module
---------------------

The Payables & Receivables module is installed as an add-on to the Finance module.

To install the module, go to Apps.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1774/ad6d6b14-1de4-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

In the list of applications, find the Payables & Receivables module and click on it.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1774/c1d512ec-1de4-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

Click the Install button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1774/cd68c34b-1de4-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

You can access the Payables & Receivables module through the Finance module menu — a corresponding subsection will appear there.

After installation, you can proceed to the module settings or begin working with obligations.

  

*   [Access Rights: Payables & Receivables](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-1774-1775--access-rights-payables--receivables/)
    
*   [Working with Payables & Receivables](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-1774-1776--working-with-payables--receivables/)
    

[Previous Issued Loans](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-1757-1760--issued-loans/)

[Next Access Rights: Payables & Receivables](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-1774-1775--access-rights-payables--receivables/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Creating the Project

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338--agile-projects/ "Agile Projects")
    
4.  [Scrum Project Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-348--project-management-with-scrum/ "Scrum Project Management")
    
5.  Creating the Project

Creating the Project
====================

* * *

A new project is created in the **Projects** section. In the opened sidebar, enter the following:

*   name;
*   prefix (or project key)—the letter values that will be assigned to each issue;
*   choose the [workflow](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-339-346--setting-the-workflow/)
    , to which the project issues will be assigned;
*   leave your name or change the project lead;
*   invite the team that will participate in the project;
*   remove any types of issues that you do not plan to use in the project.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/349/2a55c71c-64d9-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Save the project. The invited participants will receive **a push notification** of the invitation to the project.

Add the project to your favorites, if you want to have quick access to it from any section of the application. To do this, go to the project list, hover over a specific project and click on the _star sign_. After that, you can go to the project right from the Agile Projects tab.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/349/b3ef6017-64d9-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Now, you can start filling the [backlog.](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-348-350--filling-the-backlog/)

Custom Fields
-------------

For each Agile project, you can set specific custom fields. Note that they're unique for each project and apply only to tasks inside this project.

To create custom fields for your Agile project, go to Portal Settings → Custom Fields. Then scroll until you see the **Agile Projects** section.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/349/ae682a8b-7237-11ee-a5ba-fa163e2ff576.png)

To create a new custom field, click the **Add Field** button. To learn more about custom field types and how to correctly set them, explore the [related knowledge base article](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/303-304-307--where-to-fill-the-custom-fields/)
.

### How to Manage Custom Fields in Agile Projects

First, go to a specific project you created custom fields for. As mentioned earlier, custom fields in Agile are applied to tasks. In this case, to access and fill in custom fields, open the task you want to specify fields for. In the right tab, you can see all details related to the task: deadlines, priorities, assignees, etc. You can see custom fields related to the project at the bottom of the right sidebar of the task.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/349/492ee93f-723b-11ee-a5ba-fa163e2ff576.png)

To change the content of a custom field, click on it and set any changes you need.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/349/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Files

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/126--projects/ "Projects")
    
4.  [Project: Overview](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/126-412--overview-of-project-sections/ "Project: Overview")
    
5.  Files

Files
=====

* * *

Each team member can view project documents as well as add his own. For convenience, you can distribute documents of the same type in different folders.

![](https://support1.aspro.cloudassets/images/741/ebece18e-c8a3-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

The documents can be displayed in the Tile or List view.

![](https://support1.aspro.cloudassets/images/741/197cb0ce-c8a4-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

When you click on a document, there is additional information on this document displayed in the window on the right:

*   size and type of file;
*   by whom and when created;
*   by whom and when changed;
*   you can also view or download this file.

![](https://support1.aspro.cloudassets/images/741/e089ff56-c8a6-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

# Differences Between Kanban and Scrum

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338--agile-projects/ "Agile Projects")
    
4.  [Agile Method: Core Principles](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637--agile-method/ "Agile Method: Core Principles")
    
5.  [Kanban method](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637-1652--kanban-method/ "Kanban method")
    
6.  Differences Between Kanban and Scrum

Differences Between Kanban and Scrum
====================================

* * *

Kanban and Scrum are often compared when choosing the right approach for project delivery. The two are sometimes confused, but while they share similar principles, they are implemented very differently.

### **Time**

*   **Scrum** — Work is organized in short, timeboxed iterations called **Sprints**. Each Sprint has a clearly defined goal.
    
*   **Kanban** — Work follows a **continuous flow**, similar to a production line, without fixed-length iterations.
    

### **Metrics**

*   **Scrum metrics** typically include:
    
    *   Number of completed tasks within a Sprint.
        
    *   Percentage of completed tasks relative to the Product Backlog.
        
    *   Total **Story Points** delivered during the Sprint.
        
*   **Kanban metrics** focus on flow:
    
    *   **Throughput** — the number of tasks completed in a given time frame (day, week, or month).
        
    *   **Lead Time** — the total time it takes for a task to move from “in progress” to “done.” In other words, how long it takes for a card to move across the Kanban board from left to right.
        

### **Fundamental Differences**

*   **Scrum** is a **framework** — it prescribes a structured set of roles, events, and rules that define how work should be organized.
    
*   **Kanban** is a **method** — a flexible set of tools and practices that teams can adapt to build their workflows.
    

A Kanban team is not required to follow specific cadences or all standard practices. Instead, teams can experiment and even adopt certain Scrum practices if useful for their context.  

[Previous Kanban Values and Principles](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637-1652-1653--kanban-values/)

# AI Voice Task Creation

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Mobile App](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487--mobile-app/ "Mobile App")
    
4.  [Tasks & Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487-1504--tasks-projects/ "Tasks & Projects")
    
5.  AI Voice Task Creation

AI Voice Task Creation
======================

* * *

Capturing tasks on the go is now faster and more intuitive with Flowlu's AI integration. Instead of typing out every detail, you can use the voice command feature within the mobile app. The built-in AI doesn't just transcribe your voice; it analyzes the context of your speech to automatically structure the task for you.

This feature is designed for fast-paced environments where you need to log an idea or a directive the moment it occurs, ensuring no detail is lost.

How to Access AI Voice Task Creation on Your Phone
--------------------------------------------------

To use this feature, **find** the Flowlu app icon, **tap and hold** the app until the quick menu appears and **select** Open AI Form — Voice Task Creation. Flowlu will open the homepage and auto-fill the field at the bottom of the screen with what you’re saying at that moment. Speak your task, then tap **Send** when you’re done.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1705/390b065a-f134-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

You can also create a task using your voice while on the Tasks tab in the Flowlu mobile app:

*   Tap on the blue **Quick Actions (+)** button in the upper right corner of the screen.
*   From the menu that appears, select **Voice Task Creation**.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1705/efaa9cac-f118-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

Recording Your Task
-------------------

Now you can keep your tasks on track with a single sentence. Simply start speaking naturally to describe what needs to be done. You can mention the main goal, provide a background for the work, and list specific steps. The AI is trained to understand conversational language. However, to help you get the best results, here’s a simple guide to structuring your voice command:

**1\. Start with a clear beginning**

Use phrases like “Create a task…”, “Add a task…”, or “We need to…”.

**2\. Specify the person responsible**

*   “to \[Name\]” or “assign to \[Name\]”
*   If it’s for you: say “to me” or “assign to me”

**3\. Add collaborators if needed**

*   The first name is the assignee
*   Additional names are collaborators
*   You can say: “and \[Name2\]” or “collaborator \[Name2\]”

**4\. Add followers**

*   Say “followers” or “follower will be…”
*   Then list: “followers \[Name3\] and \[Name4\]”

**5\. Mention the project**

*   At the beginning: “Create a task in the \[Project Name\] project…”
*   Or at the end: “…link to project \[Project Name\]” or “attach to \[Project Name\]”

**6\. Describe the task**

A short action phrase is enough: “test the new UI,” “prepare the report,” “review the draft,” etc.

**7\. Set the deadline**

Examples:

*   “deadline tomorrow at 6 p.m.”
*   “by Friday”
*   “by the end of the day”

**8\. Start date**

If you don’t mention it, it defaults to now.

**9\. Anything else**

Any details you don’t categorize will be added to the task description.

  
Once you have finished speaking, tap the **Send** button. The system will take a few seconds to process the audio and create your task.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1705/3e4ab3ee-f11b-11f0-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

If the AI missed a detail or if you want to add more information, you can edit your task as usual by tapping on it, then find the three dots in the upper right corner and select **Edit**.  

[Previous Managing Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487-1504-1506--managing-projects/)

[Next CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487-1488--crm/)

# Kanban method

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338--agile-projects/ "Agile Projects")
    
4.  [Agile Method: Core Principles](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637--agile-method/ "Agile Method: Core Principles")
    
5.  Kanban method

Kanban method
=============

* * *

Kanban is a project management method designed around building a continuous workflow, also called a flow-based system. When working with Kanban, the team strives to achieve maximum efficiency, and the process is made transparent through visualization.  

There is no single consensus on whether Kanban fully belongs to the Agile family of methodologies.

*   Some practitioners believe that Kanban teams are even more adaptable to change than Scrum teams.
    
*   Others argue that Kanban does not implement all the principles of Agile and therefore should not be classified as a fully Agile methodology.
    

### **Origins of Kanban**

Kanban was developed at Toyota in the 1940s as a production management system. It was originally used to organize automobile manufacturing. Managers knew exactly how many cars had to be produced and how many parts were required. This planning enabled shops to follow the “just-in-time” principle, while workers gradually refined their skills to near-automatic precision.

In the 2000s, Microsoft engineers adapted key Kanban principles for software development. Teams building operating systems started creating products in a continuous flow. Over time, Kanban evolved into its modern form and spread widely in IT and services — from technical support to food delivery.

### **Strengths and Limitations**

Kanban’s main drawback stems from its greatest strength — continuous flow. While this model supports efficiency, team members may find it difficult to accurately assess their workload within a project. Estimating intellectual effort is inherently challenging, which can sometimes result in overwork and employee burnout.

  

*   [Kanban Values and Principles](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637-1652-1653--kanban-values/)
    
*   [Differences Between Kanban and Scrum](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637-1652-1654--kanban-and-scrum/)
    

[Previous Scrum Master](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637-1645-1651--scrum-master/)

[Next Kanban Values and Principles](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637-1652-1653--kanban-values/)

# Agile Principles

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338--agile-projects/ "Agile Projects")
    
4.  [Agile Method: Core Principles](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637--agile-method/ "Agile Method: Core Principles")
    
5.  Agile Principles

Agile Principles
================

* * *

The philosophy of Agile project management is outlined in **[the Agile Manifesto](https://agilemanifesto.org/iso/en/principles.html)
.**  
It was created by a group of 17 software developers in 2001.

The philosophy itself is based on **12 principles**:  

1.  _Customer satisfaction through early and continuous delivery_
2.  _Welcome changing requirements, even late in development_
3.  _Frequent delivery of working software, with a preference to the shorter timescale  
    _
4.  _Business people and developers must work together daily throughout the project_
5.  _Empower motivated individuals_
6.  _Face-to-face communication is most effective_
7.  _Working software is the primary measure of progress._
8.  _Agile processes promote sustainable development._
9.  _Continuous attention to technical excellence and good design enhances agility._
10.  _Simplicity — the art of maximizing the amount of work not done — is essential._
11.  _The best architectures, requirements, and designs emerge from self-organizing teams._
12.  _Teams should frequently reflect on how to improve and adjust their practices accordingly_

These principles are summarized by the **core values** of Agile:

*   Individuals and interactions over processes and tools
*   Working software over comprehensive documentation
*   Customer collaboration over contract negotiation
*   Responding to change over following a plan

**Following these Agile principles in management helps to:**

*   Break down large-scale development into specific milestones and tasks
*   Focus on the goals and customer needs that matter most at the moment
*   Simplify workflows and improve team communication
*   Receive regular, timely feedback from colleagues
*   Expand each team member’s area of responsibility

This system also implies a **human-centered approach to team management** and **equality among all team members.**

[Previous Agile Method: Core Principles](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637--agile-method/)

[Next Who is Agile for, and how to implement it?](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637-1641--who-is-agile-for/)

# Agile Projects

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Mobile App](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487--mobile-app/ "Mobile App")
    
4.  Agile Projects

Agile Projects
==============

* * *

The Agile Projects module in the Flowlu mobile app is a powerful tool designed for managing iterative workflows, such as software development, content creation, or marketing campaigns. It allows you to organize tasks into sprints, visualize progress on a Kanban board, and keep your team aligned, all from your mobile device.  
This guide will walk you through creating, navigating, and managing your agile projects.

Accessing Agile Projects
------------------------

To get started, navigate to the Agile Projects section from the main menu.  

1.  From the bottom navigation bar, tap on Modules.
2.  Scroll through the list and select Agile projects.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1632/f8141c13-9dbc-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

This will open the main list view, displaying all the agile projects you have access to.

The Main Project List
---------------------

On the main screen, you can search for a specific project or refine the list using filters and settings.  

*   Search: Use the search bar at the top to quickly find a project by its name or key.
*   Filters (1): Tap the Filters icon to open a panel where you can narrow down the project list by criteria such as Project Lead or Project Key. Tap Apply to see the results.
*   Settings (2): Tap the Settings icon to open view options. Here, you can Group by or change the Sort order of the projects in the list.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1632/2b14a180-9dbd-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Creating a New Agile Project
----------------------------

To start a new project:  

1.  From the main Agile projects list, tap the plus (+) icon in the top-right corner.
2.  The New Project screen will appear.
3.  Tap Save to create the project.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1632/a4e38fba-9dbe-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Navigating an Agile Project
---------------------------

Tapping on a project from the list opens its detailed workspace, which is organized into several key tabs:  

1.  Backlog: This tab contains all the issues that have not yet been assigned to a specific sprint. It is also where you plan and manage your sprints. You can create a new sprint directly from this screen.
2.  Board: This is a Kanban-style board that provides a visual representation of your workflow.
    *   Issues are displayed as cards organized into columns based on their current status (e.g., To Do, In Progress).
    *   You can use the dropdown menu at the top to switch between viewing the entire project board or focusing only on the issues in a specific sprint.
3.  Tasks: This tab provides a simple list view of all issues (tasks, bugs, stories) within the project. It includes a search bar for quickly finding specific items.
4.  Team: A list of all members assigned to the project.
5.  Description: This tab displays a summary of the project, including its description, Project Lead, workflow, and issue categories.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1632/cb456b76-9dbe-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Managing Project Items
----------------------

From within a project, you can perform several key actions to keep work moving forward.  
Creating a New Issue (Task, Bug, etc.)  

1.  Tap the three-dot menu in the top-right corner and select Create Issue. (Alternatively, on the Board tab, you can tap the plus (+) icon within a status column to add an issue directly to that stage).
2.  In the New Issue form, fill in the details:
    *   Type: Select if it is a Task, Bug, Story, etc.
    *   Name and Priority.
    *   Estimation: Add a time or point estimate for the effort required.
    *   Assignee and Followers.
    *   Task Description, Category, and attach files using the Attach Files button.
3.  Tap Save.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1632/f4e5c58d-9dbe-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Creating a New Sprint  

------------------------

1.  Use the three-dot menu in the top-right corner and select Create Sprint.
2.  In the Create Sprint form, enter a Name, Start Date, End Date, and a Goal or description.
3.  Tap Save. You can now drag and drop issues from the backlog into your new sprint.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1632/20994cc8-9dbf-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Editing a Project
-----------------

To modify the details of an existing project:  

1.  Navigate to the project's page.
2.  Tap the three-dot menu in the top-right corner and select Edit.
3.  The Edit screen will appear, allowing you to update the project's name, description, manager, and other settings.
4.  Tap Save to apply your changes.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1632/3f5a8171-9dbf-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

# Flowlu Help Center | Managing the Backlog

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338--agile-projects/ "Agile Projects")
    
4.  [Scrum Project Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-348--project-management-with-scrum/ "Scrum Project Management")
    
5.  Managing the Backlog

Managing the Backlog
====================

* * *

To quickly navigate in the backlog and find issues for a specific topic or feature, use categories, tags, links between issues.

Setting the Priorities
----------------------

It is important to update the list of the backlog elements regularly: change the order of issues, raising the most important and valuable ones above. The higher the issue is in the backlog, the more specific it should be formulated and described. Only such issues can be included in the sprint.

The issues for which there is no clear understanding of implementation or not a single solution should be located below.

To change the issues display order in the backlog, drag the field (outside the area with the name) and move it using the Drag&Drop method.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/351/02d44fea-64df-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

For moving multiple backlog elements at once, hold down the **CTRL key** (Windows) or the **Command** (Mac), left-click on the necessary issues (outside the area with the name) and drag:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/351/0d3cf33c-64e0-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

If you need quickly to move one or more requirements from the end of the backlog list to the beginning, or vice versa, right-click. In the opened menu, select the necessary action.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/351/81fb5bd7-64e1-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

The order of issues in the backlog can be changed constantly. The new requirements should be added regularly, the irrelevant ones should be deleted or closed with a comment "Will not be completed."

Grouping of Issues by Category
------------------------------

Create your own categories to combine requirements by a specific feature. For example, the category can be a project implementation stage (procurement of materials, assembly, design, promotion), type of activity (organizational questions, training, documentation), functional blocks (personal cabinet, notification system, integration). The category can also be a separate large user story that can not be executed for 1 sprint. For example, “The development and set up of automatic business processes”.

How to Create a Category
------------------------

To add a category, in the issue creation or editing window, enter a name and press **"+"**:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/351/d6b1279a-64e1-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

You can also create a new category via the **"+"** sign, located in the backlog menu next to the **Categories** column, or by clicking to the right from the **Create** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/351/f65f62ce-64e1-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

After that, the window for creating a category opens. Here you can choose a color and assign a responsible person, whose name will be automatically assigned to all issue in this category.

Due to the selected colour indicator, you can quickly determine which group a particular issue belongs to when viewing the list of backlogs and [Agile board](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)
.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/351/17338b43-64e2-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

You can also sort the requirements by categories using the Drag&Drop method, simply by dragging and dropping them into the necessary folder.

To assign several backlog elements to a certain category, press the **CTRL key** (Windows) or the **Command** (Mac), left-click on the necessary issues (outside the area with the name) and drag:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/351/28201c4d-64e2-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### How to Edit/Delete Categories

Category editing is available in the backlog list. Just move the cursor at the name:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/351/5938a6e9-64e2-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

To delete the category, go to  **Project** **Details** → **Categories**

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/351/9555b0f5-64e2-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Here you can also change the order in which categories are displayed with a simple drag and drop option:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/351/d480b66b-64e2-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

How to Find Issues in the Backlog Quickly
-----------------------------------------

To quickly find issues in the project backlog, use text search or multiple filters.

Text search matches the conditions contained in the header of the backlog elements.

To filter the list of the backlog elements by issue type, priority, category, responsible, click on the corresponding section. In the list of backlog, you will see all the issues related to it.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/351/2b89830a-64e3-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

You can select several sections at once, and in the list of the backlog, you will see the issues corresponding to the specified parameters. For example, we need to quickly find errors with high and critical priority. Select these sections and get the corresponding list:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/351/5ab3b23e-64e3-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Such issues can immediately be sent to the sprint for the soonest solution. Find out more about [sprint planning](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-348-353--sprint-planning/)
 below.

To remove a filter from the selected categories, issue types, click on them again.

Search in the Issues Section 
-----------------------------

You can also search for issues by several parameters in the **Issues** section. There is a filter here available with additional parameters such as status, issue author.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/351/74552f19-64e3-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

# Finance

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Mobile App](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487--mobile-app/ "Mobile App")
    
4.  Finance

Finance
=======

* * *

The Flowlu mobile application includes a comprehensive Finance module, enabling you to manage key financial operations directly from your device. This allows you to stay on top of your company's financial health, track transactions, and manage billing documents even when you're away from the office.

This section provides detailed guidance on how to utilize the various financial tools available in the mobile app.

Learn more about these key finance features in the articles below:

*   **How to Manage Invoices:** Learn how to create, send, and track the payment status of your invoices.
*   **How to Manage Estimates:** Discover how to prepare and send sales quotations to your clients.
*   **How to Manage Money Transactions:** Understand how to record and monitor all your income, expenses, and internal money transfers.
*   **How to Manage Imprest Fund:** Learn about managing petty cash and other funds.

  

*   [Managing Invoices](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487-1491-1492--managing-invoices/)
