*   [Managing Money](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487-1491-1508--managing-money/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Report: Invoices

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Finance](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250--finance/ "Finance")
    
4.  [Invoices](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-258--how-to-prepare-an-invoice/ "Invoices")
    
5.  Report: Invoices

Report: Invoices
================

* * *

Regardless of how payments are recorded (online payment, with or without creating a transaction), information about potential and actual invoice payments is displayed in the report. To see the report, go to the Reports section:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/321/0d3b7316-851d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Find the **Invoices** report.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/321/3b43d5c7-851d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

**This report generates the following data:**

*   The amount of expected payments on current (not overdue due) invoices.
*   The amount of overdue payments up to 15, 30 or more days.
*   The amount of expected and received payments in each month.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/321/44db1bba-85dd-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

You can also see a detailed list of issued and paid invoices each month. Under the graph you can see the amounts, clicking on which you can open the detailed list.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/321/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

[Previous How to set up WayForPay](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-258-279-656--how-to-set-up-wayforpay/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Use Case: Flowlu for Marketing Agency

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Getting Started](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/201--getting-started/ "Getting Started")
    
4.  [Use Cases](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/201-616--use-cases/ "Use Cases")
    
5.  Use Case: Flowlu for Marketing Agency

Use Case: Flowlu for Marketing Agency
=====================================

* * *

Flowlu is widely used by marketers, copywriters, advertising managers, media directors, brand strategists, SEO managers, analysts and other creative professionals to manage their daily operations. Our cloud-based platform can help you automate every stage of your marketing lifecycle and boost team productivity.

Check out our step-by-step guide for configuring [Flowlu for marketing teams and agencies](https://www.flowlu.com/solutions/marketing-project-management-software/)
.

Step 1: Create User Roles for Your Marketing Team
-------------------------------------------------

Flowlu lets you assign specific permissions not only to particular users but also groups of people. You can create **[user roles](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/201-270-272-492--user-roles/)** like SEO Managers, Brand Strategists, Video Team and pre-define their permissions to different modules. Permissions ensure your teammates see only those data they need to. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/94bdff15-c7cb-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

User roles are also useful when assigning several task collaborators or followers in bulk.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/e30ada6a-c7cb-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

> **Tip:** Utilize [**Workspaces**](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/502--workspaces/)
>  to create separate team spaces for collaboration. In Flowlu, you can relate tasks and projects to the workspaces. Plus, all workspace members have their own feed to exchange announcements or important notes available only to them.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/c9c504e8-c7cc-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Step 2: Automatically Capture New Work Requests  

--------------------------------------------------

You can either automatically create new work requests out of our built-in **web forms** or **new** **emails** with [email integration](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-512--how-to-set-up-email-sync/)
. Flowlu captures all the information and creates an opportunity.

[Web forms](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-323-329-330--creating-leads-from-the-web-form/)
 can be installed on your website to capture new leads.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/12037350-c7cd-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In Flowlu, you can [sync emails](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-323-329-517--turning-emails-into-contacts-opportunities-tasks/)
 only from particular folders. For example, you can create a rule for your inbox in Gmail and move emails with specific words to a particular label, which then will be synchronized with Flowlu. That will help you avoid any clutter in your Flowlu's emails and requests. [Learn more](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-515--managing-email/)
. 

> **Tip:** Set up [**automation rules**](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532--automation/)
>  for your pipeline with new work requests. For instance, you can automatically send a welcome message to your leads or assign a task to a teammate to call your prospect back.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/2f5b3a9b-c7cd-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Step 3: Initiate Projects for Your Clients  

---------------------------------------------

After the opportunity is won, and your prospect agrees to purchase your marketing services, create a new project out of an opportunity (Yes, [automation rules](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532--automation/)
 can help you with that too). But first, let’s touch upon a few basic steps to streamline your project management.  

### Custom Fields

Flowlu lets you [create your own fields](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/303-304--how-to-add-a-custom-field/)
, which will be displayed in the project card and project table (list).

You can always filter your projects by these fields.

What **custom fields** to include:

*   SLA (link or attachment)
    
*   Type of service, e.g. SEO Services, Content Marketing, Digital Advertising (dropdown or checkboxes)
    
*   Client’s social media handles (text)
    
*   Budget (price)
    
*   Content type (dropdown or checkboxes)
    
*   Campaign dates (date)
    
*   Campaign’s CTR, clicks, impressions, etc. (decimal number or percentage)
    
*   Guidelines (Object Picker > Knowledge Base)
    

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/453fa6ca-c7cd-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

  

### Project Templates

Use [project templates](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/126-199--how-to-create-project-workflow/)
 if you regularly take projects that follow the same path. For example, you can create project templates for Social Media, Branding, Website Design, SEO, Email Marketing services.

Go to **Projects** > **Project Workflows** > **New Project Workflow**. Indicate the main stages, milestones, and tasks that are used for this type of service. This way you will avoid starting from scratch every time you start a new project. Plus, your teammates will always know what to do next as they have a ready-mady checklists in front of them.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/d6206388-c7cd-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Project Progress

You can easily keep track of performance and progress of several projects at once on the Project Workflows board or Workload board.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/3f0ccfd7-c7ce-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

_Project Workflow Board_

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/a8dceaba-c7ce-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

_Workload Board_

A few words about a **[project card](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/126-412--overview-of-project-sections/)
**. Flowlu’s project record is the main point for your team collaboration on a project. It can store brand assets, tasks, notes, comments, spent time, estimated expenses, and paid invoices.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/f0513d6d-c7ce-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Step 4: Manage Team Tasks  

----------------------------

In Flowlu, you can manage tasks linked to a project, workspace or tasks outside any campaign or project. Each task has a [task workflow](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118-549--custom-task-workflows/)
 which governs the stages it will go through, so a task is considered completed.  

You can add **task statuses** for different processes (workflows) in your account. For example, if you define an article as one task (editorial calendar), you can create the following workflow for your Content Marketing:  

*   _Ideas > To Do > Research > Writing > Editing > Search Engine Optimization > Promoting > Analyzing > Finished_

Create **[tags](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/201-270-548--tags/)
** for your tasks to reflect post categories (Business, Team, Sales) or type of content (Guide, List, Interview).  

These labels along with task priorities will be displayed on the Kanban board.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/160b7a5f-c7e1-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

> **Tip:** Create **[task templates](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118-247--task-templates/)
> ** with checklists for routine activities such as monthly reports, bi-weekly analytics check, content approval, etc. They can be created automatically based on the [recurrence preferences](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118-248--how-to-create-recurring-task/)
> . 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/830d8fd0-c7e1-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Step 5: Try Flowlu's Extra Features for Marketing Teams  

----------------------------------------------------------

Create a **[knowledge base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/342--knowledge-base/)
** for your teammates to share best practices and tips. Your articles can contain pictures, videos, links, tables, etc.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/40641a4a-c7e2-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Use built-in **[mind maps](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/369--mind-maps/)
** to visually present different concepts, marketing strategies, buyer personas, campaign plans.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/46f2da27-d2a9-11ec-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

If you’re a spreadsheet admirer, try creating your database using Flowlu’s **[custom lists](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/303-308--installing-and-setting-the-record-lists-app/)
**. They can be used to keep track of guest writers for your blog, advertising accounts credentials, social media influencers, content production or any other information which can be organized with tables.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/67ff9e21-c7e2-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

  

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/620/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Setting the Workflow

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338--agile-projects/ "Agile Projects")
    
4.  [Main Features and Settings](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-339--main-terms-and-settings/ "Main Features and Settings")
    
5.  Setting the Workflow

Setting the Workflow
====================

* * *

The first thing you need to get started in the Agile application is setting the stages by which the issues in your projects will be performed, from creation to completion. The path or the list of steps that you specify is called a workflow. The stages will be displayed on the Agile board in the form of columns. This will allow you to see instantly how many working processes there are, at what stage, and what is already completed. You can create any number of work processes and use them in different Agile projects.

To create a workflow, go to the **Portal Settings → Agile Projects → Workflows**:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/346/77084509-6414-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Here you will see examples of processes created by default. You can delete or change them.

Creating Workflow
-----------------

#### Method 1:

Click on the **New Workflo****w** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/346/8dbe97da-6414-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Specify the process name and create the stages. For each stage, assign a global status.   

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/346/c801b6e9-6414-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/346/e1e54d47-6414-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Global statuses have a color indication, due to which you can visually evaluate their status in [backlog sprint](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-348-350--filling-the-backlog/)
 and find elements that need to be checked.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/346/4e98b1b8-6417-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

#### Method 2:

You can create workflow while creating the Agile project. To do that, click the **workflow** field while creating a new Agile project. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/346/f985f058-80f8-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Tap the **Create** button. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/346/de8b1907-80f9-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

The workflow creation field will be open, so you can quickly create a new one.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/346/2cfcb44a-80fa-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

After saving the process, at any time by clicking the workflow you can edit them or delete unnecessary ones. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/346/20cefbab-6418-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

How to Copy Workflows
---------------------

Go to the **Portal Settings → Agile Projects → Workflows**. Then mark the checkbox of the workflow you want to duplicate and click the **Copy** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/346/ed5e7363-80fe-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/346/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Populating the Backlog

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338--agile-projects/ "Agile Projects")
    
4.  [Scrum Project Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-348--project-management-with-scrum/ "Scrum Project Management")
    
5.  Populating the Backlog

Populating the Backlog
======================

* * *

The backlog is the starting point where the project planning begins. It is a list of requirements, stories, suggestions, comments to the product and other initiatives that regularly come from your customers, employees, investors, the product owner, etc.

All issues are taken into account, including those that can never be performed. For example, when developing an online store, the elements of gamification can be implemented later, or maybe not, depending on the behavior of website visitors and the cost of conversion.

Creating an Issue
-----------------

Navigate to the project you created and click the **Create** button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/350/efad2b5e-64da-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

1\. Briefly summarize the essence of the issue. The issue title should be simple, focused on the needs of the customer, understandable for the entire project team.  

2\. Specify the type. In most cases, this will be the stories. For more information about issue types, see the [Basic Concepts and Settings](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-339--main-terms-and-settings/)
 chapter.

3\. If necessary, change the priority, add a description, attach the files. When filling a backlog, you don’t have to immediately describe the details of the issue implementation, assign a responsible person to it, prepare a checklist for determining the readiness criteria, create the subtasks. All this you can specify when [planning the sprint](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-348-353--sprint-planning/)
.  

4\. Click the **Create** button.

For each issue, it is important to specify the category, assign tags, if necessary—establish relations with previously recorded issues. Thanks to this, the backlog will not turn into a chaotic list of requirements, and you can manage the work of the team effectively. You can find out more about [structuring the backlog elements](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-348-351--managing-the-backlog/)
 later on.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/350/4daff1e1-64db-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

If it is enough to specify only the name for the issue, write it in the backlog line and press Enter. By default, the requirement type—Story will be created.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/350/9f31b7bf-64db-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

All newly added backlog requirements are placed at the very end.

The project issues have a unique identifier of 2 parts:

*   project key (_SPC_ on the screenshot);
*   number.

By the number, you can quickly find an issue in the backlog and learn about its status.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/350/c6b19b66-64db-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

What Happens After Adding an Issue to the Backlog?
--------------------------------------------------

After saving the issue, the author is automatically recorded.

Later, this person receives notifications about all changes in the issue—new comments, moving to the sprint, assigning the responsible, moving the issue between the stages.

The project manager receives notifications about all changes in any issue and about adding a new issue to the backlog by any employee.

How to Edit Issues
------------------

To edit, click on the _pencil_ in the issues list.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/350/0dbfa857-64dd-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Or click on the issue title and make changes to the needed fields.

To change the priority, the type of requirement, the category assignment, you can also drag the issue into the corresponding section using the Drag&Drop method.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/350/5ee87dae-64dd-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

The button with the diagonal arrow allows you to go to the issue page. The button next to it deletes the issue.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/350/75e034e5-64de-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

You can also delete the issue from the detailed page via a button with 3 vertical dots.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/350/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Epics

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338--agile-projects/ "Agile Projects")
    
4.  [Scrum Project Management](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-348--project-management-with-scrum/ "Scrum Project Management")
    
5.  Epics

Epics
=====

* * *

Epic is a amount of associated tasks, based on the customer's need or end users. The main concept of epics is that they're not linked to just one sprint. When you have a big amount of associated task which can't be completed during the one sprint, you create an epic.

Epics help break the work into smaller parts, so you can regularly deliver value to your customers.

Epics are similar with subtasks and categories in some ways, but in other ways they're opposite:

*   Tasks with subtasks are linked to the sprint, while epics are independent.
*   Epics are different from categories because they're dynamic. Each epic has it's own status: **In** **progress** or **Completed**. Epic can't be closed if all the tasks inside are not completed.  
    

How to Create Epic in the Agile Module
--------------------------------------

Select the Agile project you want to create an epic for.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/652/d712267c-b102-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Click the small arrow next to the **Create** button and select the **Create Epic** option from the drop-down menu.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/652/59d8415f-b104-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

While creating a new epic, you can specify the following information:

*   Name.
*   Start and end dates.
*   Description.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/652/c373ab51-b104-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Click **Save.**

Where to Find Epics
-------------------

You can find all the epics linked to the project on the **Project Details** tab. On the left sidebar, go to the **Epic list** tab. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/652/b347fb26-b109-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

To find more detailed information about the epic, click on the name of it.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/652/abc81fa7-b10b-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

How to Add Issues to the Epic
-----------------------------

### In the Epic

Open the epic you want to populate. In the opened window, you can find detailed information about the epic and observe diagrams of issue types and the epic's progress. 

Click the **Create Issue** button in the top right corner to add a new issue to the epic.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/652/ce900ae7-b10c-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### In the Issue

Already existing tasks can be also added to the epic. To do that, open the issue and select the epic from the drop-down menu. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/652/f89c7653-b10d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/652/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | How to Change the Numbering

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Finance](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250--finance/ "Finance")
    
4.  [Invoices](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-258--how-to-prepare-an-invoice/ "Invoices")
    
5.  How to Change the Numbering

How to Change the Numbering
===========================

* * *

You can modify the starting number for Estimates and Invoices in [Organization Settings](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-258-259--organization-profile-settings/)
. 

> These settings are only available for users with **Portal Administrator** rights.

Select Finance in the sidebar, go to Settings -> **Accounts and Organizations**, click the name of an Organisation you want to make the changes for and go to **Invoices** tab**.**

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/390/4d5507fe-b888-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

By clicking **Change** you can set the numbering rules for both Invoices and Estimates created on behalf of this organization.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/390/550f5b25-b888-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

  
In the pop–up window, enter the number after which the numbering should start. For example, if you need the numbering of invoices to start from #50, enter 49.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/390/ba89981e-c12f-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

  
  
After saving the settings, all Invoices will be assigned a number automatically based on the entered number.

How to Change the Number of Invoice or Estimate Manually
--------------------------------------------------------

For any invoice, or estimate, you can change the number manually with the prefix. To do this, you need to enable this option in the settings of the **Finance** section.

*   Go to the **Portal Settings** → **Finance** → **Main Settings**
*   Enable **Set an invoice number manually** and **Set an estimate number manually**

After that, the field for entering the number manually will appear in Invoices and Estimates.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/390/4ee678b2-85e0-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

You can change invoice and estimate number immediately or on the Edit page after a document was created. When creating a new Invoice or Estimate, the system will automatically assign the next number in the sequence, disregarding the one that has been entered manually.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/390/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

[Previous Report: Invoices](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-258-321--invoice-payments-report/)

[Next Invoice Templates](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-258-264--invoice-templates/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Relation Types

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338--agile-projects/ "Agile Projects")
    
4.  [Main Features and Settings](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-339--main-terms-and-settings/ "Main Features and Settings")
    
5.  Relation Types

Relation Types
==============

* * *

For the issue, you can set up different types of relations, in order to link them later on a certain feature.

For example, the task to place a banner on the website cannot be performed until their layout is approved. For such a case, you can create a relation type with the _Blocked_ title. The performer will see this information in a task and will not proceed to it until the blocker is accepted.

In the same way, you can link duplicated issues when the implementation of one will mean the closure of the related issues. For example, the development of one feature for the software will close several user stories at once.

How to Create a Relation Type
-----------------------------

Go to the **Portal** **Settings** **→** **Agile Projects **→** Relation Types**. Here you can see the default relations created. You can change them, delete them, or create new ones.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/340/183a6a94-6419-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

The relations can be two–way or one–way.

Create a one–way relation and use it when the issues have the same relationship. For example, task # 1 is a duplicate of task # 2, and vice versa.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/340/92316384-6419-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Use a two–ways relation if you want to specify a different relationship between tasks. For example, task # 1 blocks task # 2, respectively, task # 2 is blocked by task # 1.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/340/cd9c069e-6419-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Establishing Relations

You can set any number of relations when creating or editing an issue.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/340/4d60762d-641a-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

_Going to the editing of an issue_

The **Relations** field is located in the lower part of the issue window. If the issue is already created, then go to Edit to add relations.

In the search field, type the name or number of the project issue with which you want to link the current one and set the relation type. Through the _New row_ button you can add another relation, and through **"–"** – cancel.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/340/9e85eb42-641a-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

_Created relations in issue_

# Flowlu Help Center | Action: Create Issue

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Knowledge Base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/342--knowledge-base/ "Knowledge Base")
    
4.  [Knowledge Base Automation](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/342-668--knowledge-base-automation/ "Knowledge Base Automation")
    
5.  Action: Create Issue

Action: Create Issue
====================

* * *

1\. To automatically create an Agile issue, click the "Create" button and select "Issue".

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/675/b39e7795-2151-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

2\. Specify a name for your automation. This should briefly outline your automated action and will be displayed on the automation board. 

3\. Set the trigger event. 

*   Create — runs when the article is created.
*   Update — runs when the article is updated.
*   Delete — runs when the article is deleted.

4\. In the **Run** field, choose a time when specific action (issue creation) will occur:

*   **Immediately.** The automation rule is run immediately after the event is triggered.
*   **X minutes/days/hours after** the **Current time.**

5. Add the **condition** that must be met for your rule to continue running. For instance, the Agile issue should be created only if the article marked as important.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/675/68a35efd-2151-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

6\. **After previous automation rule**. You may set up the sequence of automation rules.  This option empowers you to create a sequence of actions when your rule is not to be run before the previous rule is applied.   

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/675/5b76fff2-2152-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

7\. Link the issue to any of your Agile project, specify title and add a description if needed. By clicking the three-dot button, you can see the list of variables for knowledge bases.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/675/41f67f52-2153-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

8\. On the next step, you can select the type of an issue, assignee, followers, priority, set estimation and category.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/675/f18e54c2-2153-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

9\. You can also automatically set custom fields for the issue. If you have any custom fields for Agile issues, you'll see the list of available options at the bottom of the creation window. If not, the "Custom Fields" section will be empty.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/675/6a371817-2151-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

10\. Click **Save** to apply changes.  

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/675/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Inventory Management

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Finance](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250--finance/ "Finance")
    
4.  Inventory Management

Inventory Management
====================

* * *

If you produce or sell products, you need to record their availability in Warehouses, estimate their costs, and report both purchase costs and sales proceeds. The Inventory Management functionality can assist with this.

You will be able to:

*   Keep track of stock on hand and plan purchases.
*   Estimate average purchase costs.
*   Monitor the history of product movements.
*   Store all documents related to Product Arrival and Sale in one place.
*   Accurately reflect money-related transactions in Cash Flow and P&L reports.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/711/dfb5ee4e-5b86-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/711/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

*   [Inventory Management Access Rights](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-711-714--inventory-management-access-rights/)

# Managing Tasks

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Mobile App](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487--mobile-app/ "Mobile App")
    
4.  [Tasks & Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487-1504--tasks-projects/ "Tasks & Projects")
    
5.  Managing Tasks

Managing Tasks
==============

* * *

The Tasks module in the mobile app allows you to manage your work, collaborate with your team, and stay on top of your assignments from anywhere. This article explains how to navigate, view, create, and manage your tasks.

Navigation and Task Views
-------------------------

To access your tasks, open the **Modules** tab from the bottom menu and select **Tasks**. Inside, your tasks are organized into several views:

*   **Kanban board**: A visual board where tasks are organized by their status columns.
*   **Assigned to me**: All tasks for which you are the assignee.
*   **Assigned by me**: All tasks you have created and delegated to others.
*   **I'm a follower**: Tasks you are following to monitor progress.
*   **Need my approval**: Tasks that require your approval.
*   **Waiting owner's approval**: Tasks awaiting approval from the owner.
*   **Completed tasks**: A list of all finished tasks.
*   **All tasks**: A comprehensive list of every task you have access to.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1505/32b58601-5504-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Within a task list, such as a workflow or "All My Tasks", you can switch between two display modes using the icons in the top-right header.

*   **List View**: Displays tasks in a vertical list, showing the task name, dates, and related project.
*   **Board View**: Organizes tasks into columns based on their workflow stage. Each column header shows the stage name, task count, and total estimated time.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1505/3a266d1b-5504-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Filtering and Sorting Tasks
---------------------------

To refine your task list, you can apply filters or change the sorting.

Use the **Filter icon** to open the **Filters** menu. You can filter tasks by:

*   Owner
*   Assignee
*   Project
*   Priority
*   End Date
*   Group
*   Tags

Use the **Settings icon** to open the **Settings** menu. From here, you can adjust the following:

*   **Split columns by**
*   **Sort**

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1505/457c468f-5504-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Managing an Individual Task
---------------------------

Tapping on a task card opens its detailed page. The page is organized into **Description**, **Time Spent**, and **Files** tabs. From here, you can perform several actions:

*   Change the task's status from the dropdown menu (e.g., "To Do").
*   Mark the task as finished by tapping the **Complete** button.
*   Track time by using the **Start** and **Stop** buttons.
*   Add a checklist to break the task into smaller steps.
*   Add **Collaborators** and **Followers**.
*   View and edit the task's **Owner** and **Assignee**.
*   Write and view comments.

By tapping the **three-dot menu** in the top-right corner of the task screen, you can access more options:

*   Copy link
*   Share
*   Convert to milestone
*   Delegate
*   Edit
*   Delete

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1505/4d53eea1-5504-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Creating a New Task
-------------------

To create a new task:

1.  From a task list view, tap the **plus (+)** icon.
2.  On the **Add Task** screen, fill in the task details. The available fields are:
    *   Template
    *   Name
    *   Task Description
    *   Owner
    *   Assignee
    *   Collaborators
    *   Followers
    *   Start Date
3.  You can also use the **Attach Files** button to add attachments.
4.  Tap **Save** to create the task.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1505/51f02d7f-5504-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

[Previous Tasks & Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487-1504--tasks-projects/)

[Next Managing Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487-1504-1506--managing-projects/)

# Managing Organizations and Contacts

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Mobile App](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487--mobile-app/ "Mobile App")
    
4.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487-1488--crm/ "CRM")
    
5.  Managing Organizations and Contacts

Managing Organizations and Contacts
===================================

* * *

The CRM module in the mobile app is your central hub for managing your customer database. This guide will show you how to work with your Organizations and Contacts lists directly from your mobile device.

Navigating the Section
----------------------

To access your CRM database, navigate to the **Modules** tab from the bottom menu. Under the **CRM** section, you can switch between your company and individual records by tapping:

*   **Organizations (1)**: To view and manage your list of companies.
*   **Contacts (2)**: To view and manage your list of individual people.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1490/4e2e3dbd-5260-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Both sections display your records in a vertical list, showing key information like the name, email, and type (e.g., Customer, Partner, Prospect).

Filtering and Customizing the View
----------------------------------

At the bottom of the list, you have powerful tools to refine what you see:

*   **Filters (1):** Tap this button to open a list of criteria. For Organizations, you can filter by Assignee, Category, Industry, Tags, and more to narrow down your search. Tap **Apply** to see the results.
*   **Settings (2):** Tap the slider icon to open view settings. Here, you can **Group by** a specific field or change the **Sort** order of the records in the list.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1490/53a74c39-5260-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Adding a New Organization or Contact
------------------------------------

To create a new record in your CRM:

1.  Tap the **blue plus icon (1)** in the top-right corner of the Organizations or Contacts list.
2.  A **"Creating a company"** form (or a similar form for a contact) will slide open.
3.  Fill in the relevant details. For a company, this includes Name, Assignee, Category, Industry, Website, Phone, Email, and Description.
4.  Tap **Save** **(2)** to add the new record to your CRM.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1490/583f5a68-5260-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

#### **Managing an Existing Record**

To view or manage an existing organization or contact, simply tap on it from the list. This opens the record's detailed card view, which is organized into several tabs like **Main**, **Projects**, **Tasks**, and **Documents**.

The **Main** tab provides a complete overview, showing key data like the organization's type and industry, contact information, and a list of related contacts.

From the detail card, tap the **three-dot menu** in the top-right corner to access a list of additional actions. This menu allows you to:

*   Copy link or Share the record
*   Create a related Invoice, Estimate, Opportunity, Project, Task, or Event
*   Edit the record's details
*   Delete the record

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1490/5d26facf-5260-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

[Previous CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487-1488--crm/)

[Next Managing Opportunities](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/1487-1488-1489--managing-opportunities/)

# Loans

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Finance](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250--finance/ "Finance")
    
4.  Loans

Loans
=====

* * *

Loans is a vital part of any business. Many companies operate with borrowed funds for various purposes, making it essential to keep a detailed record of all payments and receipts.

For operations involving credits and loans, a dedicated module called Loans is available. This is a component of the Finance module and must be installed separately.

**Note:** The Loans module is available only on premium Flowlu subscription plans.

Module Features
---------------

*   Tracks loans, borrowings, and their interest.
*   Separates the repayment of the principal from interest payments.
*   Displays remaining debt balances and monthly payments.
*   Shows upcoming payments in the Payment Calendar.
*   Generates more accurate Financial Reports.

Within the module, the following entities exist:

*   Borrowed loans — funds that you have borrowed for business needs and are now repaying.
*   Issued loans — funds that other counterparties have borrowed from you and are now repaying to you.

Installing the Module
---------------------

Navigate to Apps from the sidebar menu.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1757/0fe34885-08d4-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

Or click on your avatar in the upper right corner and click on Apps.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1757/6c5dade4-08d5-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

Find Loans in the list of applications and click on it.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1757/3c07fd8d-08d4-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

Click Install.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1757/41c54028-08d4-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

You can access the Loans module through the main Finance module, where a corresponding subsection will appear.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1757/4a3fad2b-08d4-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

After installation, you can proceed to the module settings or start managing your borrowed funds immediately.

  

*   [Access Rights: Loans](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-1757-1758--access-rights-loans/)
    
*   [Borrowed Loans](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-1757-1759--borrowed-loans/)
    
*   [Issued Loans](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-1757-1760--issued-loans/)
    

[Previous Inventory Management and Cashflow and P&L Reports](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-711-716--inventory-management-and-cashflow-and-p-l-reports/)

# Automation

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Finance](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250--finance/ "Finance")
    
4.  [How to Record the Cash Flow](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-371--cash-flow-management/ "How to Record the Cash Flow")
    
5.  Automation

Automation
==========

* * *

With automation rules, you can automate some repetitive and administrative to-dos, such as task creation, notification, filed updates, events, etc. Once a transaction is created, updated, or deleted, you can automate what's coming next.

How to Create Automations for Transactions
------------------------------------------

Go to the **Money** tab in the Finance module.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/663/da08b70f-df56-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Click the **_three-dot button →_  Automation_._** 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/663/e57eacfe-df56-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the opened window, click the **Create Rule** button.

_![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/663/37fda6d0-df59-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)_

_For transactions, there are three types of triggers:_

*   **Money - Create:** automation starts with a new transaction 
*   **Money - Change:** automation starts when a change has been occurred to a transaction
*   **Money - Delete:** automation starts when a transaction has been deleted

_And several types of automation rules:_

*   **Notification** - creates a notification when transaction's status is changed
*   **Field Update** - updates a certain field of a transaction
*   **Event** \- creates an event based on the information from the transaction
*   **Task** - creates a task based on the information from the transaction
*   **Project** - creates a project based on the information from the transaction
*   **Issue** - creates an issue for a selected Agile Project
*   **Slack** - creates a Slack message (if **[Slack integration](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/201-597--how-to-integrate-slack-with-flowlu/)
    ** is enabled)

If you have multiple automations, they're sequenced. To change the sequence hover over the **\=** **icon** on a certain automation, then drag & drop the automation's card. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/663/c2baf03f-df5d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

To edit the automation, click the **pen icon**. 

To delete the automation, click the **recycle bin icon**.

Permissions
-----------

Only users with **administrator** access to the Finance module can create and edit automations. **Employees** and **Senior Sales** don't have access to the Money tab.

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/663/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Scrum: Key Points

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Agile Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338--agile-projects/ "Agile Projects")
    
4.  [Agile Method: Core Principles](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637--agile-method/ "Agile Method: Core Principles")
    
5.  Scrum: Key Points

Scrum: Key Points
=================

* * *

**Scrum** is a methodology for organizing team workflows. At its core are:

*   iterative development
*   continuous product improvement
*   cost reduction
*   focus on what matters most

Scrum belongs to the **Agile family**. These terms are often used interchangeably, but that’s incorrect. **Scrum** is a specific example of the Agile approach**,** whereas **Agile** is more of a philosophy or set of values.

> Agile is a **philosophy**; Scrum is its **implementation**.

This approach is inherently flexible, allowing for experimentation. That’s why it works well for **fast-paced product development**. Scrum enables teams to progress step by step while continuously assessing the effectiveness of their work.

### **Key Principles of Scrum**

*   Work is divided into short iterations, called **S****prints**.
*   The result of each sprint should be a **usable product increment** — something that brings value to the customer, even if it’s not yet released.
*   Product development is carried out by a **self-managed team**. All team members have **equal rights** and **shared responsibility** for the outcome.

### **Advantages of Scrum**

*   Continuous product development increases its value for the end user.
*   Each team member’s input is valued, boosting engagement and generating better ideas.
*   Fast results — progress is visible after each sprint.
*   Adaptability to change, whether it's market shifts, new technologies from competitors, or client requirements.
*   Transparency of tasks — everyone knows their area of responsibility.

### **Disadvantages of Scrum**

*   Assembling the right team is challenging: members must align both professionally and personally and be highly motivated.
*   Takes time to adapt to sprint planning — teams may initially misjudge the appropriate length of iterations.
*   Daily meetings and team check-ins consume time.
*   Task scope is locked during a sprint — once the sprint backlog is set, no new tasks can be added.
*   Not ideal for large companies or projects — coordination across multiple teams can become problematic.

  

*   [Scrum Team Roles](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637-1642-1643--scrum-team-roles/)
    
*   [Scrum Product Backlog](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637-1642-1644--scrum-product-backlog/)
    

[Previous Who is Agile for, and how to implement it?](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/338-1637-1641--who-is-agile-for/)

# How to Connect Telegram

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Communications](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/676--interactions/ "Communications")
    
4.  [How to Connect Messengers](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/676-677--how-to-connect-messengers/ "How to Connect Messengers")
    
5.  How to Connect Telegram

How to Connect Telegram
=======================

* * *

You can receive messages from Telegram in Flowlu via your own custom bot. By following this step-by-step guide, you will learn how to create your own Telegram bot and connect it with Flowlu.

If you already have a Telegram bot, you can skip to the paragraph about how to connect Flowlu with a Telegram bot.

How to Create a Telegram Bot
----------------------------

The easiest and safest way to create your own custom bot in Telegram is to use the official bot **@BotFather**. This bot is free and verified by Telegram and can also be used to manage all your bots.

> Note that if you manually type the name of this bot in the Telegram search panel, ensure that you're using the correct bot. Kindly check if the information of the bot you use is similar to the information in the screenshot below.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/678/0a1b9842-3512-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

Click the **Start** button to activate the bot.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/678/364ac813-3512-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

After the bot is activated, it will send you the full list of actions. Since you need to create a custom bot,  use the action **_/newbot_**.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/678/929547a5-3512-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

On the next step, you need to name the bot. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/678/bbfdbfdb-3512-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

After the name is created, you need to create a unique link for the bot. BotFather will show you examples of usernames. Note that it must end with 'bot'.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/678/d8fd6060-3512-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

Once you create the name and link, BotFather will send you a message with the unique token to access the bot. Copy the token and paste it in Flowlu on the next stage. 

How to Connect a Telegram Bot to Flowlu
---------------------------------------

Go to the **Communications** module and click the Create button.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/678/bf6d0246-ef60-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

Paste the token you've copied from the previous stage into the **Bot Token** field.

You can also set the queue of managers that will receive this message. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/678/e29e30e6-3515-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

Click the **Save** button to apply the changes.

Done! The bot is successfully linked to Flowlu, and you'll be receiving all the messages from this bot in Flowlu's built-in chat. The story of communication with this user will also be available on the page of a CRM account and in the list of chats in the Communications module. 

The bot is available in the Communications → Connected Channels.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/678/f30e8b09-ef60-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.jpg)

Bot Settings  

---------------

You can also apply additional changes to your bot. For example, customize the welcome message and create an auto-reply.

You can apply this setting while creating a bot or later in the **Message Settings** tab.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/678/024d4743-351d-11ee-8f7d-fa163e2ff576.png)

Disabling Automatic Opportunity Creation in the Communications Module
---------------------------------------------------------------------

In the Connected Channels settings, you can now choose not to specify a Pipeline for a particular chatbot or integration. When the Pipeline field is "Not set", the system will deliver new messages directly to your built-in messenger without automatically generating a new Opportunity. This feature is ideal for handling general inquiries or support requests that do not require an immediate sales record, helping you keep your CRM clean and focused only on qualified leads.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/678/713be306-07d5-11f1-991d-3a787cc0d110.jpg)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/678/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# How to Integrate Flowlu With Google Calendar on Android

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Calendar](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/299--calendar/ "Calendar")
    
4.  [How to Integrate Flowlu With Google Calendar](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/299-320--synchronizing-with-google-calendar/ "How to Integrate Flowlu With Google Calendar")
    
5.  How to Integrate Flowlu With Google Calendar on Android

How to Integrate Flowlu With Google Calendar on Android
=======================================================

* * *

Integrating Flowlu with Google Calendar allows you to manage your events more efficiently. Follow these simple steps:  

1\. Open the Flowlu app.  
2\. Tap your profile picture in the top left corner of the screen.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/730/00bb6bf3-9770-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

3. Select **Calendar Synchronization** from the menu.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/730/42e6a7a2-976d-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

4. Tap **Sign in with Google** to connect your Google account. Choose which calendars you want to export to Google Calendar. Flowlu will also import all your Google calendars.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/730/58d143b7-976d-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

5. Allow Flowlu access to your Google Calendar.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/730/fc67a704-976d-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

Events are being synced automatically every 12 hours when you open the Flowlu app. You also have the option to sync events manually by getting into Calendar synchronization menu and tapping Sync.

  

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/730/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# CRM Widget: Conversion

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  [CRM Widgets](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-1577--crm-widgets/ "CRM Widgets")
    
5.  CRM Widget: Conversion

CRM Widget: Conversion
======================

* * *

The "Conversion" widget displays the percentage of opportunity conversion from one stage to another. Depending on the chosen widget type, it shows:

*   The percentage of conversion for all opportunities to sales within a selected pipeline.
*   The percentage of conversion between two selected stages of a pipeline.

This widget allows you to dynamically track the effectiveness of managers in moving opportunities from one stage to another.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1573/a81b1af5-573d-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

### How to Install Widgets?

Widgets are installed on your dashboard. To access it, go to the "Start" tab and select your desired dashboard.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1573/adafcec8-573d-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Once on the dashboard:

*   Click the "Add widget" button.
*   In the "CRM" tab, select the "Conversion" item.
*   Choose the desired widget – either "Sales Conversion Rate" or "Pipeline Stage Conversion".

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1573/bb9b2287-573d-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

### Sales Conversion Rate

This widget shows the overall conversion from the entire pipeline to sales.

If desired, you can customize the widget's appearance and filter the information within it. To do this, open the context menu in the top right corner of the widget and go to settings.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1573/c82d3f44-573d-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

In the widget settings, you can:

*   Give the widget a name.
*   Activate the colored background highlight.
*   Modify filter settings:
    
    *   Select a specific sales pipeline – this is a required setting.
    *   Specify the currency.
    *   Choose the creation date of opportunities – either a specific date or a range.
    *   Specify the date range for the start and completion of opportunities.
    *   Select the responsible manager.
    *   Add additional fields for filtering opportunities.
    
    **Important:** When a time range is specified in the widget, a progress indicator will appear – an icon that displays changes in the metric compared to the previous period. The value in the indicator will be calculated from the difference between the values for the previous and current periods.
    

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1573/daa9ded3-573d-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

### Pipeline Stage Conversion

This widget displays the percentage of conversion between two pipeline stages. Simply put, it's the percentage of opportunities that moved from Stage 1 to Stage 2.

The same settings for appearance and filtering that apply to "Sales Conversion Rate" are also applicable to the "Pipeline Stage Conversion" widget. However, stage-specific settings are added: from which stage and to which stage the conversion is calculated.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1573/e61f4263-573d-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

Please note that you cannot calculate conversion in the reverse direction (e.g., from sale to lead). Therefore, when specifying a particular stage in the "From stage..." field, previous stages in the "To stage..." field will be unavailable for selection. These stages will be unclickable in the selector.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/1573/eec2c4ef-573d-11f0-bfde-3a787cc0d110.png)

> If a pipeline includes opportunities that have moved multiple times between stages, or have been completed and then resumed, there may be a margin of error in the conversion data. This applies to opportunities created before the release of this widget.

[Previous CRM Widget: Average Opportunity Duration](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-1577-1574--crm-widget-average-opportunity-duration/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Finance Categories

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Finance](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250--finance/ "Finance")
    
4.  [How to Record the Cash Flow](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/250-371--cash-flow-management/ "How to Record the Cash Flow")
    
5.  Finance Categories

Finance Categories
==================

* * *

Recording the cash flow by items will help you get a detailed financial report, due to which you will be able to analyze the structure of income and expenses and understand their effectiveness.

Flowlu allows you to keep track of cash flows at 2 levels: in the context of the three activities of the organization—operating, investing and financial as well as using items of income and expenses. 

Operating Activities
--------------------

Operating activities include all income and expenses related to the main activity of the company.   

**Examples of financial categories:**   

*   income from the sales of goods (services);
*   salaries;
*   payments to suppliers;
*   office rent;
*   taxes;
*   office expenses.

Investing Activities
--------------------

These activities include purchase or sale of own assets for long–term use.

**Examples of financial** **categories****:**

*   own cash investments in the business;
*   sale/purchase/repair of tangible assets long–term assets (equipment, transport, office furniture)
*   sale/purchase of intangible assets

Financial Activities
--------------------

These activities include any funds received from a bank or partners, enterprises and their return with interest. In other words, money that was not earned by the company itself.           

**Examples of financial** **categories****:**

*   obtaining loans from banks;
*   investments, borrowed funds from partners or other enterprises;
*   returns on interest;
*   cash payments on borrowed funds or loan.

For each type of activity, arbitrary items of income and expenses are set up. With the evolvement of business, new items are added and their level of detail increases.

Grouping the company's cash flow into three types of activities significantly simplifies the analysis and allows you to see the real picture of the company financial situation.

How to Set Up Financial Categories
----------------------------------

Go to **Portal Settings** → **Finance** → **Categories**.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/372/825aa9b9-8820-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

  
Here you can add and edit existing categories.

To create a new category, click the "**+"** button.

In the opened side panel enter:

*   category name;
*   category description;
*   mark what it is—the inflow or outflow.  
    
*   activity type;
*   type (revenue, other, etc.);
*   parent category. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/372/3b55eca3-0daa-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Save category.

To rename a category, click on its name. Categories that did not have any transactions can be deleted. To do this, click on the red cross sign.

To sort categories use the **Drag–and–drop** method. Put at the top the frequently used ones.

Once you complete setting up the categories, you can start recording the transactions.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/372/c47551db-8820-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/372/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | CRM Automation

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322--crm/ "CRM")
    
4.  CRM Automation

CRM Automation
==============

* * *

Automation rules can help you with streamlining your opportunity and task scenarios and avoid doing repetitive manual actions. They work based on the criteria that you set., for example, when an opportunity stage or task status changes, an automation rule gets triggered, such as assigning a task to a teammate, send a notification or create a meeting with the client in Calendar.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/61706b5f-c447-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

How to Create Automation Rules for Opportunities 
-------------------------------------------------

To create an automation rule for Opportunities, go to **CRM** → **Automation**. Select the pipeline you need to create automation rules.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/dd767c11-c44d-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

There are pipeline stages at the top of the board, automation rules can be created for each stage of a pipeline. You can also create your own automation rules on two final stages — "Closed Won" and "Closed Lost". For example, this way you can send a notification to the head of a department if the opportunity is lost.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/26ada4f7-397c-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

How to Create Automation Rules for Tasks  

-------------------------------------------

Go to **Tasks** module settings → **Workflows** and click the name of a workflow you want to create automation rules for:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/1faf3719-c451-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the popup window you will see task workflow stages, automation rules can be added to each stage of a workflow.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/98b7b1a1-397c-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

  

How to Create Automation Rules for Agile Projects
-------------------------------------------------

Go to **Agile** module, select an Agile project and switch to **Automation** tab.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/c6fc5f25-c451-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

****_How to add automation to an agile workflow_****

Navigate to **Agile** → go to module **Settings** → **Workflows**. Click on a name of a Workflow and select **Advanced Editing:**  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/6495dab0-00d9-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

How to Create Automation Rules for Invoices
-------------------------------------------

Go to **Finance** → **Invoices** and click **Automation** in the top right corner.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/5e0f0a49-c452-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

There are invoice statuses at the top of the board. You may add one or more automation rules under each status.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/cffc3dc7-1c9b-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

These statuses include:

*   Not Paid
*   Void
*   Approval Requested
*   Approved
*   Partially Paid
*   Overpaid
*   Paid

> Note: approval-related statuses can only be seen if you've enabled this option in Portal Settings. 

Some statuses contain Send Invoice/Reminder automation rule. They include Approved, Paid, Partially Paid, Overpaid statuses. The invoice or invoice reminder can be emailed by using this rule. It depends on whether the invoice was already emailed. 

**There are several types of automatic actions available in Flowlu:**

*   **[Create a notification](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-534--auto-notifications/)
    **
*   **[Create a task](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-533--auto-tasks/)
    **
*   **[Create an event](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-535--auto-events/)
    **
*   **[Create a project](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-540--automation-rule-project/)
    **
*   **[Send an email](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-539--automation-rule-email/)
    **
*   **[Update item's fields](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532-541--automation-rule-change-opportunity/)
    **
*   [**Publish a comment**](https://support1.flowlu.ru/hc/4/118-532-555--action-comment)
    
*   [**Create an activity feed post**](https://support1.flowlu.ru/hc/4/118-532-556--action-activity-feed-post)
    
*   [**Send message to Slack channel**](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/118-532-599--action-message-in-slack/)
    
*   **Create a webhook**
*   **Move to Archive** (only for Agile issues)
*   **Send Invoice or Reminer** (only for Invoices)

You can learn more about each of them by clicking on the corresponding automation.

How to Manage Automation Rules
------------------------------

Once your rule is created, you can change its details at any time by clicking on **Edit**. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/08304225-397d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

You can also delete the rule from its page or by clicking on the **X** sign from the board.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/cfb19324-397d-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

All automation rules can be easily moved between stages, as well as within one stage to change the sequence of rules.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/b9f53649-397e-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Where to View Automation Rules for a Specific Task/Opportunity 
---------------------------------------------------------------

Each opportunity record comprises the "Automation" tab, where you can see planned and finished automation rules.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/5de11f68-c4df-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)

To view the automation for a specific **task**, go to a task page and click on the three-dot button → **Workflow**.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/fe703860-3980-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

You can view the status of an automation rule here to identify whether it was executed or not:

A green check mark means that the automation rule was successfully completed.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/dde55b10-3980-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

A yellow hourglass means that the automation is to be finished when the previous task is completed.  

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/c8f8404f-3980-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Blue "gear" sign means that the action is scheduled to be performed.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/b1ce0ea2-3981-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

If you see a red cross, it indicates that an error has occurred, so the automation rule will not be executed.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/b48d73cb-3980-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

### Use Cases  

In Flowlu, you can create an unlimited number of automation rules, for example:

*   When you move an opportunity to the "Closed Won" stage, set the "Create new project" task.
*   When moving an opportunity to the "Closed Lost" stage, send an automatic notification to the head of the sales department: " The X opportunity to the amount of Y is lost".
*   When moving to the "Consideration" stage, create an event with the "Call" type. Run: 3 days after Current time. Event name: "Call the X client".
*   When moving an opportunity to the first stage, e.g. "New", create a task for the assignee: "Contact the Y customer".
*   When moving to the last stage of the opportunity, create a notification that will notify the assignee 3 days before the end date of the opportunity.
*   If the deadline is approaching, send the notification to the task owner and assignee at the "In Progress" status. 

### Permissions 

Editing and creating automation is available only to the CRM module administrators. Viewing the automation is available to all users with the ‘Employee’ access to the module.

**See also:** [Business Automation Tips to Streamline Sales Processes](https://www.flowlu.com/blog/flowlu-tips/business-automation-tips-to-streamline-sales-processes/)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/532/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Email Signature

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Email](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494--email/ "Email")
    
4.  [Managing Emails](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/494-515--managing-email/ "Managing Emails")
    
5.  Email Signature

Email Signature
===============

* * *

Flowlu lets you create email signatures for each email account. You can personalize new emails and reply messages with your contact information, logo, links, and more.

To create a signature, go to your email account settings by clicking on "three dots" in the top right corner of the email list.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/498/69e4aca2-bcbe-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Click on the **Signature** field.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/498/812ccb66-bcbd-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the opened window, add a signature to your emails.

Compose a signature using the toolbar options. Customize the text format, change the alignment or add an image.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/498/e448dbac-bcbd-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

Save.

The signature will be automatically appended to the bottom of every new message you create. It's possible to insert signature before the quoted text with the corresponding option.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/498/4dccc987-bcbe-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/498/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

# Flowlu Help Center | Access Rights: Calendar

[Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

* * *

1.  [Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/ "Flowlu")
    
2.  [Flowlu Help Center](https://www.flowlu.com/help/ "Flowlu Help Center")
    
3.  [Calendar](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/299--calendar/ "Calendar")
    
4.  Access Rights: Calendar

Access Rights: Calendar
=======================

* * *

You will see calendars to which you have been given access by your colleagues in the **Shared with me** column. Due to this option, employees will be able to see each other's schedules and plan shared activities.

How to Share Your Calendar
--------------------------

Only you can share your own calendar. The portal administrator does not have such ability. 

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/313/529e9f71-c3f6-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

To assign the access levels to your calendar, click on three vertical dots to the right of the name and click **Edit**:

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/313/6c3852c5-c3f6-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

In the opened sidebar, select the users you want to share your calendar with and save.

By default, they will be given a **View availability** access level. Change the access level for each user if necessary.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/313/970ccd94-c3f6-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

About Calendar Access Rights
----------------------------

**View availability.** Users with this access level to your calendar will see only the status of the event. Viewing the event names or descriptions will not be available.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/313/1f419edb-c3f8-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

**View availability and names**. At this access level, the employee will see your schedule and name of your events. The description and other information will be unavailable.

**See all event details.** The event details, links to opportunities, projects, CRM accounts, and comments can be accessed. Changing any information is not available.

**Edit calendar and events**. The user gets access to the creation of new events and changing the schedule in your calendar.

With **full access**, the user also can delete events from your calendar.

Access Level in Events
----------------------

When creating an event in the calendar, you can further deny or provide access to viewing the information in it.

If you create a personal event, then only those users who have access level to this calendar not lower the editing one will be able to see it.

If you create a public event, then its details will be visible to all users who have access to this calendar, regardless of the level of access.

![](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/313/3eec0b88-c3f8-11ed-a147-fa163e2ff576.png)

[![start your free trial](https://www.flowlu.com/help/assets/images/313/f09c62e4-8628-11ef-919e-fa163e2ff576.png)](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

[Previous How to Integrate Flowlu With Calendly](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/299-580--how-to-integrate-flowlu-with-calendly/)

[Next Sync your Apple Calendar with Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/299-729--apple-calendar-sync/)
